paragraphs.en: And Bocaccio was close friends to the artist Giotto di Bondone.Although a generation older that Bocaccio, they became friends when Giotto was quite old and Bocaccio was quite young. His paintings are some of the earliest Renaissance masterpieces. Giotto's paintings are some of the earliest Renaissance masterpieces. During Giotto's life, his home city of Florence began building a great Cathedral. It would become one of the great building projects in world history and would stimulate the development of the Renaissance in Florence. Giotto served as an architect in that project, and he designed its beautiful bell-tower.
paragraphs.zh: 薄伽丘是艺术家乔托·迪·邦多内 的好朋友。虽然比薄伽丘年长一 代，但他们成了忘年交。乔托的画 是文艺复兴早期的杰作。 乔托的绘画是文艺复兴时期最早 的杰作之一。 在乔托生活的时代，他的家乡佛罗 伦萨开始建造一座大教堂，它将成 为世界历史上最伟大的建筑工程 之一，并将促进佛罗伦萨文艺复兴 的发展。 乔托在这个项目中担任建筑师，他 设计了这座美丽的钟楼。

paragraphs.en: As I hope you can see now, the Renaissance really began as a small group of educated artists living in Florence in the early 1300s. They all seem to share a humanist philosophy that strives for excellence and shares an interest in the classics of Greece and Rome. This network seems to center on Petrarch and expands outwards from him. And Petrarch's passion for Latin made him an excellent teacher, and he inspired his students to carry on his work of preserving and rediscovering the knowledge ofAncient Rome. Francesco Petrarch was the teacher of Giovanni Malpaghino who was the teacher of Poggio Bracciolini. Poggio Bracciolini was born 5 years after Petrarch died, but he carried on the
paragraphs.zh: 我希望你们现在能看出，文艺复兴 实际上起源于 14 世纪早期居住在 佛罗伦萨的一小群受过教育的艺 术家。 他们似乎都有一种追求卓越的人 文主义理念，都对希腊和罗马的经 典著作感兴趣。 这个网络似乎以彼特拉克为中心 向外不断扩展。 彼特拉克对拉丁语的热爱使他成 为一名优秀的教师，他激励学生们 继续他的工作，保护和重新发现古 罗马的知识。 弗朗希斯科·彼特拉克是乔瓦尼·马 尔帕赫诺的老师，后者又是波焦·布 拉乔利尼的老师。 波焦·布拉乔利尼出生于彼特拉克 去世 5 年后，但他继承了彼特拉克

paragraphs.en: movement that Petrarch had started. Bracciolini also searched Europe looking for copies of old Roman manuscripts, saving many from destruction and preserving them for posterity. And he also got lucky, he discovered the only surviving copy of the book On the Nature of Things by the Roman philosopher Lucretius. Bracciolini worked very hard at being good writer and had beautiful handwriting, as you can see here. It was so good that the Roman Font type was based on it! Bracciolini was also a personal secretary to several Popes including Pope Nicolas V and he influenced many other Renaissance thinkers. We could spend hours and hours tracing
paragraphs.zh: 开创的运动。 布拉乔利尼还在欧洲搜集了许多 古罗马手稿的副本，让他们免于毁 灭，并为子孙后代保存了下来。 他也很幸运地发现了一本罗马哲 学家卢克莱修写的关于事物本质 的书，是唯一幸存本。 布拉乔利尼努力成为一名优秀的 作家，他的字写得很漂亮，你可以 在这里看到。 他的字好到通用的罗马字体就是 基于这一字体! 布拉乔利尼也是几位教皇的私人 秘书，包括影响了许多文艺复兴时 期思想家的尼古拉斯五世教皇。 我们可以花上几个小时来追溯不

paragraphs.en: the connections between all the different Renaissance thinkers and Humanists, but the main point is this: The idea of Humanism had taken hold, and a network of thinkers was building upon it all across Italy, and then Europe. The Renaissance had begun. And now the Catholic church was supporting the Renaissance as well. In the meantime work was continuing on the Great Cathedral of Florence. In 1400 the Baptistery needed new doors, and the city had decided to make them great work of art. Here is what the Florence Baptistery looks like by the way. So the city hired the master goldsmith Lorenzo Ghiberti, who worked with two remarkable assistants: Brunelleschi and
paragraphs.zh: 同的文艺复兴思想家和人文主义 者之间的联系，但重点是： 人文主义的思想已经根深蒂固， 思想家网络在意大利搭建起来，然 后传播到整个欧洲。文艺复兴开始 了。 现在，天主教会也支持文艺复兴。 与此同时，佛罗伦萨大教堂的工程 仍在继续。 1400 年，洗礼堂需要安装新门，于 a 是这座城市决定把门做成一件伟 大的艺术品。 这是佛罗伦萨洗礼堂的样子。 于是，这座城市聘请了金匠洛伦 佐·吉贝尔蒂大师，他与两位杰出 的助手共事：布鲁内莱斯基和多纳

paragraphs.en: Donatello. Goldsmiths are often great craftsmen because they have to be highly skilled and confident to work with such expensive material. And then in 1430 he produced his masterpiece: the statue David. This statue was the first Nude statue to created in Europe since the Roman Era. Donatello was expressing his Humanism by celebrating the human body and reviving the Greco-Roman tradition of the Nude in art. Now the stage was set for a great flowering ofArt that we associate with the Renaissance. This reached its peak the next generation during the late 1400s. There was the great painter Sandro Botticelli.
paragraphs.zh: 泰罗。 金匠通常是伟大的工匠，因为他们 必须有很高的技能和信心，才能使 用如此昂贵的材料。 1430 年，他创作出了他的杰作：《大 卫》青铜雕像。 be 这是自罗马时代以来欧洲第一个 裸体雕像。 多纳泰罗通过赞美人体和复兴古 希腊罗马艺术中的裸体传统来表 达他的人文主义理念。 现在，文艺复兴的舞台已经升起， 艺术开始百花齐放，这一运动在 15 世纪后期的下一代达到了顶峰。 有伟大的画家桑德罗·波提切利。

paragraphs.en: His paintingAYoung Man Being introduced to the Seven Liberal Arts reflects the value that Humanism on a good education, especially in the classics. This was painted in 1486. There is also his 1485 work, the Wedding of Venus and Mars, which again is a celebration of the human body. And there is one of my favorite paintings of all time: The Birth of Venus which he painted in 1486. This is the first painting to focus on a large female nude. Like Donatello's David it is a celebration of the human body, and the revival of the Nude form art, this time in painting. And then, a generation later, there were the three great masters: Leonardo, Michelangelo, and Raphael. Let's look at Leonardo first.
paragraphs.zh: 他的绘画《少年七艺》反映了人文 主义教育，尤其是经典教育的价 put 值。这幅画创作于 1486 年。 还有他 1485 年的作品，《维纳斯 和马尔斯的婚礼》，同样是对人体 的颂歌。 这是我一直以来最喜欢的一幅画： 《维纳斯的诞生》，这幅画是他在 1486 年画的。 这是第一幅以大型裸体女性为主 题的画作。就像多纳泰罗的《大卫》 一样，这是对人体的颂扬，也是裸 in 体艺术形式的复兴，而这一次它体 现在绘画上。 再一代人之后，出现了三位大师： 莱昂纳多、米开朗基罗和拉斐尔。 让我们先来看看达芬奇。

paragraphs.en: This is theAnnuciation from 1480. And this is his second most famous work, the Last Supper from 1498. Unfortunately, his version is damaged. But fortunately, Leonardo's student Giampietrino copied his teacher's masterpiece 22 years later in 1520, so we have a good idea of what it looked like. Again, notice the vanishing point centers on Jesus's right eye. And then of course there is his most famous painting of all: The Mona Lisa. If you look at her eyes she seems to smile at you, but if you look at her mouth, it vanishes. This smile has intrigued viewers for 502 year and made it the most famous painting in the world. Now let's look at Michaelangelo. Here is his famous sculpture, the Pieta,
paragraphs.zh: 这是画于 1480 年的《天使报喜》。 这是他第二著名的作品，画于 1498 年的《最后的晚餐》，不幸的是， 原版被破坏了。 幸运的是，达芬奇的学生詹彼得里 诺在 22 年后的 1520 年复制了老师 的杰作，所以我们对它的样子可以 有一个很好的了解。 再次注意，消失点是耶稣的右眼。 当然还有他最著名的画作：《蒙娜 丽莎》。 如果你看她的眼睛，她似乎在对你 微笑，但如果你看她的嘴，微笑又 消失了。这个微笑已经吸引了 502 年以来的观众，这也使得它成为了 世界上最著名的画作。 现在让我们看看米开朗基罗。 这是他的著名雕塑《圣母怜子像》，

paragraphs.en: which depicts Mary holding her son Jesus after the crucifixion. There is his sculpture of King David, capturing him in thought moments before his Battle with Goliath. On one hand he holds his sling, in the other several river stones. And then there is his painting of the Sistine Chapel, one of the great masterpieces of all time. This is one of the central chapels in the Vatican. And each section depicts a different Bible story. Here is the key if you are curious which story goes with which panel. And the most famous of all is the central panel which shows God creatingAdam. This is moments before he touches him and giveAdam consciousness.
paragraphs.zh: 雕刻出玛利亚生产后抱着儿子耶 稣的场景。 这是他的《大卫》雕像，捕捉到了 大卫王在与歌利亚交战前的沉思。 他一手拿着投石器，另一手拿着河 里的石头。 这是他不巧的杰作——画在梵蒂 冈中心教堂之一的西斯廷教堂天 顶上的壁画。 壁画的每一部分都描述了一个不 同的圣经故事。 如果你好奇哪个故事和哪组壁画 相关，你可以看这张图。 其中最著名的是这个展示上帝创 造亚当的中央壁画，画的是上帝触 碰亚当并赋予他意识之前。

paragraphs.en: Finally, there is Raphael. As you can see here, he was a master portrait artist. But his most famous work might be The School ofAthens painted in the Pope's personal apartment. It shows all the great ancient philosophers of Greece and Rome together in one place. This shows us both the Renaissance fascination with the Classical world, and heavy church support for this art. I could go on and on about the Renaissance, but this is just an introduction. If you are really curious you should take a full class on art history. But now let's shift our attention away from Italy. Because just as the Italians were discovering the world of the
paragraphs.zh: 最后是拉斐尔。 正如你在这里看到的，他是一位杰 出的肖像画家。 他最著名的作品可能就是画在教 皇居室里的《雅典学院》。 它集中展示了所有古希腊和古罗 马的伟大哲学家。 这向我们展示了文艺复兴时期人 们对古典世界的迷恋，以及教会对 这种艺术的大力支持。 我可以继续讲文艺复兴，但这只是 一个介绍。如果你真的好奇，你应 该选艺术史这门课。 现在，让我们把注意力从意大利转 移开。因为就在意大利人挖掘内心 世界和古代世界的时候，葡萄牙人

paragraphs.en: inner-self and the ancient world, the Portuguese were beginning to explore the actual world, specifically theAtlantic Ocean. Because at the same time as the Renaissance was happening in Italy, the Age of Discovery had begun further west. Please join me next time for that lesson.
paragraphs.zh: 开始探索真实的世界，尤其是大西 洋。 因为当文艺复兴在意大利兴起时， 更西边开启了探索的时代。下节课 我们再见。

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on world history. Today we will learn about theAge of Exploration. Although theAge of Exploration lasted into the 1700s, we will focus on the year from 1415 to 1492. Last time, we looked at the emergence of the Renaissance in Italy during the 1300s and 1400s. Today I want us to shift our focus west to Spain and Portugal. When we last paid attention to this part of the world, it was the middle of the 8th century and the Umayyad Caliphate had just conquered the peninsula and only the far northern region ofAsturias was not under their control.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列讲 座。今天我们将学习探索时代。 虽然探索时代一直延续到了 18 世 纪，但我们将重点关注 1415 年至 1492 年这几年。 上节课，我们知道了意大利文艺复 兴发生在 14-15 世纪。今天我想把 我们的焦点转移到西边的西班牙 和葡萄牙。 我们上次关注这一地区还是在8世 纪中叶的时候，当时倭马亚王朝刚 刚征服了这个半岛，只有阿斯图里 亚斯最北部的地区不在他们的控 制之下。

paragraphs.en: FromAsturias the Catholic Iberians were able to push back against the Muslims Iberians and by 940AD the Catholics controlled the north of Iberia.AlthoughAsturias had divided into 5 different Catholic Kingdoms: Gallicia, Leon, Castille, Pamplona and Catalonia. In 1009 the Caliphate of Cordoba collapsed into a civil war which lasted until 1031. The result was that Muslim Iberia collapsed into 9 smaller Kingdoms called Taifas and the Catholic Kingdoms gained power in the North. Leon and Gallicia unified while Pamplona divided into the Kingdoms of Navarre andAragon. The Catholic re-conquest was aided
paragraphs.zh: 在阿斯图里亚斯，信奉天主教的伊 比利亚人击退了穆斯林伊比利亚 人，天主教于公元 940 年控制了伊 比利亚北部。尽管这时的阿斯图里 亚斯已经被划分为五个不同的天 主教王国：加利西亚、莱昂、卡斯 蒂利亚、潘普洛纳和加泰罗尼亚。 1009 年，后倭马亚王朝暴发内战， 一直持续到 1031 年。 最终，伊比利亚半岛的穆斯林分裂 成 9 个小王国，即塔伊法斯帝国， 而天主教的势力在北方。 利昂和加利西亚合二为一，潘普洛 纳则分裂成那瓦尔和阿拉贡王国。 在欧洲其他地区十字军的帮助下，

paragraphs.en: by crusaders from the rest of Europe and by 1162 the Catholic Kingdoms controlled over half the country. During this time the Kingdom of Portugal split from Leon, while Catalonia andAragon unified. On the Muslims side, a Moroccan dynasty called theAlmohads managed to control half the Muslim territory and reorganize Muslim defenses, which slowed down Catholic progress. By 1212, the Catholics controlled most of the country, while the Almohad dynasty controlled the south. And then in the 1200s came a rapid burst of conquest. Leon and Castile unified and conquered almost all of the south, aided by Portugal.
paragraphs.zh: 1162 年，天主教再度控制了半数国 家。 在此期间，葡萄牙王国从莱昂中分 离，而加泰罗尼亚和阿拉贡统一起 来。 在穆斯林这边，摩洛哥的阿尔莫哈 德王朝控制了一半的穆斯林领土， 并重组了穆斯林防御体系，制约了 天主教的发展。 到 1212 年时，天主教控制着全国 大部分地区，而阿尔莫哈德王朝占 据着南方。 到了 13 世纪，征服的号角迅速吹 响。莱昂和卡斯蒂利亚在葡萄牙的 帮助下征服并统一了几乎整个南 方。

paragraphs.en: At the same time the Kingdom of Aragon expanded into the Mediterranean and conquered the island of Majorca. By 1265 the Catholics controlled most of Iberia and the Muslims were confined to the Kingdom of Granada in the far south: a reversal from the days ofAsturias. But then Catholic conquests slowed down. By 1385 the map of Iberia was still much the same. As you can see, there was Portugal in the west, Castille in the center, andAragon in the East, with Navarre in the north and Muslim Granada in the South. In that same year of 1385, Portugal ended a war with the Kingdom of Castile and began a period of peace. The King of Portugal at that time
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，阿拉贡王国扩张到地中 海并征服了马略卡岛。 到了 1265 年，天主教已经控制了 伊比利亚半岛的大部分地区，穆斯 林只有一个格拉纳达王国，在远远 的南边求存：这与阿斯图里亚斯时 代的情形截然相反。 但是后来，天主教扩张的速度放缓 了。到了 1385 年，伊比利亚半岛 的地图没出现太多变动。可以看 出，西部是葡萄牙，中部是卡斯蒂 利亚，东部是阿拉贡，北部是纳瓦 雷，南部是穆斯林格拉纳达。 1385 年，葡萄牙结束了与卡斯蒂利 亚王国的战争，进入了一段和平时 期。 1387 年，葡萄牙国王约翰一世迎娶

paragraphs.en: was named John I, and in 1387 he married Philippa of Lancaster, a grand-daughter of the English King Edward III, and sister of the English King Henry IV. With Philippa, John I had six children who lived into adulthood: Edward, Peter, Henry, Isabella, John and Ferdinand. These children were all born between the years of 1391 and 1402. At this point the youngest son, Henry began to think about Portugal's position in the world. Henry was the 3rd son, so he had little chance of ever becoming King of Portugal, instead he tried to make his mark on history another way. He looked for a way to expand his
paragraphs.zh: 了兰开斯特的菲利帕为妻，菲利帕 是英国国王爱德华三世的孙女，也 是英国国王亨利四世的妹妹。 约翰一世和菲利帕一共有六个活 到成年的孩子，他们是：爱德华、 彼得、亨利、伊莎贝拉、约翰、费 迪南德。 这些孩子都出生于 1391 年到 1402 年间。 此时，亨利最小的儿子开始思考葡 萄牙在世界上所处的地位。 亨利是第三子，所以他几乎没有机 会成为葡萄牙的国王，所以，他试 图用另一种方式在历史上留下自 己的印记。他想找个办法扩大祖国 的版图。

paragraphs.en: country. Portugal had only 1 neighbor, the Kingdom of Castile, so any expansion north or east would mean a war with them. Portugal had just finished fighting a war with Castile in 1385 and did not want to start another one. Therefore, the only logical direction in which to expand was south and east. In other words the Portuguese would need to explore theAtlantic Ocean! For centuries, European sailors had been afraid of theAtlantic Ocean. Its waters were much more rough than the Mediterranean, and nobody had any idea how big it was.
paragraphs.zh: 葡萄牙只有一个邻国——卡斯蒂 利亚王国，所以无论是向北或向东 的扩张，都免不了与他们开战。 但是葡萄牙在 1385 年刚刚结束了 与卡斯蒂利亚的战争，不想再卷入 另一场战争。 因此，唯一合乎逻辑的扩张方向就 是向南和向东。 换句话说，葡萄牙人需要探索大西 洋! 几个世纪以来，欧洲水手一直害怕 大西洋。大西洋的水比地中海汹涌 得多，没有人知道它的浪到底有多 大。

paragraphs.en: Greek and Roman galleys had a hard time in the waters of the Atlantic Ocean. So for the Romans, Ireland and the Canary Islands were the edge of the known world. We can understand the European conception of the world by looking at this map created by the Roman cartographer and astronomer Claudius Ptolemy. As you can see, Roman understanding of the Mediterranean was pretty good, but anything south of the Sahara and east of India was a mystery. By 1453, things had improved somewhat. This is the Fra Mauro map, the best European map of the world from that time.
paragraphs.zh: 希腊和罗马的军舰在大西洋吃了 很多苦头。 所以对罗马人来说，爱尔兰和加那 利群岛就是已知世界的边缘。 我们可以通过这幅由罗马制图家 和天文学家克劳迪亚斯·托勒密绘 制的地图去了解当时欧洲人对世 界的认识。 正如你所看到的，罗马人对地中海 的理解是相当深入的，但是对他们 来说，撒哈拉以南和印度以东的每 一个地方都是一个谜。 到了 1453 年，情况开始有所改善。 这是当时最好的欧洲地图——弗 拉·毛罗地图。

paragraphs.en: Here is a satellite image of the same region.As you can see the map is pretty good when it comes to European geography, but gets much cruder aboutAfrica andAsia, and the map maker had no knowledge ofAmerica. However, all this would change in the 1400s thanks to great voyages of exploration. The first great European sea-farers were the Norse, who explored and far as Iceland, Greenland and Canada. But the Norse explorers were driven out of Vinland after a couple of years and did not communicate their discoveries to the rest of Europe, so their expeditions to theAmericas had no long-term historical impact.
paragraphs.zh: 左边是同一区域的卫星图像。正如 你所看到的，这张地图描绘的欧洲 很精确，但描绘的非洲和亚洲却很 粗糙，而且地图上压根就没出现美 洲。 然而，15 世纪开启的伟大的探险之 旅让这一切发生了变化。 第一批伟大的欧洲航海者是挪威 人，他们到过冰岛、格陵兰岛和加 拿大。 但是几年后，挪威探险者被赶出了 文兰，他们没有把自己的发现告诉 欧洲其他人，所以他们的美洲探险 就没能产生深远的历史影响。

paragraphs.en: But the Norse did develop the Knarr, which became the best trading ship in Europe from the 8th to the 12th century. Gradually the Knarr was replaced by the Cog, which had a single mast and square rigged sail and was the main trading ship from the 12th century to the early 15th century. But if Portugal was going to explore theAtlantic they needed a better type of ship. The main advantage of the Caravel was that it had adopted the Lateen sail from the Muslim world. This sail allowed the caravel to travel into the wind by tacking back and forth. Both the Lateen sail and the tradition square sail can sail
paragraphs.zh: 但是挪威人确实推动了肯纳船的 发展，这是在 8—12 世纪时期欧洲 最好的贸易船。 逐渐地，肯纳船被柯克船所取代， 柯克船只有一根桅杆和方帆，是 12 —15 世纪早期的主力贸易船。 在亨利王子的指示下，葡萄牙人建 造了一种新型的船，并命名为“卡 拉维尔”帆船。 卡拉维尔帆船的主要优点在于，它 采用了来自穆斯林世界的三角帆。 这种帆使得船只可以通过摇摆行 进的方式来顶风行驶。 三角帆和传统的方帆都可以顺风 航行。

paragraphs.en: with the wind. But only the Lateen sail can sail into the wind. With their new ship, the Portuguese were now ready to expand out into theAtlantic Ocean. They began this era of exploration in 1415 with surprise attack on the city of Ceuta. The Portuguese King John I led the attack himself and Prince Henry distinguished himself in the battle. Henry was fascinated byAfrica because most of Europe's gold supply came from trade caravans that brought the WestAfrican gold across the Sahara dessert. Henry wanted to find the source of that gold, and so began a fascination
paragraphs.zh: 但能够顶风而行的只有三角帆。 有了新船，葡萄牙人现在准备向大 西洋扩张。 1415 年，葡萄牙人突袭休达城，这 个探险时代就此拉开帷幕。 葡萄牙国王约翰一世亲自率领进 攻，亨利王子也在战斗中表现突 出。 亨利对非洲十分着迷，因为大多数 的欧洲黄金都是商队从西非横穿 撒哈拉沙漠运送而来。 亨利想找到黄金之源，所以他对非 洲十分着迷。葡萄牙人的探险之旅

paragraphs.en: withAfrica. The Portuguese would have to goals in their journeys of exploration: theAtlantic Ocean and theAfrican Coast. In 1419 a Portuguese expedition led by João Gonçalves Zarco and Tristão Vaz Teixeira discovered the Madeira islands and claimed them for Portugal. The next year in 1420, the Portuguese began to settle these islands. The exploration continued, and in 1427 Gonçalo Velho Cabral discovered theAzores Islands. Here is what theAzores look like from the ocean, imagine being the first humans ever to see these islands in history. What a mysterious and exciting moment
paragraphs.zh: 一定要拿下的是：大西洋和非洲海 岸。 1419 年，一支由若昂·贡萨尔维 斯·萨尔科和特里斯唐·瓦斯·特 谢拉带领的葡萄牙探险队发现了 马德拉群岛，他们立即宣称这些岛 屿属于葡萄牙。 第二年，也就是 1420 年，葡萄牙 人开始在这些岛屿上定居。 探险仍在继续，1427 年，贡卡洛·维 略·卡布拉尔发现了亚速尔群岛。 这是亚速尔群岛的海面，想象一 下，当人们在历史上第一个看到这 些岛屿，那一定是不可思议的激动 人心时刻。

paragraphs.en: that must have been. Like Madeira, the Azores were formed by an extinct volcano. They have a warm climate and plentiful rainfall, which makes the environment very lush. And so the Portuguese began to settle the islands in 1433. Today the oldest existing settlement isAngra do Heroísmo which dates back to 1450 and today is a world heritage site. During theseAtlantic voyages, the Portuguese became more and more familiar with the prevailing currents and winds of the NorthAtlantic. And they realized that they could quickly travel out into theAtlantic and return home if they sailed in a clockwise route, because they the
paragraphs.zh: 和马德拉群岛一样，亚速尔群岛也 是由一座死火山形成的。 这儿气候温润，雨量充沛，四周植 物非常繁茂。 于是，1433 年，葡萄牙人在开始在 岛上定居。 如今，现存最古老的定居点是建于 1450 年的安格拉杜伊鲁伊兹穆，也 是世界文化遗产。 在这些航行中，葡萄牙人对北大西 洋的洋流和风向越来越熟悉。他们 意识到，如果顺时针航行，海风和 洋流就会帮助他们很快驶入大西 洋彼岸家园的方向。

paragraphs.en: winds and currents would assist them. The Portuguese called this the Volta do Mar. What the Portuguese had begun to discover was the NorthAtlantic Gyre, which is one of five major ocean gyres around the world. Next came a sustained Portuguese push down the coast ofAfrica. In 1434, the Portuguese Navigator Gil Eannes made it to cape Bajador, the farthest south any European ship had ever gone. This was a major accomplishment because the waters off of Bojador have many treacherous winds and reefs, and the land itself is part of the Sahara dessert, so not a good place to wreck your ship.
paragraphs.zh: 葡萄牙人称之为“环绕海的流”。 再后来，葡萄牙人发现了北大西洋 环流，这是世界上五大海洋环流之 一。 接下来，葡萄牙人开始向非洲海岸 逼近。 1434 年，葡萄牙航海家吉尔·伊安 尼斯到达了巴雅多角，这是当时欧 洲历史上航行最远的一艘船。 这很了不起，因为巴雅多角附近有 很危险的海风和暗礁，而且这片土 地本身就是撒哈拉沙漠的一部分， 并不是一个行船的好地方。

paragraphs.en: Here is what Cape Bojador looks like today. By 1441 the Portuguese had pushed even further south and an expedition led by Nuno Tristão and Antão Gonçalves reached Cape Blanco. Five years later in 1445 Nuno Tristao reached the mouth of the Senegal river, which marks the end of the Sahara desert. Another voyage in the service of Prince Henry, this time led by the Italian captainsAlvise Cadamosto andAntoniotto Usodimare discovered the the archipelago of Cabo Verde in Like the Madeiras orAzores, Cabo Verde is also a volcanic island chain. Here is a map and satellite
paragraphs.zh: 这就是今天的巴雅多角。 到了 1441 年，葡萄牙人进一步向 南推进，努诺·特里斯托和安唐·贡 萨尔维斯率领一支探险队到达了 布兰科角。 五年后的 1445 年，努诺·特里斯 托到达塞内加尔河口，即撒哈拉沙 漠的边缘。 亨利王子还发起了另一次航行，这 次航行由意大利船长阿尔维斯·卡 达摩斯托和安东尼奥托·乌苏代马 雷率领，此行发现了佛得角群岛。 与马德拉群岛或亚速尔群岛一样， 佛得角也是一个火山群岛。这是这 些岛屿的地图和卫星图像。

paragraphs.en: view of the islands. And here is what they look like in the interior. In 1462 the Portuguese founded the settlement of Cidade Velha on the island of Santiago at Cabo Verde. South of Cape Verde theAfrican coast begins to curve eastward, and the Portuguese explorers began to follow that coast with growing confidence and skill. In 1470 João de Santarém and Pero Escobar discovered the islands of Sao Tome and Principe, which at that point were uninhabited. Here is a map of the two islands. And here is what the interior looks like. The Portuguese had reached the lush rainforest-covered region
paragraphs.zh: 这是岛上的景观。 1462 年，葡萄牙人在佛得角的圣地 亚哥岛上建立了殖民地，旧城。 非洲海岸线自佛得角南部开始向 东弯曲，葡萄牙探险者没有退缩， 反而以更强大的信心和技巧沿着 海岸航行。 1470 年，若昂·德·圣塔伦和佩多 鲁·艾斯科巴尔发现了彼时尚无人 居住的圣多美和普林西比群岛。 这是两个岛屿的地图。 这是岛上的景观。葡萄牙人到达了 非洲茂密的雨林地带。这幅图中的 山是圣多美岛最著名的地标之一，

paragraphs.en: ofAfrica. The mountain you see in this picture is one of Sao Tome's great landmarks called Pico Cão Grande or "Great Dog Peak" This next year, in 1471 the Portuguese set up their first settlement on the African mainland called "El Mina," this will play a role in future lessons so remember that name "El Mina" In 1472 the Portuguese discovered Bioko Island, but it was already inhabited and the local people drove them off. Here is a satellite picture of Bioko island, which today is part of Equatorial Guinea. Bioko island is much closer to the mainland than Sao Tome and Principe, and you can even see it on
paragraphs.zh: 被称为“大狗峰”。 第二年，也就是 1471 年，葡萄牙 人在非洲大陆建立了他们的第一 个殖民地，“加纳”。请记住“加 纳”这个名字，我们在以后的课程 中还会讲到。 1472 年，葡萄牙人发现了比奥科 岛，但岛上已经有人居住了，当地 人把他们赶走了。 这是比奥科岛的卫星照片，如今它 属于赤道几内亚。 比奥科岛比圣多美岛和普林西比 岛离陆地近，天晴的时候甚至能够 看到它。这就解释了为什么它已经

paragraphs.en: a clear day. This explains why it was already inhabited. It is reachable by canoe from the mainland. The next milestone came in 1484 when Diego Cao became the first European to reach the mouth of the Congo River. Here is a map of the Congo River watershed and an aerial photo of the river today. The next year, in 1492 something else would happen to begin the next phase of theAge of Exploration. Back in 1469 the Queen of Castille, Isabella, and the King ofAragon, Ferdinand, had gotten married. Then with their combined armies they fought a ten-year war against the Kingdom of Granada, the last
paragraphs.zh: 有人居住了。从陆地上乘独木舟就 可以到达岛上。 下一个里程碑事件发生在1484年， 迭戈·曹成为了第一个到达刚果河 口的欧洲人。 这是如今刚果河流域的地图以及 这条河的航拍照片。 接下来的 1492 年，又发生了另一 件事，标志着探索时代进入了下一 个阶段。 早在 1469 年，卡斯蒂利亚王后伊 莎贝拉和阿拉贡国王费迪南德就 结婚了。 然后他们联合军队与伊比利亚的 最后一个穆斯林王国，格拉纳达王 国，进行了十年的战争。

paragraphs.en: Muslim Kingdom in Iberia. Granada surrendered on January 2nd, 1492. This conquest of Granada, combined with the marriage of Ferdinand and Isabella created the Kingdom of Spain. And this also brought to an end the 770-year long effort by Catholics to reconquer Iberia. In Spanish history these seven centuries are known as the "Reconquista" or "Reconquest" and it is the longest war in human history. That same year, an Italian Navigator named Christopher Columbus proposed an audacious plan to reach Asia, not by sailing aroundAfrica like the Portuguese, but by sailing west into theAtlantic.
paragraphs.zh: 格拉纳达于 1492 年 1 月 2 日投降。 征服格拉纳达，加上费迪南德和伊 莎贝拉的联姻，西班牙王国顺势而 成。 这也终结了天主教长达770年的征 服伊比利亚之战。在西班牙历史 上，这七个多世纪被称为“收复失 地”或“收复领土”，是人类历史 上持续时间最长的战争。 同年，一位名叫克里斯托弗·哥伦 布的意大利航海家提出了一项大 胆的计划，要通过向西航行进入大 西洋到达亚洲，而不是像葡萄牙人 那样绕过非洲。

paragraphs.en: Columbus had made this proposal before, but the two Catholic Monarchs were too pre-occupied with the Reconquista to give him any funding. Now they were interested in his plan. But that is a story for our next lesson. Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If so I have a recommendation for you It is the textbook Voyages in World History by Valerie Hansen and Kenneth Curtis. I have used this as my general-reference textbook while researching these classes and I recommend it highly. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 哥伦布以前就提出过这个建议，但 是这两个天主教君主太专注于“收 复领土”而没有给他任何资金。 现在他们对他的计划感兴趣了。 这就是我们下节课要讲的故事。 好了，各位，我希望你们喜欢这堂 课，并希望学到更多。如果是这样， 那我有一个推荐。 这是瓦莱丽·汉森和肯尼斯·柯蒂 斯的世界历史教科书。在研究这些 课程时，我把它作为我的通用参考 教材，我强烈推荐它。非常感谢。

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we will learn about the discovery ofAmerica. This class will focus just on the year 1492. As we've already learned, in the 1400s the Iberian Peninsula was divided into several Kingdoms. In the north was the Kingdom of Navarre, in the center was Castile, to the west was Portugal, and to the east was Aragon. And then in the south was the Muslim kingdom of Granada. In 1469, Ferdinand, the Prince of Aragon, married Isabella, the
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。 今天我们要讲的是发现美洲。 这堂课我们将聚焦 1492 年发生的 历史。 我们已经知道，15 世纪的伊比利亚 半岛分裂成了几个王国。 北部是拿瓦雷王国，中部是卡斯提 尔，西部是葡萄牙，东部是亚拉贡。 南部是穆斯林的格拉纳达王国。 1469 年，阿拉贡王子费迪南德迎娶 了卡斯提尔公主伊莎贝拉。

paragraphs.en: Princess of Castile. Their marriage unified their two kingdoms and created the Kingdom of Spain. Then once they had become King and Queen of their own kingdoms, they joined their armies together and attacked the Muslim Kingdom of Granada in the south. This war lasted from 1481 to 1492. Granada finally fell on January 2nd 1492. And the unification of Spain was complete. So 1492 also marked the final Catholic victory in the Reconquista. Once Isabella and Ferdinand had completed the unification of their
paragraphs.zh: 他们的婚姻统一了两个王国，建立 了西班牙王国。 后来，当他们成为了自己王国的国 王和王后，他们就联合起来，攻打 南部的穆斯林王国格拉纳达。 这场战争从 1481 年持续到 1492 年。 格拉纳达王国最终于1492年1月2 日陷落了。 西班牙完成了统一。1492 年也标志 着天主教取得了光复运动的最后 胜利。 伊莎贝拉和费迪南德刚一完成了 国家的统一，他们就做了一些非常

paragraphs.en: country, they did something that was very intolerant. On March 31st they issued a decree forcing all non-Christians to either convert to Catholicism or leave Spain by July 31st. On March 31st they issued a decree forcing all non-Christians to either convert to Catholicism or leave Spain by July 31st. This law was of course targeted at the Jews and Muslims of Spain. So in 1492 the Jews of Spain were expelled from their homes and forced to move to other countries. This map shows you where they went.As you can see, many of them fled to the Muslim Ottoman Empire in the east, which was more welcoming of Jews than the
paragraphs.zh: 不宽容的事情。 伊莎贝拉和费迪南德刚一完成了 国家的统一，他们就做了一些非常 不宽容的事情。 3 月 31 日，他们颁布法令，要求所 有非基督徒要么皈依天主教，要么 在 7 月 31 日前离开西班牙。 这项法律当然是针对西班牙的犹 太人和穆斯林的。 1492 年，西班牙的犹太人被赶出家 园，被迫移居他国。 这张地图显示了他们的去向。正如 你所看到的，他们中的许多人逃到 了东方的穆斯林奥斯曼帝国，那里 比西方的基督教王国更受犹太人 的欢迎。

paragraphs.en: Christian Kingdoms in the west. But there was nothing new about this expulsion. All throughout the middle ages, Christian Kings would expel the Jews from their Kingdoms. This map shows you the dozens of different Jewish expulsions that occurred between 1016 and 1593. And the historical memory of these expulsions is one reason why having a country of their own in Israel means so much to many Jews across the world. The other part of the expulsion law was this: Any Jews who chose to leave Spain rather than convert had to leave
paragraphs.zh: 但是在那里，他们还是难逃被驱逐 的命运。 整个中世纪，基督教国王都会将犹 太人驱逐出他们的王国。 对这些驱逐事件的历史记忆，正是 为什么在以色列拥有自己的国家 对世界各地的许多犹太人意义重 大的原因之一。 亚拉里克被逼撤兵，军队实力也削 弱了很多。 驱逐法令的另一部分是这样的： 任何拒绝皈依、选择离开西班牙的 犹太人都必须留下他们的金钱和

paragraphs.en: their money and property behind, on pain of death. In this way, Ferdinand and Isabella basically stole the wealth that the Jews of Spain had built up over the generations. And now, with that extra money on hand, and no longer distracted by a war, they were interested in the proposal brought to them by an Italian navigator named Christoforo Colombo. In English we call him Christopher Columbus. By 1492 Christopher Columbus was an experienced sailor and navigator. He had learned his trade by sailing with the Portuguese on many voyages.
paragraphs.zh: 财产，否则将被处死。 就这样，费迪南德和伊莎贝拉偷走 了西班牙犹太人世代积累的财富。 现在，有了这额外收入，并且他们 也不用再为战争分心，他们便对一 位名叫克里斯托佛洛·科伦坡的意 大利航海家给他们带来的建议产 生了兴趣。 在英语中我们叫他克里斯托弗·哥 伦布。 1492 年，克里斯托弗·哥伦布已经 是一位经验丰富的水手和航海家。 他多次跟着葡萄牙人航海，学会了 一身本领。

paragraphs.en: For example, Columbus was a sailor on the 1481 voyage that built the Portuguese fort at the colony of El Mina in westAfrica. Columbus had a new idea. Instead of going aroundAfrica, he proposed that Ferdinand and Isabella should fund a voyage that would sail west and reachAsia from the other direction. Columbus was trying to get to the Spice Islands, in what is now Indonesia. And when I say "spices" I mean 6 plants in particular: Cassia, Cinnamon, Ginger, Cloves, Nutmeg and Pepper. Many of these plants grew only in the spice islands and nowhere else on earth.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，1481 年哥伦布在葡萄牙前往 西非殖民地埃尔米纳建造葡萄牙 堡垒的航行中，就是一名水手。 哥伦布有了一个新主意。他建议费 迪南德和伊莎贝拉资助一次向西 的航行，从另一个方向到达亚洲， 而不是绕行非洲。 哥伦布试图到达香料群岛，也就是 现在的印度尼西亚。 我说的“香料”是指 6 种植物： 桂皮，肉桂，姜，丁香，肉豆蔻和 胡椒。 其中几种只生长在香料群岛，在地 球上其他地方都没有。

paragraphs.en: During the MiddleAges the Crusaders had brought some of these spices back to Europe, and wealthy Europeans had developed a taste for them. But these spices were enormously expensive luxury goods that only the rich could enjoy. We can understand why they were so expensive by looking at a political map of the world in 1492. The spice islanders would trade the spices with the Malay, who would trade with the Indians, who would trade with the Egyptians, who would trade with the Italians, who would trade with Western Europeans like the Spanish. At each stage of the trade, the middle-men would make a profit,
paragraphs.zh: 在中世纪，十字军曾把这些香料带 回了欧洲，富裕的欧洲人对它们产 生了兴趣。 但是这些香料是非常昂贵的奢侈 品，只有富人才能享用。 我们可以通过观察 1492 年的世界 政治地图来理解为什么它们如此 昂贵。 香料群岛的岛民会和马来人交易 香料，马来人会和印度人交易，印 度人会和埃及人交易，埃及人会和 意大利人交易，意大利人会和西班 牙人等西欧国家的人交易。 在交易的每个阶段，中间商都会获 利，价格也会翻倍。

paragraphs.en: and the price would double. So by the time the spices reached western Europe, they might be 16 times more expensive than they were at the spice islands. In terms of actual prices, here is what we are talking about. One pound of black pepper would cost about 3-days wages for a carpenter in England in the middle 1400s. One pound of ginger would cost 2-days wages. One pound of cinnamon would cost 4-days wages. And one pound of cloves would cost 6-days wages. I put those costs in modern Chinese
paragraphs.zh: 所以当香料到达西欧时，它们可能 比在香料群岛时贵了 16 倍。 就实际价格而言，可以参考以下数 据。就实际价格而言，可以参考以 下数据。 在 15 世纪中期的英国，一磅黑胡 椒的价格相当于一个木匠3天的工 资。 一磅生姜要两天的工资。 一磅肉桂要花 4 天的工资。 一磅丁香要 6 天的工资。 我把这些成本合算成现代中国的

paragraphs.en: prices below to help you understand how expensive they were. This was what Columbus wanted to do: By sailing west and reaching the spice islands directly, the Spanish could cut out the middle men and take control of the spice trade themselves. Cutting out the Middle-man is always a good business plan, but Columbus could not get any country to support his voyage. From 1485 until 1491 he was rejected again and again. The Kingdom of Portugal, the Republic of Venice and the Kingdom of England all refused to finance his voyage.
paragraphs.zh: 价格写在这里，以帮助你理解它们 究竟有多贵。 而这是哥伦布的计划： 向西航行直接到达香料群岛，西班 牙人就可以省去中间商，自己控制 香料贸易。 没有中间商赚差价永远是一个好 的商业计划，但是哥伦布找不到任 何国家来赞助他的航行。 从 1485 年到 1491 年，他一次又一 次地遭到了拒绝。 葡萄牙王国、威尼斯共和国和英格 兰王国都拒绝资助他的航行。

paragraphs.en: When I was a boy I thought that these Kings had refused because everyone else thought that the world was flat and only Columbus knew that the Earth was round. But that is a historical myth, in 1492 all educated Europeans knew that the Earth was round. In fact, they also knew its correct size. And they had known this for 1700 years, ever since the work of the Hellenistic mathematician Eratosthenes. We learned about him in our lesson on the Hellenistic age, but here's a quick review. Eratosthenes measured the triangles formed by two different shadows at Alexandria and Syene at noon on the summer solstice. Then he
paragraphs.zh: 小时候，我认为这些国王拒绝哥伦 布是因为所有人都认为地球是平 的，只有哥伦布知道地球是圆的。 然而这并不是事实。1492 年，所有 受过教育的欧洲人都知道地球是 圆的。事实上，他们还知道它的正 确大小。 自从 1700 年前希腊数学家埃拉托 斯特尼发表其发现后，欧洲人一直 都知道地球是圆的。 我们在希腊化时代的课上已经了 解过埃拉托斯特尼了，但在此，我 们再进行一次简短的回顾。 埃拉托斯特尼测量了夏至中午亚 历山大港和赛尼城这两个地区的 影子形成的三角形。然后他测量了 两地之间的距离。

paragraphs.en: measured the distance between the two places. With that information, Eratosthenes set up a geometry problem. And he concluded that the Earth had a circumference of 40,000 kilometers. His answer was over 99% accurate. What people did not know in 1492 was that there was a continent betweenAfrica andAsia. They thought it was just one giant ocean. And no ship existed in 1492 that could carry enough water to make a voyage of that distance. In fact, it was Columbus who was wrong about the size of the Earth. He thought the Earth was about 25% smaller than its true size and
paragraphs.zh: 根据这些信息，埃拉托斯特尼提出 了一个几何问题。 他的结论是地球的周长是 4 万公 里。 这个答案的正确程度超过 99%。 在 1492 年，人们不知道的是，在 非洲和亚洲之间有一块大陆。当时 他们认为这只是一片巨大的海洋。 而且，在 1492 年，还没有一艘船 能够航行这么远的距离。 事实上，是哥伦布弄错了地球的大 小。 他心中的地球比实际尺寸小了约 25%，而亚洲则向东延伸得更远。

paragraphs.en: thatAsia extended farther east. This map shows you where Columbus thoughtAsia was relative to Europe. As you can see, he thought Japan was located where Mexico actually is. So when Columbus asked the European Kings for funding, their mathematicians did the calculations and concluded that his idea was crazy. But in 1492, Isabella and Ferdinand decided to give it a shot anyway. But they would only supply Columbus with 3 small ships. If he succeeded that would be great, they would have a new source of revenue by controlling the spice trade.And if he failed, it would not
paragraphs.zh: 这张地图显示了哥伦布认为亚洲 相对于欧洲的位置。 如你所见，他认为是日本的地区， 实际上是墨西哥。 因此，当哥伦布向欧洲诸王寻求资 助时，他们的数学家进行了计算， 得出的结论是：哥伦布的想法不切 实际。 但在 1492 年，伊莎贝拉和费迪南 德决定尝试一下。但是他们只给哥 伦布提供 3 艘小船。 如果他成功了，那就太好了，他们 将通过控制香料贸易获得新的收 入来源。如果他失败了，也不会造 成巨大损失。

paragraphs.en: be a big loss. Columbus' first ship was called "La Nina", which means "Little Girl" in Spanish. It was 15 meters long and had a crew of 24 sailors. This is a reconstruction of the original ship that should give you an idea of its size.As you can see it was quite small. The second ship, "La Pinta" means "Painted One" and it was only 2 meters longer and a little bigger. Both the Nina and Pinta were caravel-type ships. The third and biggest ship was the "Santa Maria" which means "Saint Mary" in Spanish. It was twice as big as the other two ships, and was Columbus' flagship.
paragraphs.zh: 哥伦布的第一艘船被称为“尼尼亚 号”，在西班牙语中意为“小女孩”。 它有 15 米长，有 24 名水手。 这是对原船的重建，应该能让你了 解到它的大小。你可以看到，这艘 船非常小。 第二艘船，“平塔号”，意思是“刷 上油漆的”，它只比尼尼亚号长 2 米，稍大一点。 尼尼亚号和平塔号都是轻快帆船。 第三艘也是最大的一艘船是“圣玛 利亚号”，在西班牙语中是“圣母 玛利亚”的意思。 它是另外两艘船的两倍大，是哥伦 布的旗舰。

paragraphs.en: The Santa Maria was a carrack-type ship. It could carry more cargo than the other two, but was less maneuverable. By the way, those names- Nina, Pinta and Santa Maria - are one of those facts that all American children have to learn in grade school. So onAugust 3rd 1492, this little fleet set sail into the unknown. First they travelled south to the Canary islands and arrived 6 days later onAugust 9th. There they got ready for the main voyage. And then they left the Canaries one month later on September 6th. By September 24th they had made it half-way across theAtlantic, but Columbus' crew began to panic and
paragraphs.zh: 圣玛丽亚号是一艘大帆船。它能比 其他两艘运载更多的货物，但机动 性较差。 顺便一提，这三艘船的名字——尼 尼亚号、平塔号和圣玛利亚号—— 是美国小学生必学史实。 于是在 1492 年 8 月 3 日，这支小 舰队驶向了未知的海域。 首先，他们向南前往加那利群岛， 并于 6 天后的 8 月 9 日抵达。他们 在那里为主航程做好了准备。 一个月后的 9 月 6 日，他们离开了 加那利群岛。 到 9 月 24 日，他们已经横渡了半 个大西洋，但是哥伦布的船员们开 始惊慌失措，几乎发生了叛乱。

paragraphs.en: nearly mutinied. No European had ever sailed this far west before, and the men were terrified. But Columbus persuaded them to keep going. Two weeks later, on October 7th they got their first sign that land might not be too far away. They saw a great flock of sea-birds, which must have come from a nearby island. This encouraged the men and gave them hope that they would not die on this voyage. And then, early in the morning on October 12th, 1492 they spotted land! This was the island where they first came ashore. It is an island in the Bahamas and Columbus named it "San Salvador" which means "Holy
paragraphs.zh: 以前没有任何一个欧洲人到过这 么远的西部，他们都吓坏了。但是 哥伦布说服他们继续前进。 两周后的 10 月 7 日，他们得到了 第一个迹象表明：陆地不远了。 他们看到了一大群海鸟，一定是从 附近的一个岛上飞来的。这一现象 让他们看到了在这次航行中活下 去的希望。 然后，在 1492 年 10 月 12 日的清 晨，他们发现了陆地! 这就是他们首次登陆的岛屿。它是 巴哈马群岛中的一个岛屿，哥伦布 把它命名为“圣萨尔瓦多”，意思 是“神圣的救赎”。

paragraphs.en: Salvation." Columbus came ashore and claimed the island for Spain. For the first time in history, Europe andAmerica came into contact with each other, and the world would never be the same. Now who were the people already on San Salvador when Columbus arrived? These people were known as the Taino. They lived in small villages, and got their food through hunting, fishing and farming. There were four main people groups who inhabited the Caribbean in 1492. The Western Taino, the Central Taino and the Eastern Taino, and
paragraphs.zh: 哥伦布上岸，宣称该岛为西班牙所 有。 有史以来第一次，欧洲和美洲产生 了联系，世界从此改变了。 当哥伦布到达圣萨尔瓦多时，岛上 居住着的是什么人？ 这些人被称为泰诺人。他们住在小 村庄里，靠打猎、捕鱼和务农为生。 1492 年居住在加勒比海的主要有 四个民族。 西泰诺人、典型泰诺人和东泰诺 人；而在遥远的东南部岛屿，居住

paragraphs.en: then in the far southeastern islands lived the Caribs. The Carbis were much more violent than the Taino, and the name Caribbean comes from this people. But Columbus thought that he had reachedAsia and was in the Indies, so he called these people the "Indians." It was an incorrect label, but people started using it, so it stuck. And even today we call the Native Americans "Indians" even though they lived thousands of kilometers away from India and Indonesia. We know about these people because Columbus kept a journal and wrote several letters that he intended to give the King and Queen on his return to Spain.
paragraphs.zh: 着加勒比人。 加勒比人比泰诺人更暴力，加勒比 海这个名字就是从这个民族来的。 但是哥伦布认为他已经到达了亚 洲的印度，所以他称这些人为 这是一个错误标签，但随着人们的 广泛使用，便一直沿用了下来。 即使在今天，我们仍称美洲原住民 为“印第安人”，尽管他们生活在 距离印度和印度尼西亚数千公里 的地方。 我们之所以了解这些人，是因为哥 伦布坚持写日志，他还写了几封 信，打算在他返回西班牙时送给国 王和王后。

paragraphs.en: In his letter dated October 12th, he describes the native people as innocent and kind, and extremely willing to share with him and his crew. And he describes the native people as believing that the Spanish were men from heaven and being quite excited by their visit. But already things take a darker turn, because in that same journal entry Columbus notes that he could conquer the entire island with 50 men, and describes how he took seven of the native prisoner. Imagine that! The local people were kind and generous to Columbus, and he responded with cruelty and kidnapped seven of them!
paragraphs.zh: 在 10 月 12 日的信中，他称当地人 “纯真和善”，并且非常愿意与他 和他的船员分享。 据他描述，当地人相信西班牙人是 来自天堂的人，并且对他们的到来 感到非常兴奋。 但事态已然严重起来，因为在同一 篇日志中，哥伦布指出，他可以用 50 个人征服整个岛屿，并描述了他 如何俘虏了 7 名当地人。 想象一下！ 当地人对哥伦布既仁慈又慷慨，而 他却残忍地回报他们，并绑架了其 中的 7 个人!

paragraphs.en: This set the tone for future interactions between Indians and Europeans, but more on that later. Here is a map of Columbus' first voyage. He reached San Salvador in the Bahamas on October 12th. He explored the Bahamas for almost two weeks and then sailed southwest until he reached Cuba on October 28th. He spent the last days of October, all of November, and the first days of December exploring the Cuban coast. Then he sailed southeast and reached the island of Hispanola on December 5th. And then as he was exploring Hispanola, disaster struck on Christmas Day 1492.
paragraphs.zh: 这为印第安人和欧洲人的未来交 流定下了基调，后面我们会讲到更 多。 这是哥伦布第一次航行的地图。他 于 10 月 12 日抵达巴哈马群岛的圣 萨尔瓦多。 他在巴哈马群岛探险了近两周，然 后向西南航行，直到 10 月 28 日到 达古巴。 十月末、十一月和十二月初，他都 在探索古巴海岸。12 月 5 日，他又 向东南航行，到达了西班牙的伊斯 帕尼奥拉岛。 1492 年圣诞节，当他探索伊斯帕尼 奥拉岛时，灾难发生了。

paragraphs.en: The Santa Maria was wrecked off the coast of Hispanola. But with the help of the local Taino, Columbus was able to evacuate the sinking ship and retrieve most of the cargo. But he did not have enough room on the Nina and Pinta for all the men. So he used wood salvaged from the Santa Maria to build a little fort called La Navidad (which is Spanish for Christmas) and left 36 men behind on the island. Then he continued to explore the coast of Hispanola for about two weeks, and left the Caribbean on January 13th, 1493. By mid-January the Nina and Pinta were on their way home to Spain. They reached theAzores on
paragraphs.zh: 圣玛丽亚号在西班牙海岸附近失 事。 但在当地泰诺人的帮助下，哥伦布 得以撤离沉船并取回大部分货物。 然而，尼尼亚号和平塔号不足以容 纳所有人。于是，他用从圣玛利亚 号打捞来的木材建造了一座名为 “拉那维达德”(在西班牙语中是 圣诞节的意思)的小堡垒，并将 36 人留在了岛上。 然后他继续在西班牙拉班诺拉海 岸探险了大约两周，并于 1493 年 1 月 13 日离开了加勒比海。 到 1 月中旬，尼尼亚号和平塔号已 经在返回西班牙的途中。 他们于 2 月 18 日抵达亚速尔群岛

paragraphs.en: February 18th and got resupplied. And then they pushed onwards to Europe, reaching Portugal on March 4th. There the Portuguese interviewed Columbus and found out about his discoveries. And then Columbus returned to Spain where he presented the King and Queen with his Taino prisoners and told them about his discoveries. Soon word of Columbus' voyage had spread all across Europe, and the Europeans began to realize that something was over there to the west. And the world would never be the same. For the first time ever, the Eastern and Western hemispheres of planet Earth were now in contact
paragraphs.zh: 并获得补给。 然后他们继续向欧洲进发，于 3 月 4 日到达葡萄牙。 在那里，葡萄牙人采访了哥伦布， 了解了他的发现。 随后哥伦布回到葡萄牙，将泰诺俘 虏进献给国王和王后，并报告了他 的发现。 不久，哥伦布航海的消息传遍了整 个欧洲，欧洲人开始意识到西方并 非一片空白。 世界从此改变了。有史以来第一 次，地球的东半球和西半球相互接 触。

paragraphs.en: with each other. But the story of that contact, and the empires that grew out of it will have to wait for our next lesson. Okay everyone I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do I have two books to recommend. The first is called 1491: New Revelations of the World Before Columbus by Charles C. Mann. This books helped change that way we understand the Native Americans who inhabited the Americas before Christopher Columbus arrived. And the second book is called 1493: Uncovering the New World Columbus Created.
paragraphs.zh: 下节课，我们会讲到这其中的故 事，和由此产生的帝国。 好了，各位，我希望你们喜欢这节 课。如果你有兴趣了解更多，我有 两本书可以推荐。 第 一 本 名 为 1491: New Columbus，作者是查尔斯·C·曼。 这些书帮助我们改变了对克里斯 托弗·哥伦布到达美洲之前居住在 美洲的印第安人的理解。 第二本书叫做 1493: Uncovering

paragraphs.en: This book is the sequel which examines how the world changed after Columbus broughtAmerica and Europe into contact. Thank you.
paragraphs.zh: 这本书是继哥伦布把美洲和欧洲 联系起来之后，世界如何变化的续 集。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第四讲：西班牙和葡萄牙帝国

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on world history, today we will be learning about the Spanish and Portuguese empire. We will be focusing on the years between 1492 and 1550, here is what that looks like on our timeline. In early 1493, Christopher Columbus returned to Spain. He told King Ferdinand and Queen Isabella about his discoveries and quickly got funding for a second expedition. The first expedition had 3 ships and 90 men. The second expedition had 17 ships and 1500 men. This was not an expedition, it was an invading army.
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎回到世界历史系列讲座，今天 我们将会了解到的是西班牙和葡 萄牙帝国。 我们将聚焦于 1492 年到 1550 年的 时间段，这是它在我们时间线上的 样子。 1493 年初，克里斯托弗·哥伦布回 到了西班牙。 他告诉了国王斐迪南和女王伊莎 贝拉他的发现，并很快获得了第二 次航行的资金。 第一次航行有三条船和九十个船 员，第二次航行则扩充到了 17 条 船和 1500 人。这已经不是一次远 航，这是一支侵略军。

paragraphs.en: And in 1493 a war of conquest began. The native Taino tried to fight back, but the Spanish had steel, horses, war dogs and guns. The Taino had stone and wood. Any men or women who organized resistance were tortured and killed in horrific fashion. After the conquest of Hispanola, Jamaica, Puerto Rico and Cuba, the Spanish began setting up farms worked by Indian slaves and owned by men from Spain. These farms were called Encomiendas, and they became central to the new Spanish colonies and their economies. Of course, these were slave farms, and control was enforced with terrible violence.
paragraphs.zh: 1493 年，一场征服战争开始了。当 地的泰诺人试图反击，但西班牙人 有钢铁，马匹，军犬和枪炮，泰诺 人只有石头和木棍。 所有组织反抗的男女都以残酷方 式被折磨或杀害。 在征服了伊斯帕尼奥拉、亚美利 加、波多黎各和古巴之后，西班牙 人开始建立以印第安奴隶为主要 劳动力、为西班牙人所有的庄园。 这些庄园被称为 Encomiendas，它 们成为了新西班牙殖民地以及其 经济的中心。 当然，它们是奴隶庄园，控制是以 残酷暴力得以实现的。

paragraphs.en: But none of my words can do the horror of these events justice. Our primary source for all of this was an early Spanish settler who was horrified by what he saw, renounced his farm, freed his slaves, and became a priest devoted to trying to protect the Indians. His name was Bartolome de las Casas and his books tell us what the Spanish did to the poor native Taino of the Caribbean. He describes all sorts of cruelties committed by Columbus and his men in the early years of Spanish colonization. And he describes how the Taino men and women were worked to death, both on Spanish farms and in Spanish mines. And de Las Casas writes that this
paragraphs.zh: 但我不会在这里为这些事件的恐 怖性质辩护。我们得以了解这些的 第一手资源来自一位早期西班牙 殖民者，他因所目睹的东西而惊惧 不已，因此解散了他的庄园，释放 了他的奴隶，并做了牧师，致力于 保护这些印第安人。 他 的 名 字 是 Bartolome de las Casas，正是他的著作告诉我们， 西班牙人对可怜的加勒比土著泰 诺人都做了些什么。 他描述了所有哥伦布和他的手下 在西班牙殖民早期所犯下的暴行。 他也描述了泰诺男女如何劳役至 死，可能在西班牙庄园里，也可能 在西班牙人开的矿场里。 de Las Casas 写道，这样的暴行消

paragraphs.en: cruelty wiped out the native population of the Caribbean. But it wasn't just Spanish cruelty, European diseases like Smallpox also killed a large percentage of the population. 99% of the indigenous population of the Caribbean was wiped out in the years between 1493 and 1550. It is one of the great atrocities in human history. So, with the help of their weapons and diseases the Spanish had conquered Hispanola, Cuba, Puerto Rico and Jamaica by 1518. They also had set up bases along the Panama coast. This map shows you the Spanish empire in that year. In the meantime, many other navigators had begun exploring the
paragraphs.zh: 灭了加勒比海地区的土著人口。 但问题不仅是西班牙人的残暴，欧 洲疫病如天花等同样杀掉了大量 人口。 在 1493 年到 1550 年，加勒比海地 区 99%的土著居民死亡。这是人类 历史上最大的暴行之一。 所以，在武器和枪炮的帮助下，西 班牙人在 1518 年之前征服了伊斯 帕尼奥拉、亚美利加、波多黎各和 古巴。他们还在巴拿马海岸建立了 基地。这张地图显示了这一年的西 班牙帝国。 与此同时，许多其他航海家也开始 探索新世界。

paragraphs.en: New World. Columbus himself made 4 trips to theAmericas between 1492 and 1504. And soon other European countries were launching their own expeditions. From 1497-1500, John Cabot explored Newfoundland for the English. And in those same years Vasco de Gama continued the Portuguese exploration ofAfrica, but this time he rounded the Cape of Good Hope, and made it all the way to India. De Gama also discovered that you could get aroundAfrica faster if you first sailed southwest and then southeast, that way the South Atlantic Gyre and trade winds would make your ships go much
paragraphs.zh: 哥伦布本人就在 1492 年到 1504 年 之间进行了四次探索美洲的旅行。 不久后，其他欧洲国家也开始了他 们自己的远航。从 1497 年到 1500 年，John Cabot 为英国人探索了新 发现的土地。 与此同时，Vasco de Gama 继续着 葡萄牙队非洲的探索，但这一次， 他绕过了好望角，全力向印度行 进。 达伽马还发现，如果你首先向西南 航行，然后转向东南，你将更快地 抵达非洲。南大西洋环流和季风会 帮助你的船航行得更快。

paragraphs.en: faster. The next year Pedro Cabral tried this new route and accidentally bumped into Brazil in 1500. As in the Caribbean this led to first contact between the Indigenous Brazilians and Europeans, and it began the Portuguese colonization of Brazil. At the same time another navigator namedAmerigo Vespucci was wondering if this new land was reallyAsia. So he went on one voyage to the Caribbean and Mexican coast. Another expedition to the northern part of the SouthAmerican coast. Athird trip the southern parts of the SouthAmerican coast.
paragraphs.zh: 第二年，Pedro Cabral 尝试了这一 新航线，并意外地在 1500 年进入 了巴西。 正如在加勒比海地区发生的一样， 这导致了巴西土著居民与欧洲人 的第一次接触，然后成了葡萄牙人 殖民巴西的开始。 与此同时，另一位名叫 Amerigo Vespucci 的航海家正在考虑，这块 新大陆是否真的是亚洲。 所以他向加勒比和墨西哥海岸又 进行了一次航行。 还有一次远航探索了南美洲海岸 的北部。 第三次航行则探索了南美洲海岸 的南部。

paragraphs.en: And a fourth trip to Brazil again. On the basis of this exploration, Amerigo Vespucci became the first person to declare that this New World was much too close to be Asia. Amerigo reported that this was a new continent. On the basis ofAmerigo's new information, European map-makers began producing new maps that showed this land as a separate continent. One of the most important of these was the map, Universalis Cosmographia, by the German mapmaker Martin Waldseemüller. Here is a bigger picture of that map. And this map is also important
paragraphs.zh: 第四次航行再次探索了巴西。 在这次远航的基础上，Amerigo Vespucci 成了第一个宣布新世界 距离太近，因此不可能是亚洲的 人。 他提出，这是一块新大陆。 在 Amerigo 提供的新信息的基础 上，欧洲的地图作者们开始生产新 的地图，在其中这块大陆被画成一 个单独的洲。 其中最重要的版本之一是这版地 图，出自日耳曼地图制造商 Martin Waldseemüller 之 手 ， 名 为 这是这张地图的一个较大的版本。 这 张 地 图 的 重 要 性 还 在 于

paragraphs.en: because Waldeemuller gave the new continent the name "America" in honor ofAmerigo Vespucci. The name stuck, and that's why this continent is still called "America" today. Meanwhile, as Spain focused on this new “America”, Portugal continued its push into Africa and Asia... Their main goal was to seize control of the Asian spice trade. And led by theAdmiral Alfonso de Albuquerqe, they began doing just that in 1509. On February 3rd, 1509, the Portuguese defeated the Egyptian navy at the Battle of Diu. Then they fought a series of battles
paragraphs.zh: Waldseemüller 为 了 纪 念 Amerigo Vespucci 而将新大陆命 名为 America。 这就是为什么直到今天我们还将 它叫做美洲。 就在西班牙的精力集中在新“美 洲”的同时，葡萄牙仍在继续对非 洲和亚洲的探索。 他们的主要目的是控制亚洲香料 贸易。 在 海 军 司 令 Alfonso de Albuquerqe的领导下，他们在1509 年开始了行动。 1509 年 2 月 3 日，葡萄牙人在第乌 战役中击败了埃及海军。 然后他们发动了一系列战役，在整

paragraphs.en: and set up naval bases all across Asia and the Middle East. This allowed their navy to gain control of the Spice Trade. Before 1509, this was the flow of the Spice trade. But after 1509, the Portuguese cut the Egyptians and Italians out of the trade. This greatly weakened the Mamluk Egyptians, since the spice trade brought in a great portion of their revenue. The Ottoman empire to the north saw an opportunity. And so, from 1516 to 1517 the Ottomans invaded the Mamluk Egyptians and conquered them. By 1517, Egypt was now part of the
paragraphs.zh: 个亚洲和中东建立起了海军基地， 这使得他们的海军可以掌握香料 贸易的控制权。 到 1509 年之前，这是香料贸易的 流向。 但在 1509 年以后，葡萄牙人将埃 及人和意大利人赶出了香料贸易。 这严重削弱了马穆鲁克苏丹国的 埃及人，因为香料贸易在他们的收 入中占到很大比重。 奥斯曼帝国在北方看到了机会。 于是，从 1516 年到 1517 年，奥斯 曼人入侵了马穆鲁克埃及人，并征 服了他们。 到 1517 年，埃及已经是奥斯曼帝

paragraphs.en: Ottoman Empire. Meanwhile, back inAmerica... Exploration was continuing.An explorer named Vasco Balboa heard reports of a great sea on the other side of the Panama mountains. So in 1513, Balboa led an expedition across the Isthmus of Panama, and became the first European to ever see the Pacific Ocean from theAmerican side. Back in the Caribbean, Christopher Columbus turned out to be a very bad governor, and he was fired after 7 years. The Caribbean had 5 Spanish governors in the years between 1492 and 1524. The fifth was a man named Diego Velazquez.
paragraphs.zh: 国的一部分了。 与此同时，让我们回到美洲…… 探索仍在继续，其中一个名叫 Vasco Balboa 的探险家听到报告， 在巴拿马山脉的另一边有一片很 大的海。 所以在 1513 年，Balboa 领导的一 支探险队穿越了巴拿马地峡，他成 了第一个从美洲一边看见太平洋 的欧洲人。 回到加勒比地区，哥伦布被证明是 一个非常糟糕的统治者，他在七年 后被解职。 回到加勒比地区，哥伦布被证明是 一个非常糟糕的统治者，他在七年 后被解职。

paragraphs.en: The Caribbean had 5 Spanish governors in the years between 1492 and 1524. The fifth was a man named Diego Velazquez. By 1518, the conquest of the Caribbean was complete and Velzaquez began to raise an army with his secretary Hernan Cortez. Their goal was to launch an invasion of the mainland. So just who was living in the 1518？ America mainland in Well, the most advanced society was theAztec empire, located in what is now southern Mexico. Here is a closer map of theAztec empire. Their capital city was one of the largest cities in the world, it had great buildings of stone, and was
paragraphs.zh: 从 1492 年到 1524 年，加勒比地区 共有五名西班牙统治者。其中的第 五个名叫 Diego Velazquez。 到 1518 年，对加勒比的征服已经 完 成 ， Velzaquez 与 他 的 秘 书 Hernan Cortez 开始组织军队。他 们的目标是入侵大陆。 所以，1518 年生活在美洲大陆上的 人是谁呢？ 当时最为发达的社会是阿兹特克 帝国，位于今天的墨西哥南部。 这是阿兹特克帝国的更详细的地 图。 他们的首都是当时全世界最大的 城市之一，它有伟大的石头建筑， 并在 Texococo 湖的中央建造了一

paragraphs.en: built on a man-made island in the middle of lake Texococo. But theAztecs were an aggressive warlike people who frequently attacked their neighbors. They did this because theAztec religion required human sacrifice, and these wars gave them a steady supply of sacrificial victims. Cortez began his invasion in 1518 and moved further inland. He made the acquaintance of a brilliantAztec slave name Malina. She quickly became his translator, cultural interpreter, chief diplomat, head spy, and girlfriend. With Malina's help, Cortez was able to make alliances with the many local tribes who hated theAztecs.
paragraphs.zh: 个人工岛。 但阿兹特克人是是一个好战的群 体，他们经常攻击他们的邻居。 他们这样做是因为阿兹特克宗教 要求人祭，这些战争会给他们以祭 祀品的稳定供应。 Cortez 在 1518 年开始入侵并向内 陆推进。 他结识了一个名叫 Malina 的聪明 的阿兹特克奴隶，她很快成了他的 翻译，文化解说者，主要外交官， 头号间谍，和女友。 在 Malina 的帮助下，Cortez 得以 与许多仇恨阿兹特克人的当地部 落结成了同盟。

paragraphs.en: And Cortez' army reached the capital city. Having failed to stop the Spanish with either gifts or warfare, the Aztec emperor Moctezuma decided to welcome this newcomer into his city. But Cortez quickly took Moctezuma captive in his own palace and started using him to pillage as much gold as possible. Eventually theAztec's grew sick of their weak emperor, and Moctezuma was killed in a riot. The Spanish then had to escape Tenochtitlan in the middle of the night on June 30th, 1520, many of the died in the fighting. And then, Cortez had to face the wholeAztec army at the Battle of
paragraphs.zh: Cortez 的军队打到了首都。 无论是礼物还是战争都未能阻止 西 班 牙 人 ， 阿 兹 特 克 皇 帝 Moctezuma 决定欢迎这位新来者进 入他的城市。 但 Cortez 迅速在 Moctezuma 自己 的宫殿里俘虏了他，并开始用他来 掠夺尽可能多的黄金。 最后，阿兹特克人对他们的软弱的 皇帝日益愤怒，Moctezuma 最终在 一次暴乱中被杀。 而西班牙人必须在 1520 年 6 月 30 日午夜逃离 Tenochtitlan，许多人 在战斗中被杀。 随后，Cortez 必须在一周以后的奥 托姆巴战役中面对整支阿兹特克

paragraphs.en: Otumba a week later. The Spanish and their allies were outnumbered 10 to 1. But Cortez and his men had steel, guns, and horses, three weapons that terrified theAztecs. Cortez was able to lead a cavalry charge directly at theAztec general and kill him. In the resulting confusion that Spanish army was able to escape. Perhaps theAztec's could have pursued Cortez and finished him off, but in the autumn of 1521, Smallpox began to spread in the Aztec empire. It killed 25% of the population including several importantAztec leaders. 1/4 of theAztec's died, but almost none of the Europeans were infected, because the Europeans had developed immunities to the disease
paragraphs.zh: 军队。西班牙人和他们的盟友不得 不以一敌十。 但 Cortez 和他的人有钢铁、枪炮 和马匹，这三样武器令阿兹特克人 感到恐惧。Cortez 领导一支骑兵直 扑阿兹特克将军，并格杀了他。在 由此导致的混乱中西班牙军队得 以逃脱。 本来阿兹特克人是可以追击 Cortez 然后消灭他的，但在 1521 年秋天，天花开始在阿兹特克帝国 当中蔓延，这夺走了四分之一人口 的生命，其中包括许多重要的阿兹 特克领导人。 阿兹特克有四分之一人口死亡，但 几乎没有欧洲人感染，因为欧洲人 已经在过去的几百年里发展出了 对这种疾病的免疫力。

paragraphs.en: over the centuries. This epidemic was the first of the great demographic disasters that reduced the Indigenous population of Mexico from 22 million in 1520 to only 1 million in 1575. The smallpox epidemic gave Cortez time to reorganize and in 1521 he put Tenochtitlan under siege from April 28th toAugust 13th. TheAztec's fought hard but Spanish weapons and disease were too powerful, and they were eventually defeated. The lastAztec emperor, Cuauhtémoc, was captured on August 13th 1521, and theAztec empire came to an end. In the following years, the Spanish set about destroying traditional Aztec culture and religion.
paragraphs.zh: 这种传染病是最大的人口灾难中 的头名，它将墨西哥原住民人口从 1520年的2200万削减到了1575年 的只有 100 万。 天花瘟疫给了 Cortez 重振旗鼓的 时间，1521 年从 4 月 28 日到 8 月 31 日，他发起了针对特诺奇蒂特兰 的围城战。 阿兹特克人拼死战斗，但西班牙人 的武器和疾病太强大了，他们最终 依然战败。最后一个阿兹特克国王 夸乌特莫克在 1521 年 8 月 13 日被 俘，阿兹特克帝国由此覆灭。 在最后的几年里，西班牙人开始摧 毁传统的阿兹特克文化和宗教。

paragraphs.en: And they replaced it with Roman Catholicism. And on the ruins of Tenochtitlan they built Mexico City. At the same time as the conquest of theAztec empire, another Navigator named Ferdinand Magellan believed that he could be the first man to sail around the world. With the support of the Spanish Crown, Magellan organized a fleet of 5 ships and left Spain on September 20th, 1519. The returned on September 6th, 1522. They were the first humans to ever travel all the way around the Earth. But Magellan himself never made it, he was killed in the Philippines onApril 27th, 1521.
paragraphs.zh: 并代之以罗马天主教。 在特诺奇蒂特兰的废墟上，他们建 造起了墨西哥城。 与对阿兹特克帝国的征服同时，另 一个名叫斐迪南·麦哲伦的航海家 相信他将会是第一个完成环球航 行的人。 在西班牙王室的支持下，麦哲伦在 1519 年 9 月 20 日组织了一支由五 艘船构成的舰队，离开了西班牙。 他们在 1522 年 9 月 6 日返回。他 们是第一批完成了环球航行的人。 但麦哲伦本人并未完成这件事，他 在1521年4月27日在菲律宾被杀。

paragraphs.en: In fact, out of an initial expedition of 5 ships and 235 men, only 1 ship with 18 survivors, led by Juan Sebastian Elcano made it back, Afew years later, another Spanish soldier named Ferdinand Pizarro wanted to imitate the conquests of Cortez. From 1531 - 1532 he led an invasion of SouthAmerica. His target was the Incan Empire. Pizarro was able to take the Incans by surprise and capture their emperor. And they followed up that victory with a ten-year long conquest of the Inca. At the same time, an explorer
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，在最初出发的五艘船与 235 人当中，只有由 一艘船和船上的 18 个人成功返回 了。 几 年 以 后 ， 另 一 个 名 叫 Ferdinand Pizarro 的西班牙士 兵试图模仿 Cortez 的远征。 从 1531 到 1532 年，他发动了对南 美的入侵。 他的目标是印加帝国。 Pizarro 突袭了印加人并俘虏了他 们的皇帝。 在这次胜利之后，他们开始了对印 加帝国长达十年的征服。

paragraphs.en: namedAlvar Nunez Cabeza de Vaca became the first European to explore the interior of North America from 1527 to 1536. Another explorer named Francisco Vazquez de Coronado made it farther into what is now the American Southwest. They became the first Europeans to see the Grand Canyon. As well as the Great Plains. And the giant buffalo herds that lived on them. Hernando de Sota led an expedition that explored theAmerican south-east from 1539 to 1542. By 1550 both Portugal and Spain had built up the first trans-continental empires in history.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，一位名叫 Alvar Nunez Cabeza de Vaca 的探险家在 1527 年到 1536 年成了第一个探索北美 腹地的欧洲人。 另一位名叫 Francisco Vazquez de Coronado 的探险家进一步探索了 今天被叫做美国西南部的地区。 他们成了第一批目睹大峡谷的欧 洲人。 还有大平原。 还有生活在其上的巨型野牛群。 1542 年之间进行了一次对美国东 南部的探索行动。 到 1550 年之前，葡萄牙和西班牙 都建立起了史上第一批跨洲际帝 国。

paragraphs.en: Here is the Spanish Empire in 1550. As you can see, by then, Spain was in control of much ofAmerica, as well as the Philippines inAsia. The Portuguese settled Brazil, but they focused more on building a trade empire But while all of these discoveries and changes were happening outside of Europe, pushed on by the countries of Spain and Portugal, within Europe a revolution in thought was taking place that would change the world forever. And it all began with a short essay, written by an unknown professor in a small German college town. Please join me next time for that story. Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed
paragraphs.zh: 这是 1550 年的西班牙帝国，正如 你所见，西班牙控制了美洲的大部 分地区，以及亚洲的菲律宾。 葡萄牙则占据了巴西，他们更多地 将精力放在了建设商业帝国之上。 但在所有这些发现和变化都发生 在欧洲以外，由诸如西班牙以及葡 萄牙这样的国家所推动的同时，在 欧洲内部正发生一场观念上的革 命，它将永远地改变世界。 这一切开始于一篇很短的文章，它 是由一位不知名的教授在日耳曼 一个大学城写就的。下一次请和我 一起来了解这个故事。 好了，我希望大家喜欢这次课程并

paragraphs.en: that lesson and want to learn more. If you do, I have two recommendations for you. The first is the book written by Bartolome de las Casas called A ShortAccount of the Destruction of the Indies. This is our primary source for knowing about what happened in the Caribbean after Columbus's second voyage. The second is the journal kept by one of the surviving member of the Magellan expedition:Antonio Pigafetta's The First VoyageAround the World. That's all for now, thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 且希望了解更多。如果你们想的 话，我有两个推荐。 写成的一本书，名为《印第安人毁 灭简述》，这是我们所能了解到的， 在哥伦布的第二次航行之后在加 勒比地区所发生的事的第一手资 料。 其二是麦哲伦航队中的幸存成员 之一 Antonio Pigafetta 留下的 日志，《第一次环球航行》。 这就是今天的全部内容，谢谢大 家。

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on the Middle Ages. Today we will learn about the Protestant Reformation. This lesson will focus on events between the years 1320 and 1522. But it will focus on events around the year 1500. This is a map of Europe in the 1500s. And this map shows the major religious divisions.As you can see, Western Europe was Roman Catholic. Being a good Roman Catholic involves following a set of 7 practices known as the 7 sacraments.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的中世纪系 列讲座。今天我们将学习新教改 革。 本课将集中讲述 1320 年至 1522 年 间的事件。但重点是 1500 年左右 的事件。 这是 16 世纪欧洲的地图。 这张地图显示了主要的宗教分野。 如你所见，西欧是罗马天主教国 家。 成为一名好的罗马天主教徒需要 遵循 7 种实践，即 7 种圣礼。

paragraphs.en: These practices organized and gave meaning to life for almost all Western Europeans in the year 1500. And the central role of the Roman Catholic church in European life also made it enormously wealthy. The church had several ways of making money. There was a 10% tax on income called the Tithe. The church owned about 1/4 of the land in Europe and it collected rent on that land. And people made donations to church, especially right before they died, when they were worried about the afterlife. The church was also a rigid
paragraphs.zh: 这些实践在 1500 年组织并赋予了 几乎所有西欧人生命的意义。 罗马天主教会在欧洲生活中的中 心地位也让它变得非常富有。 教会有几种赚钱的方法。 对收入征收 10%的税，叫做什一税。 教会拥有欧洲四分之一的土地并 收取租金。 人们还会给教堂捐款，特别是在他 们死前，那是他们最担心来世的时 候。 教会也是一个严格的等级制度。罗

paragraphs.en: hierarchy. The Bishop of Rome or "Pope" was at the top, below him were the Cardinals, or high ranking bishops who selected popes. Below them were the bishops, high ranking church officials who oversaw cities and regions. And then came the common priests who led individual churches. Unfortunately, the church in the MiddleAges was often very corrupt, and many church reformers tried to fix this corruption. Usually they focused on three practices that they thought were wrong: 1) Simony- or the selling of church offices. 2) Crusades- or the concept of Holy War.
paragraphs.zh: 马主教或“教皇”是最高的，低于 他的是红衣主教，或者说高阶主 教，他们负责选举教皇。 在他们之下是主教，教会的高级官 员，监督城市和地区。 然后是领导各个教会的普通牧师。 不幸的是，中世纪的教会通常非常 腐败，许多教会改革者试图解决这 种腐败。 通常改革集中在三个他们认为是 错误的实践上: 1) 买卖圣职罪 ——也就是出 售教会职务。 2)十字军——也就是圣战的概念 3)赎罪券——也就是买卖赦免权。

paragraphs.en: 3) Indulgences- or the selling of pardons. The first of these reformers was the priest and scholar John Wycliff who lived in England from 1320 to 1384. Wycliff was a very well educated man who strongly opposed these practices as being against the teachings of Jesus. But since the Bible was written in Latin in those days, most normal people could not study the words of Jesus for themselves. So Wycliff began a movement to translate the Bible from Latin into English. Wycliff developed a following of people who agreed with him. These followers became known as the "Lollards" and they travelled around England making English
paragraphs.zh: 第一个改革者是牧师兼学者约 翰·威克利夫，他于 1320 年至 1384 年居住在英格兰。 威克利夫是一个受过良好教育的 人，他强烈反对这些做法，认为它 们违背了耶稣的教义。 但是，由于圣经是用拉丁文写的， 大多数正常人无法自己学习耶稣 的话。于是，威克利夫开始了一场 将《圣经》从拉丁语翻译成英语的 运动。 威克利夫培养了一批赞同他的追 随者。这些追随者被称为"罗拉德 派"。他们周游英格兰，翻译圣经， 试图改革教会。

paragraphs.en: translations of the Bible and trying to reform the church. In 1395 they created a list of 12 reforms that they thought the church needed and presented these to Parliament. These were known as the 12 Conclusions of the Lollards. These criticized many church practices including crusades and the idea of a priesthood with special powers. But it was difficult for the Lollards to spread their ideas, because their Bibles and messages had to be copied by hand. This was a slow and expensive process. Then in the early 1400s the church and government of England decided that the Lollards were a threat to
paragraphs.zh: 1395 年，他们列出了 12 项他们认 为教会需要进行的改革，并提交给 国会。这就是后来为我们所熟知的 罗拉德派的 12 条结论。 他们批评了许多教会的做法，包括 十字军东征和拥有特殊权力的主 教制度。 但是罗拉德派很难传播他们的思 想，因为他们的圣经和信息必须用 手抄写。 这是一个缓慢而昂贵的过程。 然后在 15 世纪初，英格兰教会和 政府认为罗拉德派是对他们权力 的威胁，于是他们开始监禁和杀害

paragraphs.en: their power, and they began to imprison and kill these reformers. Usually Lollards were killed by being burned alive. Wycliff died before this persecution of his movement, so the church and government officials decided to destroy his bones instead. In 1425 his body was dug up, his bones were burned, and the ashes were scattered in a river. But the reform impulse was strong among many Christians. To the east in Bohemia (now the modern day Czech Republic) another reformer named Jan Huss saying similar things. Huss was 49-years younger than Wycliff, and lived on the other side of Europe, but Wycliff's ideas still
paragraphs.zh: 这些改革者。罗拉德派通常是被活 活烧死的。 威克利夫死于这场迫害运动之前， 因此教会和政府官员决定毁掉他 的骨头。 1425 年，他的尸体被挖出来，骨头 被烧，骨灰撒在河里。 但是改革冲动在许多基督徒群体 中都非常强烈，在东面的波西米亚 (现在的捷克共和国)，另一位名叫 扬·胡斯的改革家也说了类似的 话。 胡斯比威克利夫小 49 岁，住在欧 洲的另一边，但威克利夫的思想仍 然激励着他。

paragraphs.en: inspired him. So Jan Huss began preaching sermons in Prague. But he taught the people not in Latin, but in their native Czech so they could understand him. Jan Huss very educated and intelligent priest, who had come from a peasant family, speaking the common language and using it to teach the Bible and criticize Church corruption. The common people of Prague loved him! Jan Huss also criticized church corruption and the idea of priests with special power to forgive since. He thought that was against the Bible. He also condemned Crusades as
paragraphs.zh: 于是扬开始在布拉格讲道。但他不 是用拉丁语，而是用他们的母语捷 克语来教他们，这样他们就能理解 他。 扬·胡斯是一个受过良好教育的聪 明的牧师，他来自一个农民家庭， 说着普通人的语言，用它来教导圣 经和批评教会的腐败。 布拉格的老百姓都爱他! 扬·胡斯还批评了教会的腐败，以 及自那以后拥有特殊赦免权的牧 师的想法。他认为这是违反圣经 的。 他还谴责十字军东征以及教会赚

paragraphs.en: unchristian as well as all the corrupt ways the church made money. But the church and government authorities saw Jan Huss as a threat to their power, just like the Lollards, and they had him arrested and burned to death on July 6th, 1415. John Wycliff and Jan Huss both tried to reform the church in the late 1300s and early 1400s, but they both failed. However, in the mid 1400s, two events would change the situation in Europe. The first event was the invention of the printing press. The printing press was invented in Mainz Germany in the 1440s. It was invented by a German
paragraphs.zh: 钱的所有腐败方式是违背基督教 的 。 但是教会和政府当局视扬·胡斯为 对他们权力的威胁，就像罗拉德派 一样，他们在 1415 年 7 月 6 日逮 捕并烧死了他。 约翰·威克利夫和扬·胡斯在 14 世纪末和 15 世纪初都试图改革教 会，但都失败了。 然而，在 15 世纪中期，两件事改 变了欧洲的局势。 第一个事件是印刷机的发明。 印刷机是 15 世纪 40 年代在德国美 因茨发明的。 它是由一个叫约翰内斯·古腾堡的

paragraphs.en: metalsmith named Johannes Gutenberg. Gutenberg put together the idea of a wine-press and the idea of moveable metal type to create a printing press. (Although moveable type had already existed in China for 400 years) In the 1460s, Gutenberg's invention created a whole new industry: publishing. The first two cities to have a printing press were the German cities of Mainz and Strasbourg. By 1470 the printing press had spread to France and Italy. In the 1470s it really took off as an invention and had spread across
paragraphs.zh: 德国金属工匠发明的。 古腾堡把压酒机的想法和可移动 金属活字的想法结合起来，创造了 一台印刷机。 (虽然活字印刷术在中国已经存在 了 400 年) 14 世纪 60 年代，古腾堡的发明创 造了一个全新的产业:出版业。 最早拥有印刷机的两个城市是德 国的美因茨和斯特拉斯堡。 到 1470 年，印刷术传到了法国和 意大利。 在 15 世纪 70 年代，它作为一项发 明真正起飞，并在 1480 年传遍欧

paragraphs.en: Europe by 1480. All the great cities of Europe had one by that year. By 1490 it had spread to the smaller cities. And by 1500, every city and large town had its own press. The era of mass-media had begun. The second major event was the fall of Constantinople. As we learned last series, the Byzantine Empire finally ended in 1453 when Constantinople fell to the Ottoman Turks. This caused many Greek scholars to flee west so that they could live in countries ruled by Christian Kings. They brought with them the knowledge of the Greek language. The Renaissance Humanists were
paragraphs.zh: 洲。到那一年，欧洲所有的大城市 都有了一台印刷机。 到 1490 年，它已经蔓延到较小的 城市。 到 1500 年，每个城市和大城市都 有了自己的出版社。大众传媒的时 代已经开始。 第二件大事是君士坦丁堡的沦陷。 上一节我们讲过，拜占庭帝国最终 在 1453 年灭亡，君士坦丁堡落入 奥斯曼土耳其人手中。 这导致许多希腊学者逃离西方，以 便能够生活在由基督教国王统治 的国家。 他们带来了希腊语的知识。文艺复 兴时期的人文主义者是古希腊人 的狂热粉丝，所以这些学者在西欧

paragraphs.en: big fans of the ancient Greeks, so these scholars found jobs as language teachers all across Western Europe, and created a revival of the Greek language there. One such Humanist was the Catholic scholar Desiderius Erasmus. In 1514, he used the new Printing Press to publish a dual language New Testament with Latin on one side and Greek on the other. Now scholars all across Europe could study the New Testament in its original language. Erasmus's New Testament was a hit. And in the German town of Wittenberg it inspired one man in particular. He was a priest named Martin
paragraphs.zh: 各地找到了语言教师的工作，并在 那里创造了希腊语的复兴。 天主教学者德西德里乌斯·伊拉斯 谟就是这样一位人文主义者。 1514 年，他使用新的印刷机出版了 一部一边是拉丁文另一边是希腊 语的双语新约。现在全欧洲的学者 都可以研究新约的原始语言了。 伊拉斯谟的新约非常成功。在德国 的维滕贝格镇，它启发了一个人。 他是一位名叫马丁·路德的牧师，

paragraphs.en: Luther, and was very excited to study Erasmus's New Testament. From 1512 - 1517, Luther carefully studied the bible full-time. Meanwhile Church corruption continued to be a problem. Let's look at some numbers to understand the scale of this corruption. In 1514, the Pope was named Leo X and he had an annual income of 580,000 Italian Ducats. In modern terms that would be equivalent to an income of 150,000,000 RMB. In 1514, a very rich man named Albert paid the church 48,000 ducats to become the bishop of the German region of Brandenburg. This is an example of simony.
paragraphs.zh: 对研究伊拉斯谟的新约感到非常 兴奋。 从 1512 年到 1517 年，路德全身心 投入到圣经的学习中。 于此同时，教会的腐败问题愈发严 重。 我们来看看这些数字就能对腐败 有个大体的了解了。 在 1514 年，教皇利奥十世一年的 收入是 58 万意大利达科特。按现 在的货币来换算相当于是一亿五 千万人民币。 在 1514 年，一个叫做阿尔伯特的 土豪付了教堂 48000 达科特，成为 了勃兰登堡地区的主教。这就是买 卖圣职的一个例子。

paragraphs.en: Albert had to borrow a lot of money to buy his position, so he asked the pope to approve a sale of indulgences so that he could pay back his debts. The Pope agreed to authorize the sale, ifAlbert agreed to send him half of the money. He had large building projects in Rome that he wanted to fund. The Pope even sent a specialist priest to oversee the sale of these indulgences. His name was Johann Tetzel and he even had an advertising slogan: "When a coin in the coinbox rings, a soul from purgatory springs." The idea behind the sale of indulgences was a place called purgatory.
paragraphs.zh: 阿尔伯特借了很多钱来购买这个 职位，所以他请求教皇允许他售卖 赎罪券，这样他才能还清债务。 教皇同意了，但需要阿尔伯特把一 半的钱交给他。他在罗马有很多大 型建筑项目，他需要为之投资。 教皇甚至还派去了一位专业的牧 师去监督赎罪券的售卖。 他的名字是 Johann Tetzel，他甚 至还有一句标语： “当投币箱里的一枚硬币响起时， 一个来自炼狱的灵魂就会跳出来。” 出售赎罪券的背后的概念是一个 叫炼狱的地方。

paragraphs.en: All Catholics agreed that baptised Christians who were very good went to heaven after they died, and non-Catholics and very evil people went to hell. But they also believed in an in-between place where Catholics went if they were baptised but hadn't been very good in their lives. This place was called Purgatory and you had to wait there for thousands of years before you could enter heaven. But if you were afraid that your family member might be in purgatory, you could buy an indulgence and that would make God so happy that your family member would go straight from Purgatory to Heaven.
paragraphs.zh: 所有的天主教徒都认同受洗的基 督教徒死后会去天堂，而非天主教 徒和非常邪恶的人会去地狱。 但他们也相信存在一个居中之处， 接受过洗礼但在其生年表现得不 够好的天主教徒将去往那里。这个 地方叫炼狱，你必须在那里等上千 年才能进入天堂。 但如果你担心你的家人可能身在 炼狱的话，你可以买一个赎罪券， 这会让上帝很高兴，你的家人就可 以直接从炼狱去天堂。

paragraphs.en: In that way, buying an indulgence was similar to the way people burn money inAsia to give their ancestors. But Luther objected to the selling of indulgences. He had realized that neither purgatory, not the power of priests to forgive sins is in the Bible. So Luther concluded that the selling of indulgences was a corrupt scam. So Luther concluded that the selling of indulgences was a corrupt scam. On October 31st, 1517 he published an essay called the 95 Theses. His essay made 95 points, and it criticized Catholic practices that Luther believed went against the Bible.
paragraphs.zh: 从这个意义上说，购买赎罪券就像 亚洲人烧钱给祖先一样。 但是路德反对出售赎罪券。他意识 到圣经中既没有炼狱，也没有给牧 师赦罪的能力。 但是路德反对出售赎罪券。他意识 到圣经中既没有炼狱，也没有给牧 师赦罪的能力。 所以路德总结说，出售赎罪券是一 种腐败的骗局。 1517 年 10 月 31 日，他发表了一篇 名为《95 条论纲》的论文。 他的文章提出了 95 个点，并批评 了他认为违背圣经的天主教做法。

paragraphs.en: According to tradition, Luther published his essay by nailing it to the church door in Wittenberg so that everyone in town could read it. Wittenberg was a University town, and pretty soon all the college students were discussing Luther's essay. Then in January of 1518, some of his friends translated it from Latin into German. Now everyone was talking about the 95 Theses. The printers realized that they had a best seller on their hands and started making thousands of copies to sell. So in January of 1518 The 95 Theses was translated into German. By February it had spread across
paragraphs.zh: 按照传统，路德把他的文章钉在威 登堡教堂的门上，这样镇上的每个 人都能读到。 威登堡是一个大学城，很快所有的 大学生都在讨论路德的文章。 1518 年 1 月，他的一些朋友将其从 拉丁语翻译成德语。 现在每个人都在谈论这《95 条论 纲》。印刷商们意识到他们手上有 一本畅销书，于是开始印成千上万 册来卖。 1518 年 1 月，《95 条论纲》被译 成德语。 到 2 月份，它已经传播到了德国各

paragraphs.en: Germany. And by April of 1518 it had been translated into a dozen languages and was being read all across Europe. That was the power of the printing press. By 1519, the Church realized they had a problem on their hands. In January of 1519 Luther's boss, Cardinal Cajetan, called Luther in for a meeting to discuss his essay. The meeting started off quite polite. But one thing led to another, and soon the two men were shouting at each other. That same there was a debate over the essay which also turned into a shouting match. The next year, in 1520, Luther published 3 books to further
paragraphs.zh: 地。 到 1518 年 4 月，这本书已经被翻 译成十几种语言，在欧洲各地广为 流传。这就是印刷机的力量。 1519 年，教会意识到他们面临着一 个问题。 1519 年 1 月，路德的上司、卡捷坦 红衣主教召集路德开会讨论他的 文章。会议一开始很有礼貌。但是 事情一件接一件，很快这两个人就 互相大喊大叫起来。 同样，这篇文章也引发了一场辩 论，最后演变成了一场争吵比赛。 第二年，1520 年，路德出版了 3 本 书来进一步拓展他的思想。

paragraphs.en: develop his ideas. At the same time, the Pope realized that he needed to take Luther seriously. In June 1520 he published an official rebuke to Luther. The Pope identified 41 errors in Luther's work. He told Luther that he needed to apologize for these 41 mistakes and publicly admit that he was wrong. Otherwise he would be kicked out of the church. Luther received the Pope's warning letter in December of 1520. He thought about what to do, then strode across the courtyard to where some people were keeping themselves warm around a fire.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，教皇意识到他需要认真 对待路德。 1520 年 6 月，他发表了一篇官方谴 责路德的文章。 教皇指出了路德工作中的 41 个错 误。他告诉路德，他需要为这 41 个错误道歉，并公开承认自己错 了。 否则他将被赶出教会。 路德在 1520 年 12 月收到了教皇的 警告信。 他思考了一下该怎么办，然后大步 穿过院子，来到一群人围着火炉取 暖的地方。

paragraphs.en: Luther threw they warning into the blaze. He was not going to turn back. Luther could challenge the church like this, because unlike Jan Huss or John Wycliff, he was protected by a powerful prince named Frederick III. Frederick had founded the University of Wittenberg in 1505, and now Luther had made it famous all across Europe. Frederick was determined to protect his star teacher. In 1520 the Holy Roman Empire was ruled by Charles V. You may remember from previous lessons that Charles V was also the King of Spain and the Spanish empire, as well as southern Italy,
paragraphs.zh: 路德把他们的警告扔进火里。他不 会回头的。 路德可以像这样挑战教会，因为不 像扬·胡斯或约翰·威克利夫，他 受到一位名叫弗雷德里克三世的 强大王子的保护。 弗雷德里克于 1505 年创办了威登 堡大学，而现在路德使它在整个欧 洲闻名。弗雷德里克决心保护他的 明星老师。 1520 年，神圣罗马帝国由查理五世 统治。 你可能还记得以前的课，查理五世 也是西班牙国王以及西班牙帝国， 以及意大利南部，荷兰和奥地利的 王。

paragraphs.en: the Netherlands and Austria. He wore many crowns! But Charles' power was limited in the Holy Roman empire, and he needed to listen to his powerful nobles like Frederick III. Frederick convinced Charles to invite Luther to a big meeting at the German city of Worms. There Luther could defend his ideas in front of the Emperor, all the nobility of the Holy Roman Empire, and high officials of the Church. This sort of meeting was called a "Diet" so history remembers this meeting with the named: "The Diet of Worms". Charles V promised Luther that he would be safe if he travelled to
paragraphs.zh: 他戴着许多王冠! 但查理的权力在神圣罗马帝国是 有限的，他需要听取像弗雷德里克 三世这样有权有势的贵族的意见。 弗雷德里克说服查尔斯邀请路德 参加在德国沃姆斯市举行的一次 大型会议。 在那里路德可以在皇帝、神圣罗马 帝国的所有贵族和教会的高级官 员面前捍卫他的思想。 这种会议被称为“帝国议会”，所 以历史中记载这次会议的名字是: “ 沃尔姆斯议会”。 查理五世承诺路德，会保证他在沃 尔姆斯之旅中的安全，路德同意

paragraphs.en: Worms, and Luther accepted. The meeting began onApril 21st, 1521. It must have been a terrifying moment for Luther. There he was, a 37-year old common priest and college professor, speaking to the most powerful men in Central Europe. But for the next 3 days he defended and explained his beliefs in front of them all. Charles V was unimpressed, but many other princes in that room began to think that Luther was making very good points. He concluded his defense with these famous words: Unless I am convinced by the
paragraphs.zh: 了。 会议于 1521 年 4 月 21 日开始。 对路德来说，这一定是一个可怕的 时刻。他在那里，一位 37 岁的普 通牧师和大学教授，在与中欧最有 权势的人对话。 但在接下来的三天里，他在众人面 前为自己的信仰辩护和解释。 查理五世对此无动于衷，但房间里 的许多其他王公开始认为路德的 观点很有道理。 他最后用这句名言为自己辩护: 除非我相信圣经的证词或明确的

paragraphs.en: testimony of the Scriptures or by clear reason (for I do not trust either in the pope or in councils alone, since it is well known that they have often erred and contradicted themselves), I am bound by the Scriptures I have quoted and my conscience is captive to the Word of God. I cannot and will not recant anything, since it is neither safe nor right to go against conscience. Here I stand, I can do no other. God help me! Amen The judges at the meeting were divided, and so no decision was reached about Luther. But Charles V had decided that Luther was a heretic and was looking for a way to arrest him. So on his way back to Wittenberg,
paragraphs.zh: 原因(因为我不相信教皇或议会本 身,众所周知,他们经常犯错误,自 相矛盾), 我所引的圣经约束我， 我的良心也被神的道捆绑。我不能 也不会改变任何事情，因为违背良 心既不安全也没有权利。 我站在这里，我别无选择。上帝帮 助我!阿门 会上的法官意见不一，所以没有就 路德作出决定。 但查理五世认为路德是个异教徒， 正在寻找逮捕他的方法。 因此，在返回威登堡的路上，弗雷

paragraphs.en: Frederick III had his men "pretend kidnap" Luther and then hide him at his castle near the town of Wartburg. There Luther spent a year in hiding from 1521 to 1522. He actually lived and wrote in this room. During that year, Luther decided to keep himself busy by making a translation of the Bible from the original Greek into the German language. That way everyone in Germany could read the words of Jesus for themselves, not just scholars who knew Greek or Latin. Luther published his German language New Testament in September of 1522, and soon the printing presses were making
paragraphs.zh: 德里克三世让手下“假装绑架”了 路德，然后把他藏在了瓦特堡附近 的城堡里。 从 1521 年到 1522 年，路德在那里 躲藏了一年。 他在这个房间里生活和写作。 在那一年里，路德决定投身于把圣 经从希腊语原文翻译成德语的工 作中。这样，在德国的每个人都可 以自己阅读耶稣的话，而不仅仅是 懂希腊语或拉丁语的学者。 路德于 1522 年 9 月出版了他的德 语《新约》，很快印刷机就印了成 千上万册。路德成为历史上第一位 畅销书作家。

paragraphs.en: thousands of copies. Luther had become history's first best-selling author. And all across Germany people began reading his Bible and questioning Church practices for themselves. Now the reform movement was unstoppable. But the details of that story, and its consequences must wait for out next class when we discuss the wars of religion. Okay everyone I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do I have a recommendation for you: That is the documentary series: Martin Luther-The Idea that Changed the World, produced by
paragraphs.zh: 整个德国的人都开始阅读他的圣 经，并质疑教堂的做法。 现在改革运动势不可挡了。 但这个故事的细节及其后果必须 等到下节课我们讨论宗教战争时 再讲了。 好了，各位，希望你们喜欢这堂课 并希望能学到更多。如果有的话， 我有个建议: 那就是 PBS 电视台制作的纪录片 《马丁路德——改变世界的理念》。

paragraphs.en: PBS television. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 非常感谢。

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, welcome back to our lecture series on world history, today we will be learning about the Protestant Reformation. This lecture will focus on the years between 1521 and 1559. Last time we learned how Martin Luther defended his ideas at the Diet of Worms, inApril of 1521. Then he spent a year in hiding at Wartburg Castle to keep him safe. To keep himself busy during this year, Luther created the first-ever translation of the New Testament from Greek into German. And he published this translation in September of 1522.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 课程，今天我们要学习的是宗教改 革。 这节课，我们将关注 1521-1559 年 这一时期。 上节课，我们学习了马丁·路德是 如何在 1521 年四月的沃尔姆斯会 议捍卫自己的观点的。 在那之后的一年，为了保得自身平 安，他藏身于沃伯格城堡。 那时，为了找点事做，路德首创性 地将希腊语《新约》翻译为了德语。 1522 年 9 月，这一译文出版了。

paragraphs.en: It was a hit and all across Germany people started buying copies of Luther's German New Testament. One person who was inspired by all this was Ulrich Zwingli. Like Luther, Zwingli was also a Catholic priest who believed that the Church needed reform. But Zwingli lived south of Luther in the city of Zurich. Here is a picture of Zurich today. In January of 1523 Zwingli convinced the city government to support his ideas. The city government ruled that 1) Decorations should be removed from churches. 2) The doctrine of transubstantiation is rejected. 3) Monasteries would be closed and
paragraphs.zh: 译文一经出版，就在德意志大受欢 迎，各地的人们开始购买路德的德 语版《新约》。 这一切启发了一个人：乌尔里希·茨 温利。 和路德一样，茨温利也是一 位天主教牧师，也认为教会 需要改革。不同的是，茨温 利住在苏黎世市，在路德的 南边。 这是如今苏黎世的照片。 1523 年 1 月，茨温利说服苏黎世政 府支持宗教改革。政府规定： 1）除去教堂的装饰； 2）废止圣餐变体论； 3）关闭修道院，将这些资源分给穷 人； 4）城市里不允许乞讨，为需要工作

paragraphs.en: their resources given to the poor. 4) No beggars would be allowed in the city, those who needed work would be given jobs. But as Protestants began removing decoration from churches, they went a little far and began to burn and smash the artwork. This horrified those who wanted to remain traditional Catholics.
