To help you understand the difference. This is an example of what a Roman Catholic church can look like, see all the decoration? And this is what a Reform Church often looks like. The Reformists believed that churches should focus on the word of god, and that decorations were a distraction and waste of time at best
paragraphs.zh: 的人提供工作。 但是当新教徒开始从教堂移除装饰 物时，他们做的更过分了——他们 开始焚烧和粉碎教堂里的艺术品。 这吓坏了那些想继续做传统天主教 徒的人。 为了帮助你们理解装饰前后的区 别，我们先来看一个罗马天主教堂 的图片，看到这些装饰了吗？ 这张照片是改革后的教堂照片。 改革派认为，在教堂里就应该专注 于上帝的话语，而装饰会让人分心， 浪费人时间，更糟糕的是，这会引 起神像崇拜。

paragraphs.en: and idolatry at worst. But now that anyone could read the Bible, within Zwingli's reform movement other disagreements began to emerge. Some of Zwingli's most ardent students began to argue that infants should no longer be baptized, since they didn't understand its meaning. This was going too far for Zwingli, so these men split off and formed the "Anabaptist" church which rejected infant baptism. In 1529, Luther and Zwingli met to see if they could create an agreement and unify their two churches. The two reformers agreed about everything except the meaning of the sacraments.
paragraphs.zh: 但是在茨温利的改革运动中，在任 何人都可以阅读圣经的情况下，慢 慢产生了其他分歧。茨温利最激进 的一些学生开始争辩说，婴儿不应 该再受洗礼，因为他们根本就不明 白受洗礼的意义。 这对茨温利来说太过分了，所以这 些人分裂出去，成立了“再洗礼派” 教会，拒绝接受婴儿洗礼。 1529 年，路德和茨温利见面并尝试 统一这两个新教派。 两位改革家除了对圣礼的意义各自 保留意见外，对其他一切都达成了 一致。

paragraphs.en: Luther still held on to the Catholic view that the sacraments were a way for God's grace to enter a believer's life. But Zwingli believed that God's grace entered your life through faith and that the sacraments were just rituals you did to show the world that you were a Christian. This disagreement prevented the two churches from unifying. So by 1530, there were now 5 major Christian churches in Europe. The Roman Catholics, theAnabaptists, the Reform Church, the Lutherans, and the Eastern Orthodox. The three churches that emerged as a result of the movement that Luther began became known as "The Protestant" churches.
paragraphs.zh: 路德坚持天主教的信条，认为圣礼 是上帝向信众传达福祉的途径。 但是茨温利认为上帝的福祉是通过 信仰传达到你的生活中的，圣礼只 是你向世界展示你是基督徒的仪 式。 这一分歧使两个教派无法统一。 所以到了 1530 年，欧洲有 5 个主要 的基督教教会。罗马天主教会，再 洗礼派教会，改革派教会，路德派 教会和东正教。 三个形成于路德运动后的教会就被 称为“新教”教会。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, north in England, Luther's New Testament was inspiring another Church reformer named William Tyndale. Tyndale got a copy of Luther's translation and was impressed. He decided that the same thing needed to be done for English speakers, and he decided that he was the person to make that translation. Tyndale was a brilliant human being. Born in 1494, he started university at the age of 12 and graduated at the age of 18 in 1512. For the next 12 years he led an academic life and became fluent in French, Greek, Hebrew, German, Italian, Latin, Spanish in addition to his native English. In 1522, at the age of 28, he became inspired by Luther's New Testament
paragraphs.zh: 同时，在北边的英格兰，路德的《新 约》启发了另一位教会改革者—— 威廉·廷代尔。 廷代尔拿到了路德的译本后，深受 启发。他决定在英国做同样的事情 ——翻译《新约》。 廷代尔是一个才华横溢的人。他生 于 1494 年，12 岁开始上大学，1512 年，18 岁的他大学毕业。 在接下来的 12 年里，他不断学习， 精通法语、希腊语、希伯来语、德 语、意大利语、拉丁语、西班牙语 以及，他的母语——英语。 1522 年，28 岁的他受到路德《新约》 的启发，决定将《圣经》从希腊语

paragraphs.en: and decided that he would make his own translation of the bible from Greek and Hebrew into English. In 1523 he was denied permission by church authorities in England. So in 1524, he decided to travel to Germany and begin work anyway in secret. In 1525 Tyndale assumed a false name and travelled to Hamburg Germany, where Lutheranism was quite popular. There he labored in secret for 2 years until he had completed a translation of the New Testament in 1526. Tyndale's translation was immediately declared illegal, but was also immediately a best seller in England.
paragraphs.zh: 和希伯来语翻译成英语。 1523 年，英格兰教会拒绝了他的翻 译请求。 1524 年，他决定去德意志秘密开始 翻译工作。 1525 年，廷代尔化名前往路德教很 受欢迎的德意志汉堡市。 他在那里秘密工作了两年，1526 年，他完成了《新约》的翻译。 廷代尔的译文立即被宣布为禁书， 但也立即成为了英格兰畅销书。

paragraphs.en: His Bible was printed as a very small book that you could easily hide and was then shipped over to England by smugglers where it was sold on the Black Market. Unfortunately, an English spy named Henry Phillips pretended to be Tyndale's friend and then betrayed him to the authorities in 1535. Tyndale was arrested, thrown into prison, and executed on October 6th, 1536. His crime was illegally translating the Bible. Tyndale was strangled to death and then his body was burned.And with that, the world lost one of the greatest linguistic geniuses who had ever lived.
paragraphs.zh: 他的《圣经》被印成很小的一本书， 可以很容易地把它藏起来，然后被 走私者运到英格兰，在黑市上出售。 不幸的是，一个名叫亨利·菲利普 斯的英国间谍伪装成廷代尔的朋 友，然后在 1535 年向当局出卖了 他。 廷代尔被捕入狱，并于 1536 年 10 月 6 日被处决。 他的罪名是非法翻译圣经。 廷代尔被勒死，尸体被焚烧。就这 样，世界上少了一位当世最伟大的 语言学天才之一。

paragraphs.en: As the ropes tightened around Tyndale's neck, his final words were these: "Oh Lord! Open the King of England's eyes!" So who was this King whose eyes Tyndale wanted God to open? Well, his named was Henry VIII, he was the second King of the Tudor dynasty. Henry was married to a Spanish Princess named Catherine of Aragon, she was the daughter of Ferdinand and Isabella. As you can see from this family tree, Catherine was also the aunt of Charles V the great King of Spain and the Holy Roman Emperor. By 1525, Henry had begun to grow
paragraphs.zh: 当廷代尔的脖子被绳子勒紧 时，他说了这样一句话： “噢上帝！请打开英格兰国王的双 眼吧！” 所以廷代尔想让上帝打开哪一位国 王的双眼呢？ 好了，他的名字是亨利八世，都铎 王朝的第二任国王。 亨利娶了西班牙阿拉贡的凯瑟琳公 主，她是费迪南德和伊莎贝拉的女 儿。 从这个家谱中可以看出，凯瑟琳还 是西班牙国王查理五世和神圣罗马 帝国皇帝的姑姑。 到了 1525 年，亨利开始对凯瑟琳失

paragraphs.en: impatient with Catherine. She had been pregnant seven times, but only one baby, their daughter Mary, had survived into adulthood. But Henry was desperate for a son to secure the succession.And by 1525 Catherine was 40 and unlikely to give him any more children. So Henry's eye began to wander and he fell in love with one of Catherine's ladies-in-waiting.A 24-year old woman namedAnne Boleyn. Henry wanted to divorce Catherine and marryAnne. But the problem is that Jesus forbade Christians to get divorced for any reason other than adultery, and Catherine had been faithful to Henry. So in this case, only the Pope could
paragraphs.zh: 去耐心。她曾怀孕七次，但只有一 个女孩玛丽活到了成年。 但亨利迫切希望有个儿子来继承王 位。到了 1525 年，凯瑟琳已经 40 岁了，不可能再给他生孩子了。 于是亨利的目光开始瞄准别人，他 爱上了凯瑟琳的一位侍女——24 岁的安妮·博林。亨利想和凯瑟琳 离婚，迎娶安妮。 但是现在有一个问题，耶稣禁止基 督徒以通奸以外的任何理由离婚， 而凯瑟琳并没有对亨利不忠。 所以在这种情况下，只有教皇才能

paragraphs.en: grant Henry his divorce. But Catherine was the aunt of the powerful Charles V, who did not want this divorce. So the Pope was trapped between two powerful Kings, one who wanted the divorce and one who opposed it. But the Pope was more afraid of Charles because he had a giant army just miles away from the Pope's home in Rome. So the Pope never said "yes" and never said "no", he just kept delaying the divorce. Henry grew more and more impatient. But by 1530, the Protestant movement had begun to grow secretly in England, encouraged by Tyndale's bibles.
paragraphs.zh: 允许亨利离婚。 但是凯瑟琳是有权有势的查尔斯五 世的姑姑，查尔斯五世不想他们离 婚。 教皇被夹在两个有权势的国王之间 了，一个想离婚，一个不让离婚。 但是教皇更忌惮查尔斯五世，因为 查尔斯五世有一支庞大的军队，距 教皇家仅数英里路。 所以教皇不敢说“好”，更不敢说 “不”，只能采取拖延之计。 亨利越来越不耐烦了。1530 年，在 廷代尔《圣经》的启发下，新教运 动在英格兰悄然兴起。

paragraphs.en: AndAnne Boleyn had introduced Henry to her personal priest, a man named Thomas Cranmer. Henry had also grown close to a man named Thomas Cromwell, who had become his chief minister. But these two men were both secret Protestants, and they began to whisper in Henry's ear: "Why not break from the Church of Rome and become a Protestant? Then you could be the head of the Church of England. You could appoint an archbishop to give you that divorce, and you could seize all the church property in England." Henry liked that idea. So in 1533, Henry declared that the Church of England would be separate from the Roman Catholic Church.
paragraphs.zh: 安妮·博林把亨利介绍给她的私人 神父托马斯·克兰默。亨利还和一 个叫托马斯·克伦威尔的人走得很 近，后者后来成为了亨利的首席大 臣。 这两个人都是秘密的新教徒，他们 开始在亨利耳边低语:“为什么不脱 离罗马教会，成为一名新教徒呢？ 这样的话，你就可以成为英格兰国 教会的领袖了。你可以任命一位大 主教让你离婚，你还可以没收英格 兰所有的教会财产。” 亨利喜欢这个主意。 1533 年，亨利宣布英格兰教会从罗 马天主教会中脱离。

paragraphs.en: So now there would be 6 major churches in Europe. Henry then appointed Thomas Cranmer the newArchbishop of Canterbury, which made him the leader of the Church of England. Thomas Cranmer's first action was to give Henry that divorce, on May 23rd, 1533. And then, 5 days later, he married Anne Boleyn to Henry. This enraged the Pope who excommunicated both Henry and Thomas Cranmer. But Henry didn't care, Anne Boleyn was already pregnant. For the summer of 1533,Anne and Henry were happy together. And then on September 7th,Anne
paragraphs.zh: 现在欧洲有 6 个主要的教会。 亨利随后任命托马斯·克兰默为坎 特伯雷大主教，担任英国教会的领 袖。 托马斯·克兰默的第一个动作是在 1533 年 5 月 23 日让亨利离婚。 5 天后，他让亨利娶了安妮·博林 为妻。 这激怒了教皇，他将亨利和托马 斯·克兰默逐出了教会。 但是亨利不在乎，安妮·博 林已经怀孕了。 1533 年的夏天，安妮和亨利幸福地 生活在一起。 9 月 7 日，安妮生下了……

paragraphs.en: gave birth... ... to a daughter. They named her Elizabeth. Henry was disappointed but they tried again. However, over the next 3 yearsAnne had three more pregnancies, but all of them ended in a miscarriage. Eventually Henry also grew frustrated withAnne's inability to give him a son. But the other aspects of the split with Rome continued apace. Henry closed down all the Catholic monasteries and seized their property for himself. With no one left to take care of them, the English monasteries fell apart. Today you can see these ruined buildings all across England.
paragraphs.zh: ……一个女儿。他们给她取名伊丽 莎白。 亨利很失望，他们又试了一次。然 而，在接下来的三年里，安妮虽然 又怀孕了三次，但都以流产告终。 最后，亨利再次因为安妮不能给他 生儿子而感到沮丧。 但另一方面，英格兰教会与罗马教 会的分裂形势仍在迅速发展。亨利 关闭了所有的天主教修道院，并将 其财产据为己有。 由于无人照管，英国修道院分崩离 析。如今，你可以在全英国看到这 些被毁的建筑。

paragraphs.en: By 1536 Henry had fallen in love with another woman named Jane Seymour and was looking for a way to get rid ofAnne. So immediately after her third miscarriage, Henry hadAnne put on trial for the false charge of committing adultery. The trial began on May 15th. Anne was found guilty on May 17th. And she was beheaded on May 19th. Anne was executed for committing adultery, but that charge was almost certainly false. Her real "crime" was failing to give Henry a son. Henry and Jane were married just 11 days after the death ofAnne.
paragraphs.zh: 到了 1536 年，亨利爱上了另一个叫 简·西摩的女人，并想办法摆脱安 妮。 因此，在安妮第三次流产后，亨利 立即以通奸罪对她进行了审判。审 判于 5 月 15 日开始。 5 月 17 日，安妮被判有罪。 她在 5 月 19 日被斩首。 安妮因通奸而被处决，但这一指控 几乎可以肯定是子虚乌有。她真正 的“罪过”是没能给亨利生个儿子。 安妮死后 11 天，亨利和简就结婚 了。

paragraphs.en: And on October 12th, 1537, Jane gave Henry the son he had always wanted: Edward VI. But the birth had been long and difficult and Jane was injured by the process and died of an infection 12 days alter. Henry remarried 3 more times, but none of his later wives gave him any children. Mary, Elizabeth and Edward were his only offspring. Of course, Henry doted on Edward, nothing in the world was too good for his precious baby boy. But Henry himself had grown enormously fat and unhealthy during the 1530s. He was 185 centimeters tall, and at the age of 18 he had been a svelte 91 kilograms.
paragraphs.zh: 1537 年 10 月 12 日，简给了亨利梦 寐以求的儿子：爱德华六世。 但分娩过程漫长而艰难，简在分娩 过程中受了伤，并在 12 天后死于感 染。 亨利又结了三次婚，但他后来的妻 子都没有给他生孩子。 玛丽、伊丽莎白和爱德华是他仅有 的儿女。 毫无疑问，亨利很宠爱爱德华，他 的宝贝儿子配得上世上任何东西。 但亨利本人在 16 世纪 30 年代变得 非常肥胖多病。他身高 185 厘米， 在 18 岁时只有 91 公斤。

paragraphs.en: But by the late 40s his weight had ballooned to 145 kilograms and he was suffering from diabetes. At the age of 56, Henry VIII died. This picture shows the dying King instructing his ministers to support his son Edward. Henry died on January 28th, 1547. And Edward was crowned King a month later on February 28th. But Edward was a boy king, he was only 9 years old at the time of his coronation. Unfortunately, Edward's reign was short. He died at the age of 15 from tuberculosis and his crown passed to his older sister Mary. Mary was the daughter of Catherine
paragraphs.zh: 但到了 40 多岁，他的体重飙升至 145 公斤，并患上了糖尿病。 56 岁时，亨利八世去世。 这张照片描绘了垂死的国王命令大 臣们支持他的儿子爱德华的场景。 亨利于 1547 年 1 月 28 日去世。 一个月后的 2 月 28 日，爱德华加冕 成为国王。 但爱德华是个男孩国王，他加冕时 只有 9 岁。 不幸的是，爱德华的统治时间很短。 他 15 岁时死于肺结核，王位传给了 他的姐姐玛丽。 玛丽是阿拉贡的凯瑟琳的女儿，还

paragraphs.en: ofAragon, remember, Henry had split from the Church of Rome just to divorce her mother. And her main enemy was Thomas Cranmer, the man who had given Henry his divorce from Catherine 23 years before. Mary had Thomas Cranmer burned to death when he would not renounce his Protestant beliefs. The she set about killing Protestant leaders all across the country, usually by burning them to death. For this reason, later generations of Protestants called her "Bloody Mary" But Mary was unable to have a son, and she died of Uterine cancer after just 5 years, so the crown passed to
paragraphs.zh: 记得吗，亨利脱离罗马教会的原因 就是为了和她母亲离婚。 她的主要敌人是托马斯·克兰默， 这个 23 年前让亨利和凯瑟琳离婚 的男人。 当托马斯·克兰默不肯放弃他的新 教信仰时，玛丽把他烧死了。 她开始杀害全国各地的新教领袖， 通常情况下是把他们烧死。 因此，后来的新教徒称她为“血腥 玛丽”。 但玛丽没有儿子，5 年后的 1558 年 11 月 17 日，玛丽死于子宫癌，她 的妹妹伊丽莎白继承了王位。

paragraphs.en: her younger sister Elizabeth after her death on November 17th, 1558. Elizabeth was formally crowned on January 15th, 1559.And all across the country Protestants rejoiced, because this daughter ofAnne Boleyn was also a Protestant. So England changed religions again. It had been Catholic under Henry, then Protestant under Henry. Then more Protestant under Edward. Then Catholic again under Mary, and now it was Protestant once more. But Elizabeth understood how divided England was under religion after all this switching back and forth. So she decided that the Church of England would be a very
paragraphs.zh: 伊丽莎白于 1559 年 1 月 15 日正式 加冕。全国的新教徒都很高兴，因 为安妮·博林的女儿也是新教徒。 所以英格兰宗教又一次改弦更张。 在亨利治下由天主教变为新教，在 爱德华的统治下变得更加新教化， 在玛丽的统治下，天主教再次盛行， 而现在又变成了新教。 但伊丽莎白明白，在这一切反复无 常之后，英格兰的宗教十分混乱。 她决定将英格兰教会变成一个折中 的新教教会。

paragraphs.en: moderate Protestant Church. It would be separate from the Church of Rome, but would still keep many Catholic traditions. This graph shows you how the Church of England had a mix of Protestant and Catholic practices. Catholic practices are in green, Reform practices are in orange. Meanwhile more changes were also happening elsewhere across Europe. But those changes are a story for next time. So please join me to learn about Calvinism and the Later Reformation. Also, if this time period interests you, then I have a good recommendation for you.
paragraphs.zh: 它将与罗马教会分离，同时仍保留 许多天主教传统。 这张图表展示了英格兰教会是如何 将新教和天主教结合起来的。 天主教的做法是绿色的，改革的做 法是橙色的。 与此同时，欧洲其他地区也发生了 更多变化。 但这些变化将在下节课中讨论。所 以请和我一起学习加尔文主义以及 之后的宗教改革。 同时，如果你对这一时期感兴趣， 那么我推荐一个好东西。

paragraphs.en: It is the novel "Wolf Hall" by Hilary Mantel. Although it is a work of historical fiction, it tries to stay pretty close to the facts of Henry VIII's reign, while making the story dramatic.
paragraphs.zh: 我的推荐是希拉里·曼特尔的小说 《狼厅》。 虽然这是一部历史小说，但它试图 在保持故事富有戏剧性的同时，尽 量还原亨利八世统治时期的史实。

titleZh: 第七讲：加尔文教派和宗教改革

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we will be learning about the wars of religion. This lesson will focus on the years between 1522 and 1560. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. So far in this lesson we have repeatedly talked about an important King: Charles V. Charles V was the King who oversaw the Diet of Worms, which we learned about two lessons ago. And Charles V was the king who blocked Henry's divorce in the last lesson. Charles V was the grandson of Ferdinand and Isabella, the King
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程。今天我们将学习宗教战 争。 这节课的内容主要发生在 1522 年 至 1560 年间。在时间轴上是这样 的。 到现在，我们在课程中已经多次提 到一位重要的国王:查尔斯五世。 我们在前两节课里了解到，查理五 世主持了沃尔姆斯会议。 查理五世同时也是上节课里阻止亨 利离婚的那位国王。 查理五世是费迪南德和伊莎贝拉的 孙子，这对国王和王后曾派遣哥伦

paragraphs.en: and Queen who had sent Columbus on his expedition. He was descended from them through his mother Joanna of Castille. Charles was also the grandson of Mary of Burgundy, who ruled the low countries which are now Belgium, the Netherlands, Flanders and Luxembourg.And he was the grandson of Maximillian I, the Holy Roman Emperor. Charles V inherited the territory of all 4 of his grandparents. This map shows you which of Charles's grandparents controlled which territory. And it was also under Charles that the Spanish conquered theAztecs and the Inca. So all of Spain's American territory was also under
paragraphs.zh: 布远征。他的血脉是从他的母亲， 卡斯蒂利亚的乔安娜传下来的。 查尔斯还是勃艮第的玛丽的孙子， 玛丽统治着低地国家（即现在的比 利时、荷兰、弗兰德斯和卢森堡地 区）。他也是神圣罗马帝国的皇帝 马克西米利安一世的孙子。 查尔斯五世继承了他（外）祖父母 四人的领地。 从这张地图可以看出查尔斯的祖父 母们分别统治着哪些地区。 在查理的统治下，西班牙人征服了 阿兹特克人和印加人。因此他还控 制着西班牙在美洲的领地。

paragraphs.en: his control. All of this made Charles V easily the most powerful King in Europe. Charles V was also the King of the Spanish and Germans during both the conquest ofAmerica and the Protestant Reformation. So he is a key figure for this period of European history. But one problem with being King of so much territory is that you have to fight constantly to protect what you have. During his 37 years in power, Charles V had to fight no fewer than 13 wars to defend his empire or put down rebellions. This meant that Charles had only 5 years of peace in his entire reign. Those were the years from 1531 to 1535. He was fighting at least one war every other year of his rule.
paragraphs.zh: 这使得查理五世轻而易举地成为欧 洲最有权势的国王。 查理五世也是西班牙和德国在征服 美洲和新教改革期间的国王。所以 他是这一时期欧洲历史上的关键人 物。 但作为一个拥有广阔领土的统治 者，他面临着一个无法避免的问题， 那就是必须不断地战斗，以捍卫手 中的一切。查理五世在位 37 年间， 为保卫帝国或镇压叛乱，至少打了 13 场战争。 这意味着在查理整个统治生涯里， 他只在 1531-1535 年间度过了 5 年 的和平时光。在他统治期间，每两 年至少要打一场战争。

paragraphs.en: Charles could afford these wars because the Spanish stole huge amounts of gold from theAztec and Incas in the 1520 and 1530s. And then in 1545, they discovered the richest silver mine in history in SouthAmerica at Potosi Mountain. This single mountain produced 60% of the entire world's silver in the second half of the 1500s. The silver ore's were refined into pieces of silver called "ingots" and then the ingots were turned into coins called "pieces of eight" And the Spanish crown collected 20% of the silver in taxes. Of course, human beings had to do the hard, dangerous work of actually digging up the silver. And much of the most dangerous work was done
paragraphs.zh: 查理之所以能够负担得起这些战 争，是因为西班牙人在1520到1530 年间从阿兹特克和印加人手中偷走 了大量黄金。 1545 年，他们在波多西山发现了南 美历史上最丰富的银矿。 16 世纪下半叶，这座山出产了全世 界 60%的白银。 银矿被提炼成叫做“锭”的银块， 然后锭被制成称为“八块”的硬币。 西班牙国王征收 20%的银税。 当然，人们必须做艰苦、危险的工 作，才能真正挖出银矿。大部分最 危险的工作都是由奴役的印第安人 来干的。

paragraphs.en: by enslaved Indians. Untold thousands of people died while working in the Potosi mines, most of them NativeAmerican or African slaves. That silver was mined only at the expense of great human suffering. But that human suffering did make the Spanish crown rich with silver for over 100 years. Therefore, from 1520 onwards, giant treasure fleets would cross the ocean, carrying all the gold and silver ofAmerica to the Spanish kings. But of course the treasure fleets were also huge targets for pirate attacks. But we will talk more about that in the next class.
paragraphs.zh: 不计其数的人在波托西矿工作时死 亡，其中大多数是美洲土著人或非 洲的奴隶。 这座银矿的开采，建立在了无数人 所遭受的巨大痛苦之上。 但这些人遭受的苦难确实让西班牙 王室在往后的 100 多年的时间里拥 有着丰富的白银。 因此，从 1520 年起，大型的宝藏船 队穿越大洋，把美洲所有的金银运 往西班牙国王手中。 当然，宝藏船队也是海盗袭击的主 要目标。我们下节课再详细讲。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, back in Europe, Charles power alarmed the King of France. As you can see from this map, Charles' territory completely surrounded France, which made the King of France, Francis I, feel very nervous. As a result, Francis did everything he could to weaken Charles, and fought several major wars with him, usually over northern Italy. In fact, there were three major war between Charles and Francis during their times in power. There was a war from 1521 to 1526. Another war from 1526 - 1530. And a third war from 1536 to 1544. But aside from his wars with Spain, Francis I was an interesting man for
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，在欧洲，查尔斯的权力 惊动了法国国王。 从这张地图上可以看出，查理的领 土完全包围了法国，这让法国国王 弗朗西斯一世非常紧张。 因此，弗朗西斯倾尽全力来削弱查 理的权势，并与他打了几场大战， 主要集中在意大利北部。 事实上，在查理和弗朗西斯执政期 间，他们之间发生过三次大战。 1521年到1526年爆发了一场战争。 1526 - 1530 年爆发了另一场战争。 第三次战争发生在 1536-1544 年。 抛开与西班牙的战争不谈，弗朗西 斯一世还是一个有趣的人。1516

paragraphs.en: other reasons. In 1516 he convinced Leonardo da Vinci to move to France in his old age. Leonardo accepted the offer, and spent the last three years of his life living in this house from 1516 to 1519. Leonardo and Francis became close friends, and Francis was at Leonardo's death bed when the great artist died. And that's why Leonardo's most famous painting, the Mona Lisa, is in France today. You can go see it for yourself if you visit the Louvre museum in Paris. But be prepared for crowds, it is the most famous painting in the world! Francis also supported the expedition of Giovanni Verazanno,
paragraphs.zh: 年，他说服列奥纳多·达·芬奇晚 年移居法国。 列奥纳多接受了这个邀请，并在 1516 年到 1519 年间在这所房子里 度过了他生命中的最后三年。 列奥纳多和弗朗西斯成为了亲密的 朋友，当这位伟大的艺术家去世时， 弗朗西斯就在列奥纳多的病床边上 陪伴他。 这也是列奥纳多最著名的画作《蒙 娜丽莎》今天被保存在法国的原因。 如果你有机会去巴黎的卢浮宫，就 可以看见这幅画。但要做好人挤人 的准备，因为这是世界上最著名的 画作！ 弗朗西斯还资助了乔瓦尼·韦拉赞 诺的探险。1524 年，韦拉赞诺探索

paragraphs.en: who explored the East Coast of NorthAmerica in 1524, and became the first European to see New York harbor. And Francis supported the expeditions of Jacques Cartier who explored the St. Lawrence River in northern Canada during the 1530s. So now with that background, let's talk about the Protestant Reformation in France. When the Protestant reformation first began, Francis didn't quite know what to make of it. But from 1524 to 1526, hundreds of thousands of German Peasants became inspired by reading the bible and launched a rebellion in Germany called the German Peasants War.
paragraphs.zh: 了北美东海岸，成为第一个看见纽 约港的欧洲人。 弗朗西斯还资助了雅克·卡地亚在 1530 年代探索加拿大北部圣劳伦 斯河的探险。 讲完了这些背景知识，我们来谈谈 法国的新教改革。 新教改革刚开始时，弗朗西斯对此 还不太了解。 但是在 1524 年到 1526 年间，成千 上万的德国农民受到圣经的启发， 在德国发动了一场名为“德国农民 战争”的叛乱。

paragraphs.en: Eventually the Peasants were crushed, but it made Francis like the Protestant movement because it seemed to be causing trouble for Charles. In addition, Francis' older sister Marguerite de Navarre also had Protestant sympathies. So Francis at first tolerated the Protestant movement in France. But then on the 17th of October, 1534 several radical Protestants went too far. Because on that night, hundreds of posters appeared all across Paris. The posters condemned Catholic practices in very vicious terms. But even worse, one poster was even pasted to the King's bedroom door!
paragraphs.zh: 这场农民运动最终被镇压下来，但 弗朗西斯因此对新教运动有了好 感，因为这似乎能给查理制造不小 的麻烦。 此外，弗朗西斯的姐姐玛格丽 特·德·纳瓦雷也支持新教。 所以弗朗西斯一开始对法国境内的 新教运动是容许的。 但 1534 年 10 月 17 日，一些激进的 新教徒做得有点过火了。 那天晚上，成百上千的海报出现在 巴黎的大街小巷。海报以非常恶毒 的言辞谴责天主教的做法。 更糟糕的是，有一张海报甚至被贴 到了国王卧室门上!

paragraphs.en: Once he got over his shock, Francis became furious and declared that all Protestants must leave France within a month. Then Francis began a terrible persecution of French Protestants, including having them killed by his soldiers or burned alive in public executions. In 1540 Francis formalized this persecution with his Edict of Fontainebleau. In this Edict he declared that Protestantism was "High treason against God and Mankind" and that Protestants deserved "The loss of property, public humiliation and death." As these persecutions were beginning a Protestant Frenchman named Jehan Cauvin, or John
paragraphs.zh: 当弗朗西斯从震惊中恢复过来后， 他勃然大怒，宣布所有的新教徒必 须在一个月内离开法国。 随后，弗朗西斯开始对法国新教徒 进行可怕的迫害，包括让他的士兵 杀害，或在公开处决中活活烧死新 教徒。 1540 年，弗朗西斯颁布了《枫丹白 露敕令》，将这种迫害正式化。在 这条法令中，他宣布新教是“对上 帝和人类的背叛”，新教徒应该遭 受“财产损失、公开羞辱和死亡”。 迫害开始后，一个名叫杰汉·考文， 英文为约翰·加尔文的法国新教徒， 决定离开法国。

paragraphs.en: Calvin in English, decided that he needed to get out of France. John Calvin had been born in the town of Noyon in France on July 10th, 1509. His father Gerard Calvin had wanted his son to become a Catholic priest, but then changed his mind and decided John would make more money as a lawyer. So John was extremely well educated. He went to the University of Paris where he learned Latin as a young boy, then he studied. When he got older he went to the University of Orleans where he studied law.And then Calvin entered the University of Bourges where he learned Greek. Around 1530, when he was 21 years
paragraphs.zh: 约翰·加尔文于 1509 年 7 月 10 日 出生在法国的诺恩镇。 他的父亲杰拉德·加尔文希望他成 为一名天主教牧师，但后来又改变 了主意，决定让约翰做律师赚更多 的钱。 所以约翰受到了良好的教育。年轻 的他去了巴黎大学学习拉丁语。长 大后，他又前往奥尔良大学学习法 律。随后，加尔文又进入了博尔赫 斯大学学习希腊语。 1530 年左右，21 岁的加尔文获得了

paragraphs.en: old, Calvin had a strong religious experience and became a Protestant. But Calvin became a Reform Protestant like Ulrich Zwingli, not a Lutheran. As we learned last time, the big difference between Lutheran and Reform was that the Reform thought that the sacraments were just rituals that displayed a Christian's love and obedience to God to the outside world. But the Lutherans still kept the Catholic view that they were a way for God's power and grace to enter a believer's life. As we learned last time, there were 3 different Protestant churches by 1530: the Lutherans, the Reformed, and theAnabaptists.
paragraphs.zh: 深刻的宗教经验，成为了一名新教 徒。 但是加尔文成为了一名像乌尔里 希·茨温利那样的改革派新教徒， 而不是一名路德教徒。 上节课我们讲过，路德教和改革派 的最大区别在于，改革派认为，圣 礼只是一种仪式，用于对外界展示 基督徒对上帝的爱和服从。 但路德教会仍然坚持天主教的观 点，认为这能够让上帝的力量和恩 典进入信徒的生活中来。 上节课我们讲过，1530 年时有 3 个 不同的新教教派：路德教，改革派 和再洗礼派。

paragraphs.en: In that same year of 1530, Switzerland had become divided into Protestant areas and Catholic areas. This map shows you the Protestant areas in Orange and the Catholic areas in Green. And tension between the Protestants and Catholics increased until 1531 when Switzerland exploded into a Religious Civil War, the first of Europe's wars of religion. Historians call this conflict, the Kappel War. And Ulrich Zwingli himself was killed in this war on the 11th of October, 1531. After that came the affair of the Placards on October 17th, 1534. When Francis began his persecution of Protestants, Calvin decided to
paragraphs.zh: 1530 年，瑞士被分为新教地区和天 主教地区。这张地图里，橙色表示 新教地区，而绿色表示天主教地区。 新教徒和天主教徒之间的紧张关系 不断升级，瑞士终于在 1531 年爆发 宗教内战，这是欧洲第一次宗教战 争。 历史学家称之为卡佩尔战争。 1531 年 10 月 11 日，乌尔里希·茨 温利在这场战争中身亡。 之后则是1534年10月17日的布告 事件。 当弗朗西斯开始迫害新教徒时，加 尔文决定离开法国。1535 年 1 月，

paragraphs.en: leave France. In January 1535 he fled to the Swiss city of Basel. If you are curious, this is what Basel looks like today. In Basel, in March 1536, Calvin published the first edition of his most famous book The Institutes of the Christian Religion. Which very clearly and methodically explains Calvin's beliefs about religion. As had been the case with Luther and Tyndale, the Printing Presses spread Calvin's book all across Europe where it had a huge influence of Protestant thinkers, especially those in the Reform Churches. And so Calvin became the intellectual leader of the Reform Movement after Zwingli's death.
paragraphs.zh: 他逃往瑞士城市巴塞尔。 这是今天巴塞尔的样子。 1536 年 3 月，加尔文在巴塞尔出版 了他最著名的作品——初版的《基 督教要义》。这本书清晰、系统地 阐释了加尔文的宗教信仰。 和路德与廷代尔一样，出版社把加 尔文的书传遍了整个欧洲，这对新 教思想家，尤其是改革教会的思想 家产生了巨大的影响。 所以，加尔文在茨温利死后成为了 改革运动的思想领袖。

paragraphs.en: Calvin, then travelled to Ferrara Italy, then he returned to Paris to settle the affairs of his father who had died a few years before. In the first months of 1536, Calvin travelled to Ferrara Italy, then he returned to Paris to settle the affairs of his father who had died a few years before. At Paris he decided that he had no future in France and instead would move to the German city of Strasbourg. But the third war between Francis and Charles was beginning in 1536. So the border between France and Germany was very dangerous and swarming with troops. So to keep his family safe, Calvin decided to travel south and pass
paragraphs.zh: 加尔文后来去了意大利费拉拉市， 随后回到巴黎，处理他几年前去世 的父亲的后事。 1536 年的头几个月，加尔文去了意 大利的费拉拉，然后回到巴黎，处 理几年前去世的父亲的后事。 在巴黎，他觉得自己在法国没有前 途，于是决定搬去德国的斯特拉斯 堡。 但是弗朗西斯和查尔斯于 1536 年 爆发了第三次战争。 所以法国和德国的边境非常危险， 到处都是军队。 为了保护家人的安全，加尔文决定 途经瑞士日内瓦南下。

paragraphs.en: through the Swiss city of Geneva. So Calvin and his family travelled to Geneva and arrived there in late 1536. Here is a picture of what Geneva looks like today if you are curious. Calvin's plan was to stay in Geneva for only one night and continue on to Strausborg. But on the night of his arrival the Reform minister William Farel quickly heard that the author of the Institutes was in town. Farel had recently led the conversion of Geneva from Catholicism to Reform Protestantism and he was super excited to hear that John Calvin was in his city. William Farel had also been friends
paragraphs.zh: 于是加尔文和他的家人动身出发， 并于 1536 年底抵达日内瓦。如果你 好奇的话，可以看看这张日内瓦今 天的照片。 加尔文的计划是只在日内瓦停留一 晚，然后继续前往施特劳斯堡。 但在他到达的当晚，改革部长威 廉·法雷尔很快就打听到，《基督 教要义》的作者就在城里。 法雷尔最近领导了日内瓦从天主教 向新教的转换，知道约翰·加尔文 在他的城市之后，他非常兴奋。 威廉·法雷尔也是改革运动的创始

paragraphs.en: with Ulrich Zwingli, the founder of the Reform movement. At first Calvin refused to stay in Geneva, but Farel was not willing to let him go! Farel put a guilt-trip on Calvin. He told him that if he refused to stay in Geneva than he would be cursed by God for refusing his call. This worked and Calvin agreed to stay. From 1536 to 1564 Calvin and Farel turned Geneva into the leading city of the Reform movement. You could even call it the "Protestant Rome." In fact, Calvin had such an influence on the Reform movement, that today the Reform church is also called "Calvinism" and his followers are called "Calvinists."
paragraphs.zh: 人乌尔里希·茨温利的朋友。 起初加尔文拒绝留在日内瓦，但法 雷尔不愿意让他走！ 法雷尔想让加尔文感到内疚。他告 诉加尔文，如果他拒绝留在日内瓦， 就会被上帝诅咒，因为他拒绝了他 的召唤。 这招奏效了，加尔文同意留下来。 从 1536-1564 年，加尔文和法雷尔 把日内瓦变成了改革运动的领导城 市。你甚至可以称之为“新教的罗 马”。 事实上，加尔文对改革运动有如此 大的影响，以至于改革教会在现在 也被称为“加尔文教会”，他的追 随者也被称为“加尔文教徒”。我 们将在后面的课程中多次使用这个

paragraphs.en: We will be using this term again and again in future lessons. Just a side note now: When I discuss these different churches, especially on maps, Im going to use these symbols to represent them. These symbols are a little anachronistic, because they are the modern logos for these churches, but they'll work for our purposes. Now let's return to Calvin. While he lived in Geneva, Calvin preached at St. Pierre Cathedral, the main Church in Geneva. Here is what St. Pierre looks like today on the outside. And here is what it looks like on the inside. Notice the lack of decoration. I've used this picture before as an example what a Reform
paragraphs.zh: 术语。 这里有一个补充说明: 当我讲到这些不同的教派，特别是 在地图上时，我会用这些符号来代 表它们。 这些符号的时代可能有点对不上， 因为它们是这些教派的现代标志， 但是用来示意则问题不大。 现在让我们将目光转回加尔文。 加尔文居住在日内瓦期间，在日内 瓦的中心教堂圣皮埃尔大教堂传 教。 这是现在圣皮埃尔大教堂的外观。 这是它内部的情况。不难发现，它 没有华丽的装饰。我以前用过这张 照片来作为改革派教堂的范例。

paragraphs.en: Church looks like. Calvin also founded the university of Geneva and used it to train Calvinist ministers. And he then sent these teachers all across Europe to spread his ideas. This had a strong influence on France, the Netherlands and Scotland. This map shows you the Religious situation in Europe by 1560. As you can see, by 1560 the Netherlands, Scotland and Switzerland had become majority Calvinist countries, and there were many Calvinists in France, England, the Holy Roman Empire and Eastern Europe. John Calvin also had a strong
paragraphs.zh: 加尔文还建立了日内瓦大学，并用 来培训加尔文教派的牧师。 然后他派这些人去欧洲各地传播他 的思想。 这对法国、荷兰和苏格兰产生了深 远的影响。 这张地图显示了 1560 年前欧洲的 宗教状况。 如你所见，到 1560 年，荷兰，苏格 兰和瑞士已经成为加尔文教派主导 的国家，法国，英格兰，神圣罗马 帝国和东欧都有很多加尔文教派的 教徒。 尽管政府试图压制教会，但约翰·加

paragraphs.en: influence on his home country of France, despite the government's attempts to repress the church. By 1560, Calvinist Protestants were about 10% of the French Population. These French Calvinists are known as the Huguenots and on this slide I have a map showing you where they had churches and also a pie graph showing their percentage of the population. In fact, I have been using the Huguenot Cross as my symbol for Calvinism in general. These Huguenots will play a huge role in our next lesson. Meanwhile, more changes were taking place during Calvin's lifetime.
paragraphs.zh: 尔文对他的祖国——法国同样产生 了很大的影响。 到 1560 年，加尔文新教徒约占法国 人口的 10%。 这些法国的加尔文教徒也被称为胡 格诺派。这张地图展示了他们的教 堂的位置，还有一张饼状图展示了 他们在人口中所占的比例。 事实上，我一直用胡格诺十字架作 为加尔文主义的象征。这些胡格诺 派教徒是下节课的重要人物。 与此同时，加尔文的一生也发生了 更多的变化。

paragraphs.en: In 1510, a man named Albert of Prussia became the Grandmaster of the Teutonic Order of Knights. The Teutonic order were a Catholic military order that had been founded in 1190 during the crusades. These Teutonic Knights were the main force behind the Northern Crusades, which lasted from 1174 to 1290. During these crusades the Teutonic Knights conquered the Baltic, which at that time was the last pagan part of Europe. By 1300AD the Order had branches all across Europe and ruled the Baltic as you can see on this map. Their territory was known as the Teutonic State. But the Teutonic State was not led
paragraphs.zh: 1510 年，一个叫阿尔伯特的普鲁士 人成为日耳曼骑士团的总团长。 日耳曼骑士团是天主教的军事组 织，成立于 1190 年十字军东征期 间。 这 些 日 耳 曼 骑士 是 1174 年 至 1290 年间北方十字军东征的主力。 在这一过程中，日耳曼骑士征服了 欧洲最后一个异教徒地区——波罗 的海。 到了公元 1300 年，这个教团的分支 遍布欧洲，统治着波罗的海。他们 的领土被称为日耳曼国家。 但是这个日耳曼人的国家不是由国

paragraphs.en: by a King, it was led by a Grand-Master who took a religious vow never to marry or have children. In 1510,Albert of Prussia became the 37th Grand Master of the Teutonic State. By 1524,Albert was in his mid 30s, and increasingly wanted to get married and have a son who could inherit his territory. He realized that if he became a Lutheran, he would have a religious justification for doing this. So in 1525,Albert became a Lutheran; he abolished the Teutonic Order in his realm; and he announced the formation of the Duchy of Prussia. This became the first Protestant country in Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 王领导的，而是由一位发誓永远结 婚生子的骑士团团长领导。 1510 年，普鲁士的阿尔伯特成为日 耳曼王朝的第 37 任骑士团团长。 到了 1524 年，阿尔伯特已经 30 多 岁了，他越来越想结婚生子，来继 承他的领地。他意识到，如果他成 为路德教徒，就能有宗教理由这样 做了。 1525 年，阿尔伯特皈依路德教；他 废除了条顿骑士团的秩序，宣布成 立普鲁士公国。这是欧洲第一个新 教国家。

paragraphs.en: Albert then married. His first wife died after only giving him a daughter, but his second wife,Anna Marie gave him a son namedAlbert Frederick, who was born in 1553. Albert Frederick became the second Duke of Prussia and their family, the House of Hohenzollern would rule Prussia and then Germany until 1918. So remember the Prussians, they will play an increasingly important role in the history of Germany. Now lets look at the Protestant movement in other parts of Europe. Well another King was also sitting in that room. He was Christian III, the Crown Prince of Denmark and Norway. And he thought Luther was making
paragraphs.zh: 阿尔伯特随后结婚。他的第一任妻 子只给他生了一个女儿就去世了， 但他的第二任妻子安娜·玛丽于 1553 年给他生下了一个儿子，名叫 阿尔伯特·弗雷德里克。 阿尔伯特·弗雷德里克成为普鲁士 的第二位公爵，他们的家族——霍 亨索伦家族先后统治普鲁士和德国 直到 1918 年。记住这些普鲁士人， 他们将在德国历史上扮演越来越重 要的角色。 现在让我们看看欧洲其他地区的新 教运动。 当时还有一个国王也坐在那个会议 室里。 他就是丹麦和挪威的王储克里斯蒂 安三世。他非常赞同路德的观点。

paragraphs.en: good points. So, when Christian III became King of Denmark and Norway, he declared that his country would be Lutheran in 1534. At the same time the King of Sweden, Gustav I was having a fight with Pope. I won't get into the details here, but it had to do with internal Swedish politics. So in 1531, Gustav Vasa made Sweden into a Lutheran country. And remember from last time, Henry VIII also declared that England would be Protestant in 1533. By 1560, most of northern Europe had broken away from the Catholic Church.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，当克里斯蒂安三世于 1534 年成为丹麦和挪威的国王时，他宣 布他的国家将成为路德教派国家。 与此同时，瑞典国王古斯塔夫一世 与教皇发生了争执。我这里就不细 讲了，这与瑞典国内的政治有关。 1531 年，古斯塔夫·瓦萨将瑞典变 成路德教派国家。 还记得吗，就在不久之前，亨利八 世在 1533 年宣布英格兰为新教国 家。 到了 1560 年，北欧大部分地区脱离 天主教会。

paragraphs.en: Prussia and large parts of Germany were Lutheran, as was all of Scandanavia. England had broken away and formed the Church of England. The Netherlands and Scotland were now Calvinist. And Calvinist and Lutheran churches existed all across Europe. In just 40 years, the Roman Catholic church had gone from controlling all of Western Europe, to just its southern half. These religious divisions completely changed European politics. From 1517 until 1648, Protestant-Catholic conflict was the single most important issue in
paragraphs.zh: 普鲁士和德国的大部分地区，以及 斯堪的纳维亚半岛的大部分都是路 德教会。 英格兰脱离后，成立了英格兰国教 会。 荷兰和苏格兰现在是加尔文教会国 家。 加尔文教派和路德教会遍布欧洲。 在短短 40 年的时间里，罗马天主教 会从控制整个西欧，变成只控制着 欧洲南部。 这些宗教分歧彻底改变了欧洲政治 形势。 在 1517-1648 年这段时间里，新教 和天主教的冲突成为欧洲最主要的 问题。

paragraphs.en: Europe. The Reformation and its consequences would absolutely dominate European history for about 150 years. And we've already seen how these disagreements led to war in Switzerland in 1531. But then around 1560, the conflict intensified and Europe entered one of its most terrible historical periods. So please join me next time as we study the Wars of Religion. Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more, if you do I have a recommendation for you. And that is the Cartoon History of
paragraphs.zh: 宗教改革及其后果主导了欧洲 150 年的历史。 我们已经见识到这些分歧是如何导 致 1531 年瑞士战争的。 但 1560 年左右，冲突加剧，欧洲进 入最可怕的历史时期之一。 下节课我们将一起学习宗教战争。 好了，各位，我希望你们喜欢这堂 课，如果你们还想了解更多的话， 我有一个建议。 这就是拉里·戈尼克的《漫画现代

paragraphs.en: the Modern World by Larry Gonick. This is book 4 in his Cartoon history and it covers this time period in detail, and in a way that is fun and engaging to read. This is a picture of my own copy, and as you can see it is much worn out from being read over and over again. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 世界史》。这是他漫画世界史系列 的第四本书，对这段时期有详细的 阐述，读起来很有趣，也很吸引人。 这本漫画现代世界史是我自己的藏 书，不难看出它已经被我看过很多 遍，磨损得厉害了。 谢谢大家。

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History, today we will discuss the beginning of the Wars of Religion. This class will focus on the years between 1555 and 1572. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. I'd like to begin today's lesson by showing you a painting. It is called AHuguenot on St. Bartholomew's Day. Study this painting carefully, you will see it again at the end of the class. Last class, we discussed the wars between Charles V and Francis I in the early 1500s. In 1547, Francis I died and his
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列讲 座，今天我们将讨论宗教战争的开 端。 这节课主要讲的是 1555 - 1572 年 间的历史。这是时间轴。 先给大家看一幅画。这幅画名称是 《一位胡格诺教徒在圣巴塞洛缪 的一天》。 好好看看这幅画，你会在课程结束 时再次看到它。 上节课，我们讨论了 16 世纪早期， 查理五世和弗朗西斯一世之间的 战争。 1547 年，弗朗西斯一世去世，王位

paragraphs.en: crown passed to his son Henry II. Henry II married, Catherine de Medici, an Italian princess and they had 7 children by 1555. In that same year, Charles V retired from being king, the stress of the job had worn him down. His Empire had two parts the Spanish territory and theAustrian territory. Charles passed control of the Netherlands, Southern Italy and his Spanish territory to his son Phillip II but hisAustrian Territory and Holy Roman Imperial crown went to his brother Ferdinand. Charles V belonged to the Hapsburg family, after 1555 no one King ever controlled both Spain and Germany again, but his Hapsburg family
paragraphs.zh: 传给了儿子亨利二世。 亨利二世娶了意大利公主凯瑟 琳·德·梅迪西为妻，到了 1555 年，他们已经拥了有 7 个孩子。 同年，查理五世退位，因为工作的 压力使他疲惫不堪。 他的帝国分为两部分：西班牙和奥 地利。 查理五世将荷兰、意大利南部和西 班牙领土控制权交给了自己的儿 子菲利普二世，而奥地利领土和神 圣罗马帝国的王位则传给了他的 弟弟费迪南德。 查理来自哈普斯堡家族，1555 年之 后的 150 年中，没有哪个国王能够 同时控制西班牙和德国，只有哈普 斯堡家族一直控制着这两个地方。

paragraphs.en: remained in control of both parts of Europe for the next 150 years. We refer to the two sides of this royal family as the "Spanish Hapsburgs" and "Austrian Hapsburgs" Meanwhile, Charles arranged a marriage alliance between Mary I of England and his son Phillip II in 1554. But Mary was unable to have children and died of uterine cancer on November 17th, 1558, so the English crown passed to her younger sister Elizabeth. Elizabeth was crowned Queen on January 15th, 1559 and made England Protestant again. Charles V had also died in 1558 and Philip became the King of Spain,
paragraphs.zh: 我们将这两处的皇家分别称为“西 班牙哈布斯堡王朝”和“奥地利哈 布斯堡王朝”。 同时，在 1554 年，查尔斯安排英 格兰的玛丽一世和他的儿子菲利 普二世联姻。 但玛丽无法生育，并于 1558 年 11 月 17 日死于子宫癌，因此英国王 位传给了她的妹妹伊丽莎白。 1559 年 1 月 15 日，伊丽莎白加冕 成为英国女王，英格兰再次推行新 教。 1558 年，查理五世去世，菲利普成 为了西班牙、意大利南部和荷兰的

paragraphs.en: southern Italy and the Netherlands. Many European princes were interested in marrying Elizabeth, but she turned them all down.. Elizabeth never married, because then all her power would have gone to her husband. So Phillip gave up on the idea of marrying Elizabeth and proposed a marriage between himself and, the oldest daughter of the King of France. Henry II accepted this offer, and decades of war between Spain and France came to an end. OnApril 3rd 1559, Spain and France formalized this agreement with the Peace Treaty of Cateau Cambresis.
paragraphs.zh: 国王。 许多欧洲王子都想娶伊丽莎白，但 她都拒绝了。 伊丽莎白终生未嫁，因为如果她结 婚的话，她所有的权力都将归她丈 夫所有。 于是菲利普放弃了和伊丽莎白结 婚的念头，转而向法国国王的长女 求婚。 亨利二世接受了这个提议，就这 样，西班牙和法国之间长达数十年 的战争结束了。 1559 年 4 月 3 日，西班牙和法国正 式签署了《卡图坎布雷齐和约》。

paragraphs.en: And then Phillip II and Elizabeth of Valois were married 3 months later on June 22nd, 1559. By all accounts it was a happy marriage.And Phillip loved his French wife, although he was 18 years older than her. Henry II must have felt good about this marriage alliance. Just the year before, he had secured another great marriage alliance for his eldest son Francis II. Henry had married Francis to a Scottish princess named Mary Stuart. This marriage was also a wise strategic choice. Mary Stuart was the only daughter of James V, the King of Scotland, and a descendant of Henry VII of England. This meant that Mary was
paragraphs.zh: 1559 年 6 月 22 日，菲利普二世和 伊莉莎白完婚。 尽管菲利普比他的法国妻子大 18 岁，但他十分爱妻子，在众人看来， 这是一段幸福的婚姻。 亨利二世一定觉得这种联姻很好。 就在一年前，他还为长子弗朗西斯 二世争取到了另一个伟大的联姻。 亨利让弗朗西斯二世迎娶了苏格 兰公主玛丽·斯图亚特为妻。 这段婚姻也是一个明智的战略选 择。 玛丽·斯图亚特是苏格兰国王詹姆 斯五世的独生女，也是英格兰国王 亨利七世的后裔。这意味着玛丽是 苏格兰合法的女王，是继伊丽莎白

paragraphs.en: the rightful Queen of Scotland, and next in line to the English throne after Elizabeth. And on Mary's maternal side, she was a member of the House of Guise, one of the most powerful noble families in France. With four healthy sons, three healthy daughters and two excellent marriage alliances, Henry II must have felt like he was on top of the world. So on June 30th, 1559, Henry II called for a tournament in order to celebrate the peace with Spain and the marriage of his daughter. And that's when everything began to go wrong... Henry himself took part in the tournament, and during the jousting
paragraphs.zh: 之后英国王位的第二顺位继承人。 而玛丽的母系是法国最有权势的 贵族家族之一——吉斯家族。 拥有四个健康的儿子，三个健康的 女儿以及两个优秀的婚姻联盟，亨 利四世一定感觉自己身处世界之 巅。 因此，1559 年 6 月 30 日，亨利二 世发起了一场锦标赛，庆祝与西班 牙的和平以及女儿的婚礼。 就从那以后，一切开始变得糟 糕…… 亨利亲自参加了比赛，在与苏格兰 卫队队长加布里埃尔·德·蒙哥马

paragraphs.en: the lance of Gabriel de Montgomery, the Scottish captain of his guards, shattered against his shield. This sometimes happens in jousting as you can see in this picture. And they attacked the unarmed men, women and children in their church.After this they set the church on fire. By the time it was all over, Francis had killed 62 innocent people and wounded over 100 more. All across France, Huguenots were enraged by the massacre. So a Huguenot's rebellion began. It was the beginning of the French Wars of Religion. The Catholic forces were led by the
paragraphs.zh: 利的骑枪比赛中，他的盾牌被对方 打得粉碎。 正如你在这幅图中所看到的，这种 情况在格斗中时有发生。 弗朗西斯他们袭击了教堂里手无 寸铁的男女老少。之后他们放火烧 了教堂。 最终，他们共杀害了 62 名无辜者， 打伤了 100 多人。 整个法国的胡格诺派都对这场大 屠杀感到十分愤怒。 于是，胡格诺派发起了叛乱。这就 是法国宗教战争的开始。 天主教势力由吉斯家族领导，被称

paragraphs.en: Guise family and were called the Catholic Leauge. Several Protestant noblemen became leaders on the Protestant side, and the Royal family and more moderate Catholics were stuck in the middle and were known as the "Politiques". Sporadic fighting and killing continued for 8 years from 1562 to 1570 as Protestants and Catholics battled each other all across the country. Meanwhile back in Spain, Phillip had his own concerns. He was running out of money. Like his father Phillip II was constantly at war. Here is a list of
paragraphs.zh: 为天主教联盟。 一些新教徒贵族成为新教的领袖。 皇室与更温和的天主教徒被夹在 中间，称为“政治派”。 从 1562 年一直到 1570 年，新教徒 和天主教徒在全国各地不断地进 行零星的战斗和杀戮，共持续了 8 年。 同时，西班牙的菲利普也有所担 忧。 同时，西班牙的菲利普也有所担 他的钱不够花了。 就像他经常打仗的父亲菲利普二 世一样，这是他所有打过的战争的

paragraphs.en: all his wars. And wars eat money. Despite all the Gold and Silver coming from America, the Spanish crown got deeper and deeper into debt. So Phillip decided that the Dutch part of his empire needed to pay more taxes. This is a map of the Habsburg Netherlands, home of the Dutch people. It main city was Antwerp. At the time, the city ofAntwerp was rich. That's becauseAntwerp and the Netherlands were the center of European trade. At the time, Phillips half sister Margaret of Parma ruled the Netherlands, and was assisted by
paragraphs.zh: 列表。 战争消耗金钱。尽管有美洲不断输 送金银，西班牙国王还是变得负债 累累。 所以菲利普决定向荷兰部分征更 多的税。 这是荷兰人的家园——哈布斯堡 的地图。它的主要城市是安特卫 普。 当时，安特卫普是一个富裕的城 市。 因为安特卫普和荷兰是欧洲贸易 的中心。 当时，菲利普同父异母的妹妹玛格 丽特·帕尔马统治着荷兰，并得到 三位荷兰贵族的帮助：拉莫拉、菲

paragraphs.en: the three leading Dutch noblemen: Lamora, Philip and William the Silent. These three men formed her council of government. Over the last 30 years John Calvin had been sending Calvinist ministers to the Netherlands, and they had been converting people and establishing Calvinist churches. So by 1566, the northern Netherlands had become majority Calvinist, just like Scotland. The Netherlands also had a strong tradition of liberty and self rule. Power was divided among many different factions, and the Dutch ruled themselves with a Parliament called the Estates General. Phillip was determined to change all that.
paragraphs.zh: 利普和沉默者威廉。这三个人组成 了她的政府委员会。 在过去的 30 年里，约翰·加尔文 不断地派遣加尔文派神父到荷兰， 在那里收服信众，并建立了加尔文 派教会。 到了 1566 年，荷兰北部成为了加 尔文主义的势力范围，就像苏格兰 一样。 荷兰拥有坚实的自由和自治传统。 荷兰人将权力划分成许多不同的 派别，并通过一种叫做三级会议的 议会进行统治。 菲利普决心改变这一切。

paragraphs.en: He wanted to end self rule. Destroy the Protestant movement. and Increase taxes. So Philip began a persecution of Protestants in the Netherlands. This combined with a bad harvest in 1565 to make the Dutch Protestants deeply angry. So in 1566, Dutch Protestants began attacking Catholic churches and smashing their decorations. Dutch historians call this the Beeldenstorm or "Statue Storm" Here is a painting of Dutch Protestants tearing down a statue in a Catholic church. After a few months, the Beeldenstorm was stopped by Margaret and her council.
paragraphs.zh: 他想结束自治； 摧毁新教运动； 增加税收。 于是菲利普开始迫害荷兰的新教 徒。 再加上 1565 年的歉收，这一切激 怒了荷兰的新教徒。 1566 年，荷兰新教徒开始攻击天主 教教堂，砸毁教堂装饰。 荷兰历史学家称之为“圣像破坏运 动”或“雕像风暴”。 这是一幅描绘荷兰新教徒推倒天 主教教堂雕像的画。 几个月后，“圣像破坏运动”被玛 格丽特和她的委员会阻止了。

paragraphs.en: But the Beeldenstorm enraged Phillip.And he decided to send a Spanish army to the Netherlands to punish the Dutch and take direct control of the situation. This army was led by Don FernandoAlvarez, the Duke of Parma. This army was led by Don FernandoAlvarez, the Duke of Parma. Alvarez marched 10,000 to Brussels and arrived on the 22nd ofAugust, 1567. In September Alvarez invited Lamoral and Phillip to have dinner with him, and then arrested them in his house. But William of Orange smelled a trap and escaped. Margaret was horrified by this
paragraphs.zh: 但是这场运动激怒了菲利普。他决 定派一支西班牙军队去荷兰惩罚 这些人，并且控制局面。这支军队 由帕尔马公爵唐·费尔南多·阿尔 瓦雷斯领导。 1567 年 8 月 22 日，阿尔瓦雷斯率 领 1 万人前往布鲁塞尔。 阿尔瓦雷斯行军 1 万英里，于 1567 年 8 月 22 日抵达布鲁塞尔。 9 月，阿尔瓦雷斯邀请拉莫拉和菲 利普共进晚餐，然后在家中逮捕了 他们。 但是奥兰治的威廉嗅到了陷阱的 味道，于是逃跑了。 玛格丽特对同事们所受到的待遇

paragraphs.en: treatment of her colleagues and resigned in protest. Lamoral and Philip were both put on trial, found guilty under false pretenses, and executed on June 5th, 1568. ThenAlvarez set up special courts called the Council of Troubles. These courts began executing Protestant leaders by the hundreds. But instead of crushing Dutch resistance, these actions enraged the Dutch and a rebellion began. And then William returned to the Netherlands at the head of an army in 1568. The Dutch Revolt had begun. The Spanish were more powerful, but they kept running out of money,
paragraphs.zh: 感到震惊，辞职以示抗议。 拉莫拉和菲利普都被送上了法庭， 以莫须有的指控被判有罪，并于 1568 年 6 月 5 日处决。 后来，阿尔瓦雷斯设立了特别法 庭，名为“麻烦委员会”。 这些法庭处决了数百名新教领袖。 但是，这非但没有粉碎荷兰人的抵 抗，反而激怒了他们，于是，叛乱 开始了。 1568 年，威廉率领军队回到荷兰。 荷兰起义开始了。 西班牙人更强大，但他们总是缺 钱，荷兰人为保卫家园而战，所以

paragraphs.en: and the Dutch were fighting to defend their homes, so they fought harder.
paragraphs.zh: 他们更拼。

titleZh: 第九讲：穆斯林的黄金时代

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to learn about the Islamic Golden Age. Our class will focus on the years from 661 to 811AD. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. The year 661AD marks a major turning point in Islamic history. In that year Ali was assassinated and Muawiyah became the 5th Caliph. After Muawiya died, his son succeeded him. From now on the position of Caliph would be passed on through families. Muawiya's family were known as
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们世界历史系 列讲座。今天我们将要学习穆斯林 的黄金年代。 我们的课程会聚焦于公元 661 年到 811 年。这里是这段时间在时间线 上的样子。 公元 661 年是穆斯林历史上的一个 重要转折点。 那一年，阿里被暗杀。穆阿维耶成 为了第五任哈里发。 在穆阿维耶死后，他的儿子继承了 王位。 从这以后，哈里发便成为了世袭制 穆阿维耶也被叫做倭马亚，因为他

paragraphs.en: the Umayyads in honor of an ancestor named Umayyah. Here you can see all the Umayyad kings on this family tree. And here they are in the order in which they reigned. As you can see, the Umayyad Dynasty was relatively short, it lasted for only 89 years and most Umayyad caliphs ruled for only a few years. The two greatest Umayyad Caliphs wereAbd al-Malik ibn Marwan and his sonAl-Walid ibnAbd al-Malik. Together they expanded a unified caliphate to its largest extent ever, and their combined reigns added up to 30 years of power and stability. Let's look at their accomplishments
paragraphs.zh: 们有个祖先叫做 Umayyah。 这里你可以看到家族谱上所有的 倭马亚国王。 这里是按先后次序排列的。 你可以看到，倭马亚王朝相当短 暂，只存在了 89 年，大部分倭马 亚哈里发只统治了几年时间。 两个最伟大的倭马亚哈里发是 Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan 和他的儿子 他们一起把一个统一的哈里发国 扩张到有史以来最大的程度，他们 的联合统治加起来，带来了长达 30 年的权力和稳定。 让我们来详细了解一下他们的成

paragraphs.en: in more detail. Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan was caliph from 685 until 705.And during that time he had several accomplishments. First, he madeArabic the official language of the caliphate. Second, he reorganized the postal service. Third, he gave the Caliphate its own currency. The silver dirham which weighed 3 grams and the golden dinar which weighed 4.25 grams. Abd al-Malik also made a decision which still affects our world today. When Israel was an independent Kingdom, the Jews had built a temple to God under King Solomon. This first temple existed from 970 -
paragraphs.zh: 就。 685 年到 705 年间担任哈里发，在 此期间，他取得了几项成就。 首先，他使阿拉伯语成为哈里发国 的官方语言。 其次，他重组了邮政系统。 第三，他制定了哈里发自己的货 币。重 3 克的银迪拉姆和重 4.25 克的金第纳尔。 Abd al-Malik还做了一个影响我们 至今的决定。 当以色列是一个独立国家的时候， 犹太人在所罗门王的统治下为神 建造了一座圣殿。第一个神殿存在 于公元前 970 - 587 年，直到被巴

paragraphs.en: 587 BC until it was destroyed by the Babylonians. Later, in 516 BC, the Jews built a second temple that lasted untilAD 70. The second temple was destroyed by the Romans in 70AD because the Jews had rebelled against Roman rule. When the Roman's retook Jerusalem, they sold the entire population into slavery and destroyed the city. The only thing that remained of the second temple was a small segment of an exterior retaining wall on the western side. This became known as the "Western Wall."
paragraphs.zh: 比伦人摧毁。 后来，在公元前 516 年，犹太人建 造了第二座圣殿，一直持续到公元 70 年。 第二圣殿在公元 70 年被罗马人摧 毁，因为犹太人反抗罗马人的统 治。 当罗马人重新占领耶路撒冷时，他 们把所有人都卖为奴隶，摧毁了这 座城市。 第二座寺庙仅存的东西是西侧的 一小段外墙。 这就是著名的“西墙”。（在中国 我们叫它哭墙）

paragraphs.en: And it is a holy place for Jews because of its connection to the Second Temple. But in 691,Abd al-Malik built a mosque on the spot where the Jewish temple had been. This mosque is called the Dome of the Rock. But this created a conflict. Now BOTH the Muslims and the Jews were claiming the same exact spot as a Holy place! The Jews cannot rebuild their temple because a Muslim mosque sits at that exact location. Conflict over the whole area, known as the Temple Mount, continues to this very day! Abd al-Malik’s other great accomplishment was continuing the
paragraphs.zh: 它是犹太人的圣地，因为它与第二 圣殿相连。 但是在 691 年，Abd al-Malik 在犹 太圣殿的原址上建造了一座清真 寺。这座清真寺被称为圆顶清真 寺。 但这造成了冲突。现在，穆斯林和 犹太人都声称拥有同样的圣地! 犹太人不能重建他们的寺庙，因为 一个穆斯林清真寺就坐落在那里。 冲突蔓延了整个圣殿山，一直延续 到今天。 Abd al-Malik的另一项伟大成就是 继续将哈里发国扩张到北非。

paragraphs.en: expansion of the Caliphate into NorthAfrica. And capturing Carthage in 695AD. After this,Arabic became the main language of NorthAfrica. Abd al-Malik died in 705AD, and his sonAl-Walid I took power after him. Al-Walid built the great Umayyad Mosque in Damascus. UnderAl-Walid, Muslim forces invaded Spain in 711AD. They defeated the Goths and had mostly completed the conquest by 715AD. And Muslim armies pushed eastward too. By 712 they had reached the Indus river. These Muslim conquests were so
paragraphs.zh: 并在公元 695 年攻占迦太基。 此后，阿拉伯语成为北非的主要语 言。 公元 705 年，Abd al-Malik 去世， 他的儿子 Al-Walid 一世继位。 AI-Walid 在大马士革建造了大乌 马亚德清真寺。 公元 711 年，在 Al-Walid 的领导 下，穆斯林军队入侵西班牙。 他们打败了哥特人，并在公元 715 年完成了大部分的征服。 穆斯林军队也向东推进。到了 712 年，他们到达了印度河。 这些穆斯林的征服是如此广泛，我

paragraphs.en: extensive, that I think we need to take a moment to look at the big picture. In 624 Muhammad and the Muslims controlled only the city of Medina. In that year they began their war against Mecca. By 628 they controlled the cities north of Medina. And when Muhammad died in 632, they controlled half ofArabia. By 634,Abu Bakr had re-conquered theArabian peninsula and was ready to push north. By 640 the Muslims had conquered all of Mesopotamia and Syria. By 661, the Muslims had added Egypt, Cyprus, and Persia to their territory.
paragraphs.zh: 认为我们需要花一点时间来看看 穆斯林发展的始末。 624 年，穆罕默德和穆斯林只控制 了麦地那市。那一年他们开始了对 麦加的战争。 628 年，他们控制了麦地那北部的 城市。 穆罕默德死于 632 年，他们控制了 阿拉伯半岛的一半。 到 634 年，阿布·巴克尔重新征服 了阿拉伯半岛，并准备向北推进。 到 640 年，穆斯林征服了美索不达 米亚和叙利亚 到 661 年，穆斯林已经把埃及、塞 浦路斯和波斯添加到他们的领土 上。

paragraphs.en: By 710, they had conquered all of NorthAfrica and Central Asia. And Spain was theirs by 733! All of this had been accomplished in just 110 years. You can certainly understand why many Muslims felt that God was on their side. In just over a century they had conquered the largest empire in history. But these conquests had limits. The Nubians drove them off in 652 at the Second Battle of Dongola. In 732 an Umayyad army was defeated by the Franks at the Battle of Tours. This was their farthest advance into Western Europe. From 674-678 theArabs tried to capture Constantinople. But they
paragraphs.zh: 到 710 年，他们征服了整个北非和 中亚。 到 733 年，西班牙已经是倭马亚的。 这一切都是在 110 年里完成的。 这样你就可以理解为什么许多穆 斯林认为上帝站在他们一边。仅仅 一个多世纪，他们就征服了历史上 最大的帝国。 但这些征服是有限度的。 在 652 年的东古拉第二战役中，努 比亚人把他们赶走了。 732 年，一支倭马亚军队在图尔战 役中被法兰克人击败。这是他们进 入西欧最远的地方。 674-678 年间，阿拉伯人试图占领 君士坦丁堡。但他们失败了。

paragraphs.en: failed. They tried again in 717, and were defeated again. TheArab's learned the same lesson that almost all armies learned for 1000 years, the mighty Theodosian walls of Constantinople were just too strong. In addition, the Byzantines had developed a new secret weapon: Greek fire! This was a chemical substance that could be set on fire and used as a flamethrower. Even more terrible, the Greek fire burned on water and could therefore be used against theArab ships. For the next 736 years the walls of Constantinople functioned as the
paragraphs.zh: 717 年他们又试了一次，又失败了。 阿拉伯人学到了几乎所有军队 1000 年来所吸取的教训，君士坦丁 堡强大的狄奥多西城墙实在是太 强大了。 此外，拜占庭人还发明了一种新的 秘密武器:希腊火药! 这是一种化学物质，可以被点燃， 用作喷火器。 更可怕的是，希腊火药可以在水上 燃烧，因此可以用来对付阿拉伯的 船只。 在接下来的 736 年里，君士坦丁堡 的城墙一直充当着“欧洲的盾牌”，

paragraphs.en: "Shield of Europe", no invading army could break through them. It was only the development of gunpowder that finally allowed to Turks to knock them down in 1453. But that's a story for another day. The Umayyad CaliphAl-Walid died in 715.After his death the Umayyad's went into decline. One caliph, Hisham, would have a long reign of 19-years, but all the others ruled for only a few years at a time. In 747, a group ofArabs claiming descend from Muhammad's uncle Al-Abbas rebelled against Umayyad rule. The Rebellion started in Persia and moved west. TheAbbasid leader, As-Saffah defeated the Umayyad
paragraphs.zh: 入侵的军队无法突破。 直到 1453 年，火药的发展才最终 使土耳其人将其打垮。但这是另一 个故事。 倭马亚哈里发 Al-Walid 死于 715 年。他死后，倭马亚开始衰落。 一个哈里发 Hisham 将拥有长达 19 年的统治，但其他所有哈里发一次 只统治了几年。 在 747 年，一群阿拉伯人声称自己 是穆罕默德的叔叔阿巴斯的后裔， 反对倭马亚统治。 叛乱开始于波斯并向西移动。阿巴 斯的领袖，As-Saffah 在几场战役 中击败了倭马亚军队。

paragraphs.en: army in several battles. After the last battle in 750, the Battle of the Zab, Umayyad power was destroyed. The entire Umayyad family was massacred by theAbbasids, but one prince managed to escape the killing. His name was Abdul Rahman I. For the next 5 years he and a servant named Bedr made their way through Mesopotamia, Palestine, the Sinai and Egypt. Then he crossed all of northAfrica. Finally he made it to Spain where his family still had supporters. Once he reached SpainAbdul Rahman I launched a rebellion and broke away from theAbassids. Spain became an independent
paragraphs.zh: 在 750 年的最后一场战役，扎布战 役之后，倭马亚政权被摧毁。 整个家族都被阿巴斯家族屠杀，但 有一位王子设法逃脱了杀戮。他的 名字是阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼一世。 在接下来的五年里，他和一个名叫 Bedr 的仆人穿越了美索不达米亚、 巴勒斯坦、西奈和埃及。然后他穿 越了整个北非。 最后他来到了西班牙，在那里他的 家族仍然有支持者。 他一到西班牙，阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼 一世就发动叛乱，脱离阿巴西德家 族。西班牙成为一个独立的穆斯林 王国:科尔多巴酋长国。

paragraphs.en: Muslim kingdom: The Emirate of Cordoba. The entire Muslim world would never again be unified under a single Muslim ruler. But aside from Spain, theAbbasid's maintained control of the Umayyad caliphate's territory at first. TheAbbasid Dynasty was founded byAs-Saffah whose major accomplishment was to overthrow the Umayyads. He ruled for four years from 750-754. ButAs-Saffah died of smallpox in 754 and was succeeded by his brotherAl-Mansur. And it was Al-Mansur who really made theAbassids great. Al Mansur decided to move the capital away from Damascus closer
paragraphs.zh: 整个穆斯林世界再也不会在一个 穆斯林统治者的统治下统一。 但除了西班牙，阿巴斯家族最初一 直控制着倭马亚哈里发国的领土。 阿巴斯王朝是由阿萨法建立的，他 的主要成就是推翻了倭马亚王朝。 从 750 年到 754 年，他统治了四年。 但是阿萨法在 754 年死于天花，王 位由他的弟弟 Al-Mansur 继承。 正是 Al-Mansur 使得阿巴斯真正强 大起来。 Al-Mansur 决定把首都从大马士革 搬到离他在美索不达米亚的权力

paragraphs.en: to his power base in Mesopotamia. Instead of choosing an existing city for his capital,Al-Mansur decided to build a brand new city: Baghdad. So in 762 Baghdad was born and it soon became the biggest and greatest city of Mesopotamia. The old Persian capital of Ctesiphon was abandoned as people move from it to go live in the new capital. Many of its stones were even taken to build Baghdad. Al Mansur chose the place where the Euphrates and Tigris rivers came closest together as the location for his new capital. He said these words about it: "Indeed, this island between the Tigris in the East and the Euphrates in the West is the harbor of the
paragraphs.zh: 基地更近的地方。 Al-Mansur 没有选择现有的城市作 为首都，而是决定建立一个全新的 城市:巴格达。 所以在 762 年巴格达诞生了，它很 快就成为了美索不达米亚最大最 伟大的城市。 当人们从波斯首都泰西封搬到新 首都后，泰西封被废弃了。它的许 多石头甚至被用来建造巴格达。 Al-Mansur 选择了幼发拉底河和底 格里斯河最接近的地方作为他的 新首都。他是这样说的: 的确，这个位于东部底格里斯河和 西部幼发拉底河之间的岛屿是世 界的港口。所有在底格里斯河上航

paragraphs.en: world.All the ships that sail up the Tigris...and the adjacent areas will anchor here... it will indeed be the most flourishing city in the world." Meanwhile, theAbassids were continuing to push their territory and religion further east. In July of 751 an Abassid army came up against a Tang Chinese army at the Battle of the Talas river. You see, in 751 the Tang Dynasty was at the height of its power, its territory stretched all the way into the central Asia Tarim Basin. The battle was going well for the Chinese at first. But halfway through the battle, the Karluk mercenaries, switched sides and helped theArabs.
paragraphs.zh: 行的船只……以及在相邻的区域 行驶的船只会在这里抛锚……它 将成为世界上最繁华的城市。 与此同时。阿巴斯家族继续把他们 的领土和宗教推向更东边。 751 年 7 月，一支阿巴斯军队在塔 拉斯河战役中与一支唐朝军队交 战。 你看，在 751 年唐朝正处于权力的 巅峰，它的领土一直延伸到中亚的 塔里木盆地。 这场战役一开始对中国人来说很 顺利。 但在战斗进行到一半时，卡鲁克雇 佣军改变了立场，帮助了阿拉伯 人。

paragraphs.en: The Tang soldiers were massacred, and the battle turned into a rout. The Battle of Talas is a turning point in Chinese history. Before the battle, the Tang Empire controlled the area of Transoxiana. After the battle they lost control of that region. Perhaps the Tang could have re-taken the area, but just four years later An-Lushan launched a rebellion in 755. This war was devastating and lasted for 8 years until 763. The Tang dynasty survived for another 144 years, but it was never as powerful again. Another consequence of the Battle of Talas, was that theAbbasids got paper-making technology from the
paragraphs.zh: 唐朝的士兵被屠杀，战争变成了溃 败。 塔拉斯战役是中国历史上的一个 转折点。在战争爆发前，唐朝控制 了河中地区。 战斗结束后，他们失去了对那个地 区的控制。 也许唐朝本可以重新占领这一地 区，但仅仅四年之后，安禄山就在 755 年发动了叛乱。这场战争是毁 灭性的，持续了 8 年，直到 763 年。 唐朝又延续了 144 年，但它再也没 有那么强大过。 塔拉斯战役的另一个结果是，阿巴 斯家族从中国人那里得到了造纸 技术。

paragraphs.en: Chinese. In 794, a paper making factory was opened in Baghdad and by 850 there were over 100 paper-making workshops. This new supply of paper also helped fuel a great period of learning. Al-Mansur founded a school and library called "The House of Wisdom". He did this in imitation of the great PersianAcademy of Gondishapur. AndAl-Mansur began an effort to translate all the classics of Greek, Indian and Persian literature into Arabic. This effort, called the translation movement, preserved many works
paragraphs.zh: 794 年，巴格达开办了一家造纸厂， 到 850 年，已有 100 多家造纸厂。 在纸张方面的供应也促进了学习 的发展。 Al-Mansur 建立了一所学校和图书 馆，名为“智慧之屋” 他这样做是在模仿波斯的贡迪沙 普尔学院。 Al-Mansur 开始努力将所有希腊、 印度和波斯文学的经典著作翻译 成阿拉伯语。 这种努力被称为“翻译运动”，它 将许多古典的作品保留到了现代。

paragraphs.en: of classical antiquity into the modern era. Probably the single most valuable advance that theArabs gained from this effort was the Indian number system. TheArabs learned about the Hindu Numeral System and developed the Arabic Numerals that the whole world uses today. This number system was vital to the development of higher mathematics. For example, there was the great mathematicianAl-Khwarism who discovered algebra. There was the scholar Jabir ibn Hayyan who developed many tools of chemistry. There was Al-Kindi who admired
paragraphs.zh: 阿拉伯人从这一努力中获得的最 有价值的进步可能是印度的数字 系统。 阿拉伯人了解了印度数字系统，并 发展出了今天全世界使用的阿拉 伯数字。 这个数字系统对高等数学的发展 至关重要。 例如，伟大的数学家 Al-Khwarism 发明了代数。 有一位学者 Jabir ibn Hayyan 多 化学工具。 Al-Kindi 崇拜亚里斯多德，他被认

paragraphs.en: Aristotle and is considered the father ofArab Philosophy. And much of this learning was made possible by tireless translators like Hunayn Ibn Ishaq who translated 116 books in his lifetime. By 800AD, the Muslim world was experiencing a golden age, and the Abbasid caliphate could claim to be the center of world civilization. But now we will leave this flourishing society behind and turn our attention west to Spain. Please join me next time for that story.
paragraphs.zh: 为是阿拉伯哲学之父。 这些知识大部分都是由像 Hunayn Ib Ishaq这样孜孜不倦的译者完成 的，他一生翻译了 116 本书。 公元 800 年，穆斯林世界经历了一 个黄金时代，阿巴斯哈里发国可以 说是世界文明的中心。 但现在我们将把这个繁荣的社会 抛在身后，把注意力转向西方的西 班牙。 欲知后事如何，且听下回分解

titleZh: 第十讲：阿里乌派，哥特人和伊比利亚|信仰真主的西班牙

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture on World History, today we are going to look at what was happening in the Iberian peninsula during the Early MiddleAges. This lesson will focus on the 4 centuries between 313 and 732AD. Our story begins in 313AD, when the Roman Empire was still the dominant power in Europe. On that year (according to legend) Constantine the Great saw the Chi Rho sign in the sun. And then he went on to win the Battle of the Milvan bridge. Whatever the real reason, Constantine became the first Christian Roman Emperor.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列，今天我们将要看看在中世纪 早期的伊比利亚半岛发生了些什 么。 这一课将会关注从公元 313 年到 732 年之间的四个世纪。 我们的故事开始于公元 313 年，那 时罗马帝国仍主宰着欧洲。 根据传说，在那一年君士坦丁大帝 在太阳中看见了 Chi 和 Rho(代表 耶稣)符号。 然后他在米尔维安大桥战役中获 得了胜利。 无论真实原因为何，君士坦丁成了 第一位信仰基督的罗马皇帝。

paragraphs.en: But by the time Constantine converted, Christianity was already about 300 years old, and there were disagreements within the church about what Christian believed exactly. So Constantine called a meeting to sort it all out. He chose the town of Nicea inAsia Minor, a short distance from Constantinople. The meeting lasted about a month from May 20th to June 19th, 325 AD.And eventually the bishops agreed on a statement of faith. This statement of faith is known as the Nicean Creed, and even today it stands as a summary of what orthodox Christians believe. Here is the English version.
paragraphs.zh: 但到君士坦丁皈依之时，基督教已 有大约三百年历史，教会内部关于 基督徒究竟在信仰什么的问题异 议丛生，因此，君士坦丁召集了一 次会议，以厘清这些问题。 他选择了小亚细亚的尼西亚城，距 离君士坦丁堡不远。 会议持续了大约一个月，从公元 325 年的五月二十日到六月十九 日。最终，主教们达成了一份信仰 声明。 这份信仰声明被称为尼西亚信经， 直到今天它依然代表了对于正统 基督徒信仰的全面总结。 这里是其英文版。

paragraphs.en: And here is the Chinese version. But not everyone agreed with the Nicean Creed. An influential priest fromAlexandria named Arius disagreed about the nature of Jesus. Christians believe that there is one God in three persons: The Father, The Son, and the Holy Ghost. This is called the Trinity. The Nicene Creed stated that Jesus (God the Son) was eternal like God the Father, butArius thought that God the Father must have existed before Jesus at some point, if Jesus was "begotten" of the father. These sorts of arguments about unprovable mysteries might seem silly, but in the Byzantine Empire, people argued about the trinity and the nature of Jesus all the time, and
paragraphs.zh: 这是它的中文版。 但并非每个人都认同尼西亚信经。 一位来自亚历山大港的很有影响 力的牧师叫做阿里乌斯，他不同意 其中对于基督本质的说法。 基督徒相信在三个人当中存在着 同一的神：圣父、圣子和圣灵。这 就是三位一体。 尼西亚信经声称，耶稣（神子）是 永恒的，一如圣父上帝，但阿里乌 斯认为圣父上帝必须在耶稣之前 就已存在，如果说耶稣是由其父 “产生”的话。 这些关于无法证明的谜团的不同 观点可能看起来有点傻，但是在拜 占庭帝国，人们因为三位一体和基 督本质的问题终日争吵，这些分歧 甚至削弱了帝国。

paragraphs.en: these divisions weakened the empire. Some Christians agreed withArius, and this caused a split in the Christianity between the Nicene Catholics Christians and theArian Christians. This disagreement might have been an unimportant detail in history, except that Arius had a big influence on two important people, a Bishop named Eusebius (who was a friend ofArius) and a priest named Ulfilas who was Eusebius's student. In the 340s, Eusebius sent Ulfilias into Gothic territory to convert them to Christianity. To fulfil his mission, Ulfilas created the GothicAlphabet in order to translate the Bible. Since the goths
paragraphs.zh: 有些基督徒认同阿里乌斯的观点， 这导致了教内尼西亚正统派和阿 里乌派的分裂。 这一分歧在历史上可能会是一个 无关紧要的细节，但事实上，阿里 乌斯对两个重要人物产生了强大 影响：一位名叫尤西比乌的主教， 他是阿里乌斯的朋友，还有一位名 叫乌尔菲拉的牧师，他是尤西比乌 的学生。 在公元四世纪四十年代，尤西比乌 将乌尔菲拉派往哥特人的领地，去 向他们传教。 为了完成使命，乌尔菲拉创设了哥 特字母表以翻译圣经。因为当时的 哥特人是没有书写系统的一伙文

paragraphs.en: were an illiterate people without a writing system. Modern German, (and English) evolved from Old Gothic, so you could say that Ulfias's Gothic bible is the very beginning of German and English literature. Ulfilas had some success as a Christian missionary and converted many Gothic chiefs. But he converted them toArian Christianity, not traditional Nicene Catholicism. This meant that when the Goths invaded the Roman Empire in 376, many of their chiefs like Frittigern andAlaric were already Christians. Then came the crossing of the Danube on the last day of 406, and the sack of Rome in 410 and 455.
paragraphs.zh: 盲。 现代德语（以及英语）是从古哥特 语演化而来，所以你可以说乌尔菲 拉的哥特语圣经就是德语和英语 文学的起源。 作为基督教传教士，乌尔菲拉取得 了相当的成功，他使得一些哥特官 员皈依了基督教，但他们所接受的 是阿里乌派基督教，而非传统的尼 西亚正教。 这意味着，当哥特人在公元 376 年 入侵罗马帝国的时候，其中的许多 将领，例如弗里蒂杰恩和亚拉里 克，已经是基督徒了。 他们在公元 406 年的最后一天渡过 了多瑙河，并在 410 年和 455 年两 次劫掠了罗马。

paragraphs.en: As we've already seen, the Western Empire collapsed under this invasion of different Germanic tribes. The three most important tribes from this period were the Goths, Franks and Vandals. The Vandals eventually settled in NorthAfrica under the leadership of their king Genseric. And their Kingdom survived for 99 years, until they were conquered by the Byzantine Romans under Emperor Justinian. NorthAfrica became Roman territory again, and remained that way for over 150 years, until it was conquered by theArabs in 698. The other two major Germanic tribes were the Goths who settled in
paragraphs.zh: 正如我们已经看见的，西罗马帝国 在多个日耳曼部落的侵袭之下崩 溃了。 这一时期最重要的三个部落是哥 特人、法兰克人和汪达尔人。 汪达尔人最终在他们的国王盖萨 里克的领导下定居北非。 他们的王国存在了 99 年，最终被 查士丁尼大帝领导的拜占庭罗马 军队所征服。 北非再次成为罗马领土，并持续了 超过一百五十年，直到公元 698 年 它被阿拉伯人攻占为止。 另外两个主要的日耳曼部落当中， 哥特人定居在了西班牙，而法兰克

paragraphs.en: Spain, and the Franks, who settled in Northern Gaul. As you might have guessed, the Franks built up their Kingdom which became the ancient origin for the modern country of France. We will learn more about them next class. For now, lets focus on the Goths. Here is what the Mediterranean world looked like in 476 when the Western Roman Empire finally fell. The first King of the Goths was Alaric, who led them to the first sack of Rome in 410AD. ButAlaric died later that year from malaria, and his brother in law Ataulf became the second Gothic King.
paragraphs.zh: 人在高卢北部安了家。 你可能已经猜到了，法兰克人所建 立的王国就是现代法国的始祖。 我们将在下一次课中了解到关于 这些的更多信息。 现在我们还是聚焦于哥特人。这是 476 年的地中海世界，西罗马帝国 就在这个时候最终陷落。 哥特人的第一位国王是亚拉里克， 他领导他们进行了公元 410 年对罗 马的第一次劫掠。 但亚拉里克在同一年晚些时候死 于疟疾，他的妹夫阿陶尔夫成为了 第二任哥特国王。

paragraphs.en: Ataulf led them to Southern Gaul where they settled down. Alaric andAtaulf were the beginning of a line of 34 kings who ruled the Goths from 395 to 714. After them the next really important King was the 5th King, Theodoric the First. It was Theodoric I who made the alliance with the Roman general FlaviusAetius and defeated Atilla the Hun at the Battle of the Catalaunian Plains. Unfortunately, Theodoric I died in battle, defending his people against the Huns. After Theodoric the next really important King wasAlaric II, who was the 9th King.
paragraphs.zh: 阿陶尔夫带领他们到了高卢南部， 然后定居在那里。 亚拉里克和阿陶尔夫是哥特多达 34 位的国王谱系的开端，这 34 位 国王从公元 395 年到公元 714 年一 直统治着哥特人。 在他们之后，下一位真正重要的国 王是第五位国王，狄奥多里克一 世。 正是狄奥多里克一世与罗马将军 艾提乌斯结成了同盟，并在卡塔隆 平原战役中击败了匈人统治者阿 提拉。 不幸的是，狄奥多里克一世在这场 战役中阵亡，面对匈人保卫了他的 族民。 在狄奥多里克死后，下一位真正重 要的国王是亚拉里克二世，他是第 九位皇帝。

paragraphs.en: Alaric was important for his failure. He was defeated by the Franks and killed in the fighting at the Battle of Vouille in the summer of 507. As a result of this battle, the Franks pushed the Goths out of Gaul entirely.After 511 their Kingdom would be limited to the Iberian peninsula. The next really important King was Recarred I, who was 17th King of the Goths. During Recarred's life, the Byzantines had recaptured southern Spain for the Roman Empire. To help him re-conquer this territory, Recarred switched his religion fromArian Christianity to Nicene Catholicism.
paragraphs.zh: 使得亚拉里克格外重要的是他的 败绩。他被法兰克人所击败，并在 507 年夏天的武伊勒战役中被杀。 这次战役导致法兰克人完全将哥 特人从高卢驱逐了出去。在 511 年 以后，他们的王国疆域已经被限制 在了伊比利亚半岛之内。 下一位真正重要的国王是哥特人 的第十七位国王，雷卡里德一世。 在雷卡里德生活的年代，拜占庭人 已经重新攻占了西班牙南部，使之 重回罗马帝国统治。 为了重新夺回这块领土，雷卡里德 把自己的宗教信仰从阿里乌派基 督教改成了尼西亚天主派。

paragraphs.en: After his conversion in 587, the Nicene Catholics in southern Spain were willing to support him, and Recarred was able to drive the Byzantines out of Iberia. Under Recarred, the Goths became Catholic, which is an important event in the history of the Catholic Church. The Franks had converted a few decades earlier, so Recarred's conversion made Nicene Catholicism the strongest religion in Europe. After Recarred, the next really important Gothic Kings were Wittiza and Roderic, the 32nd and 33rd Kings. They are important because Roderic overthrew Wittiza in 710AD.
paragraphs.zh: 在他于 587 年皈依以后，西班牙南 部的尼西亚天主教开始支持他，而 雷卡里德由此得以将拜占庭人赶 出了伊比利亚半岛。 在雷卡里德的统治下，哥特人成为 了天主教徒，这是天主教会历史上 的一个重要事件。 法兰克人已经在几十年前皈依了 天主教，所以，雷卡里德的皈依使 得尼西亚天主教成了欧洲最强大 的宗教。 在雷卡里德之后，接下来真正重要 的哥特国王是威特萨和罗德里克， 分别是第 32 位和第 33 位国王。 他们之所以重要，是因为罗德里克 在公元 710 年推翻了威特萨。

paragraphs.en: This badly divided the Goths and started a civil war. This was terrible timing, because the Umayyad Caliphate was completing its conquest of North Africa in 710Ad. The Umayyads saw an opportunity and offered to help Wittiza's enemies, and with their support, the Arabs invaded Spain in 710. The Muslim army was led by a general named Tariq ib Ziyad.After he crossed into Iberia, Roderic rushed south to meet him in Battle. This battle, known to history as the Battle of Guadalete was a major turning point in Spanish history. Half of Roderic's army was still mad at him for overthrowing Wittiza and they abandoned him in the middle
paragraphs.zh: 这严重分裂了哥特人，并导致了内 战的爆发。 战争发生在一个糟糕的时间，因为 倭马亚王朝在公元 710 年正将完成 它对于北非的征服战争。 倭马亚人在其间看到了机会，并向 威特萨的敌人提供了帮助，在他们 的帮助下，阿拉伯人在 710 年入侵 了西班牙。 穆斯林军队由一位名叫塔里克·伊 本·齐亚德的将军率领，在他进入 伊比利亚半岛以后，罗德里克赶往 南方与他交战。 这场战役史称瓜达雷特战役，是西 班牙历史上的一个重要转折点。 有一半的罗德里克的军队仍对他 推翻威特萨一事感到恼怒，他们在 战役中背弃了他。

paragraphs.en: of the Battle. Roderic was then overwhelmed by theArabs and was killed along with him men. However, it was a mistake for Roderic's Gothic enemies to betray him, because theArabs turned around and crushed them afterwards. Within a short time, theArabs had conquered the Gothic capital of Toledo and then began their conquest of Iberia. Divided and weakened by Civil War, the Goths were overwhelmed and crushed. The Gothic Kingdom came to an end, and Spain now became another province of the Umayyad caliphate. By 732, the conquest of Spain was
paragraphs.zh: 罗德里克随即被阿拉伯人推翻，并 与他的亲信一同被杀。 然而，罗德里克在哥特的国内敌人 选择背叛他是一个严重错误，因为 阿拉伯人很快转过身来，也摧毁了 他们。 在很短的时间里，阿拉伯人攻占了 哥特人首都托雷多，开始了他们对 于伊比利亚的征服行动。 因内战而四分五裂且虚弱不堪的 哥特人一败涂地。哥特王国因此灭 亡，而西班牙现在成了倭马亚王朝 的一个省。 到 732 年，西班牙已经大体被征服，

paragraphs.en: largely complete, except for resistance in the northern mountains ofAsturias. At this point, theArabs began thinking about pushing the Caliphate even deeper into Europe. In 732 the generalAbdul Rahman al Ghafi led an army across the Pyrenees mountains. His goal was to conquer the Franks. The Battle fought that year would become one of history's turning points. But that is a story for another day... Okay everyone I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do, I have a recommendation for you. My recommendation is the Youtube
paragraphs.zh: 只除了北部的阿斯图里亚斯山区 还有一些抵抗力量。 此时，阿拉伯人开始考虑将哈里发 国疆域进一步推向欧洲腹地。 732 年，阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼领导一 支军队翻越了比利牛斯山，他的目 标是征服法兰克人。 这一年爆发的战役将成为一个历 史转折点。 但这是另一个故事了。 好了，我希望你们喜欢这次课程并 且希望了解更多，如果你们想的 话，我会给你们一些推荐。 我的推荐是 Dr. Ryan Reeves 的

paragraphs.en: channel by Dr. Ryan Reeves, an Associate Professor of Historical Theology at Gordon-Conwell Theological Seminary. He offers clear explanations of Christian History, and helps his audience better understand things like theArian controversy or early Christian Europe. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: Youtube 频道，他是戈登康威尔神 学院的历史神学助教。 他给出了对于基督教历史的清晰 解读，并且帮助他的观众能够更好 地了解很多事，例如阿里乌派争端 以及早期的基督教欧洲。 谢谢你们。

paragraphs.en: Welcome back to our lecture series on World History, today we will learn about the Carolingian dynasty. This lecture will focus on the years between 411 and 870AD. As we've already learned when the Western Roman Empire fell, there were three major Germanic tribes in the west: The Goths, The Franks and the Vandals. So far we've talked about the Goths and Vandals, but haven't said much about the Franks. In the 300s, when the Roman Empire was still strong and the Franks were Roman allies who lived on the other side of the Rhine river. Their main enemies were the
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎回到我们的世界历史系列讲 座，今天我们将了解到的是加洛林 王朝。 这一讲将聚焦于公元 411 年到 870 年。 正如我们已经在西罗马帝国陷落 时了解过的，当时在西部存在三个 主要的日耳曼部落：哥特人，法兰 克人和汪达尔人。 到目前为止，我们已经谈及过哥特 人和汪达尔人，但还没有过多涉及 法兰克人。 在公元四世纪，罗马帝国尚且强 盛，法兰克人还是生活在莱茵河对 岸的罗马的盟友。 他们的主要敌人是撒克逊人，他们

paragraphs.en: Saxons and their capital city was Cologne. Cologne was actually a Roman settlement on the Frankish side of the Rhine river. The Romans gave the city to the Franks as part of the deal that made them allies. But then the Western Roman Empire collapsed between 395 to 476.At first the Franks were loyal to the Romans, but they eventually joined the migration into former Roman territory. To help you see what a big change happened in the 400s, here is Roman Gaul in 400AD, and here it is in 500AD. During this period, the Franks were led by a great King named Merovech. He is a partly historical
paragraphs.zh: 的首都是科隆。 科隆实际上是罗马在莱茵河法兰 克人占据的一岸的定居点。作为缔 结联盟的协议的一部分，罗马人将 这个城市送给了法兰克人。 但在 395 年到 476 年，西罗马帝国 崩溃了，起先法兰克人忠于罗马 人，但最终他们加入了向罗马国土 移民的行列。 为了帮助你们明白在公元五世纪 发生的是多么大的变化，这里是公 元 400 年时的罗马治下的高卢，而 这里是公元 500 年它的样子。 在这段时间里，法兰克人被一位名 叫墨洛维的伟大国王所统治，他是 一位半历史、半传说的人物。

paragraphs.en: and partly legendary figure. Merovech became the ancestor to a line of Frankish Kings called the Merovingians. He had a son named Childreic and a grandson named Clovis. Clovis led the Franks to victory against the Goths at the Battle of Vouille and drove them out of Gaul. Here in Green you can see a map of the Kingdom that Clovis built. Clovis was the leader who really built the Kingdom of the Franks. Clovis was also mainly a war leader, to maintain power he had to fight wars every year, win battles, and provide his followers with plunder. But Clovis is also important because he had a Nicene Catholic wife named Clotilde.And Clotilde tried
paragraphs.zh: 墨洛维是一系法兰克国王的祖先， 他们被称作墨洛温王朝。他有一个 儿子名叫希尔德里克，还有一个孙 子名叫克洛维。 克洛维在武伊勒战役中领导法兰 克人击败了哥特人，将后者赶出了 高卢。 在这里的绿色部分你可以看到克 洛维所建立的王国的疆域。克洛维 是法兰克王国的真正创立者。 克洛维大部分时间也是一位战争 领导人，他每年都在进行战争，赢 得战役，并向他的追随者提供战利 品。 不过，克洛维的重要性还在于他有 一位笃信尼西亚天主教的妻子，名 叫克洛蒂尔德。克洛蒂尔德试图让

paragraphs.en: to get her husband to convert to Christianity. Eventually Clotilde succeeded and Clovis accepted baptism on Christmas Day 496AD. Here is that scene depicted in an ivory carving. And here is that same scene in a painting. The baptism of Clovis is a major event in Christian history, because it meant that the Franks would be Christians from now on. But the Franks also had a tradition that hurt their power again and again. They practiced "progeniture." This meant that when a man died, his land and possessions were divided equally among his sons. And when Clovis died, he had 4 sons. One named Theuderic from his first marriage .And three other
paragraphs.zh: 她的丈夫归信基督教。 最终克洛蒂尔德成功了，克洛维在 公元 496 年的圣诞日接受了洗礼， 这是一幅象牙版画，描绘了当时的 情景。 这是描绘同一场景的一副画作，克 洛维的受洗是基督教历史上一个 重要事件，因为它意味着法兰克人 从此将成为基督徒。 但法兰克人也有一项一再削弱了 他们自己力量的传统，他们执行诸 子均分制。 这意味着，当一个人死了，他的土 地和财产将被均等地分给他的三 个儿子。 当克洛维去世的时候，他共有四个 儿子。其中一个名叫提乌德里克， 是他的第一次婚姻所生，另外三个

paragraphs.en: sons from his marriage to Clotilde. So when Clovis died in 511, his Kingdom was divided into 4 pieces, which fragmented Frankish power. Fortunately for the Franks, three sons died without heir, and Chlothar was able to reunify the Kingdom in 558. But the problem repeated itself, Chlothar also had 4 sons. So the empire was once again divided into 4 pieces and each son (Chiperic, Charibert, Sigebert, and Guntram) got their own piece. As you can see, this practice of Progeniture frequently divided the Frankish Kingdoms. So about 1000AD, the practice began to change, and the Western
paragraphs.zh: 儿子则是他与克洛蒂尔德所生。 所以，当公元 511 年克洛维去世的 时候，他的王国被分成了四块，这 削弱了法兰克人的力量。 不过法兰克人还算走运，其中的三 个儿子死时都没有留下继承人，克 洛泰尔由此得以在558年重新统一 了王国。 但问题重演了，克洛泰尔也有四个 儿子。 所以帝国再次被分成了四个部分， 每一个儿子（希尔佩里克，查理贝 尔特，西吉贝尔特和贡特朗）分别 得到了他们各自的部分。 正如你所见，诸子均分制频繁地分 裂着法兰克诸王国。 所以，在公元 1000 年左右，事情 开始起了变化，西欧人开始实行长

paragraphs.en: Europeans practiced Primogeniture instead. In this system, the oldest surviving son got all the land, and his younger brothers got nothing. For Kings, Primogenture meant that the crown was always passed down along the line of oldest sons, while the younger sons became members of the nobility instead. In Europe in the MiddleAges, the Nobility were usually cousins of the King, and almost all of them had a King as an ancestor. That's what made them nobility. The King is the descendant of the oldest son of the King. And the Nobility are the descendants of the younger sons.
paragraphs.zh: 子继承制。 在这一系统下，存活下来的最年长 的儿子继承全部土地，他的弟弟们 则不会得到任何东西。 对于国王们，长子继承制意味着王 位将总是传给最为年长的儿子，而 小儿子们则成为贵族成员。 在中世纪欧洲，贵族通常是国王的 表兄弟，他们几乎所有人都有一个 共同的国王祖先，这是导致他们成 为贵族的原因。 国王是那位国王最年长的儿子的 后代。 而贵族是那些比较年轻的儿子们 的后代。

paragraphs.en: Gradually three social classes or estates developed in Europe. The First Estate was the church, and everyone who worked for it. The first estate was those who pray. The Second Estate was the nobility. The second estate was those who fight. And the Third Estate was everyone else, the commoners. The third estate was those who work. This system reached its height in the years between 1000 and 1500AD. And for those centuries, members of the nobility valued two things above all else. Your bloodline or ancestral connection to the King's family. And your skill in battle or
paragraphs.zh: 逐渐地，欧洲发展出了三个阶级， 或者说三个社会阶层。 第一阶层是教会，以及所有为教会 工作的人，这一阶层是那些负责祈 祷的人。 第二阶层是贵族，是那些负责战斗 的人。 第三阶层是剩下的所有人，普通人 们。第三阶层是那些负责工作的 人。 这一系统在公元 1000 到 1500 年达 到了其巅峰。在这几个世纪里，贵 族阶层成员把两件事看得比其他 一切都要重要。 你与国王家族的血脉或者祖先方 面的联系。 以及你在搏斗中的技巧，或者说

paragraphs.en: "prowess". But now lets return to the Franks. The Merovingian dynasty ruled the Franks for almost 300 years across 11 generations. But by the 600s, the Kings were losing power, they stopped leading their men in battle and left the business of government to their Majordomo, or "Mayor of the Palace". By the late 600s, real power shifted from the Kings to the Majordomos. One of the first really powerful Majordomos was Pepin of Herstal. Technically Theodoric III was the King, but he was a puppet King. Pepin was the real leader. In 688, Pepin had a son named Charles Martel who became Major
paragraphs.zh: “英勇”。 但现在让我们回到法兰克人。墨洛 维王朝统治法兰克人近三百年，历 经 11 代。 但到公元七世纪，国王已经失掉了 权柄，他们不再在战斗中身先士 卒，也将朝政事务丢给了他们的宫 相，或者叫“宫廷的主事”。 到七世纪晚期，真正的权力已经从 国王转移到了宫相的手中。 其中最早也最有权柄的大管家名 叫赫斯塔的丕平。技术上，提乌德 里克三世是国王，但他只是个傀儡 国王，丕平才是真正的领导人。 688 年，丕平有了一个名叫查理·马 特的儿子，他在公元 714 年丕平死

paragraphs.en: Domo after Pepin died in 714AD. As you can imagine, the Franks had been once again divided by progeniture. There were three Frankish Kingdoms. And Charles Martel was only the Majordomo of the one in the northeast. So for the next 4 years, Charles Martel fought a war and conquered the Kingdom in the Middle. By 718, there were only 2 Frankish Kingdoms. The one in the north ruled by Charles Martel and the one in the south ruled by Odo the Great. Of course these two men were rivals at first. But during this time a more dangerous enemy was emerging in
