paragraphs.zh: 后接任了宫相一职。 正如你所能想象到的，法兰克人再 次因为诸子均分制而被分裂了，出 现了三个法兰克王国。 而查理·马特只是其中位于东北的 那个王国的宫相。 所以，在接下来的四年里，查理·马 特发动了一次战争，征服了位于中 部的王国。 到公元 718 年，只剩下两个法兰克 王国了。北边的那个由查理·马特 统治，南边的由厄德大帝统治。 当然，他们起初是对手。 但正当这个时候，南方出现了更危 险的敌人。

paragraphs.en: the south. Because in 710 the Ummayyads crossed into Spain. They defeated the Goths at the Battle of Guadalete. And from 711 to 732 they completed their conquest of Spain. In the 720s they began launching raids into Odo's territory. Suddenly Odo was facing a bigger threat than Charles Martel. So Odo reached out to his old enemy. He offered to accept Charles as his boss if Charles would help him defend against the Ummayyads. Charles Martel agreed. When the Umayyad army invaded Gaul in 732, the Franks were unified and ready to face them.
paragraphs.zh: 因为在 710 年，倭马亚军队进入了 西班牙。 他们在瓜达莱特战役中击败了哥 特人。 从 711 年到 732 年，他们完成了对 西班牙的征服。 八世纪二十年代，他们开始侵袭厄 德的领地。 厄德突然间面临着比查理·马特更 大的威胁。 所以厄德向他的旧日敌人伸出了 手。他提出，他可以承认查理为他 的君主，如果查理帮助他击败倭马 亚人的话。 查理·马特同意了。 公元732年，倭马亚军队入侵高卢， 法兰克人于是团结起来，准备迎 战。

paragraphs.en: On October 10th, 732 they met the Umayyad army at the Battle of Tours. In this battle it was Abdul Rahman al Ghafi with a force of cavalry against Charles Martel, who led an infantry force, and Odo, who led a small cavalry force. The battle was fierce, but the Franks had prepared well and they fought off the Umayyads. The Ummayad army decided to turn south and go back to Spain in order to make it home before winter. The Battle of Tours marks the high point of Muslim expansion into Western Europe. In fact the famous historian of Rome, Edward Gibbon, believes
paragraphs.zh: 732 年 10 月 10 日，他们在图尔战 役中遭遇了倭马亚军队。 在这场战役中，正是阿卜杜勒·拉 赫曼率领着一支骑兵部队，对战由 查理·马特率领的步兵部队和由厄 德率领的一支较小的骑兵部队。 战斗是惨烈的，但法兰克人准备充 分，最终击退了倭马亚人。 倭马亚军队决定转而向南，回到西 班牙，以便在冬天降临之前在那里 安营扎寨。 图尔战役标志着穆斯林在西欧扩 张的巅峰。 事实上，罗马著名的历史学家爱德 华·吉本相信，图尔战役是欧洲历

paragraphs.en: that the Battle of Tours was one of the great turning points in European history. He speculates that the Muslims may have conquered all of Europe if the Franks had lost that battle. Although modern historians are a little more skeptical of that idea. In any case, the Battle of Tours made Charles Martel a hero all across the Christian world, especially to the Pope in Rome, who began to see Charles's family as potential allies. When Charles died, in 741 his position was passed down to his son Pepin the Short. But after being Majordomo for 10 years, Pepin decided that he wanted to be the actual King of the Franks.
paragraphs.zh: 史上最重要的转折点之一。 他怀疑，如果法兰克人输掉那场战 役，穆斯林可能会征服整个欧洲。 尽管现代历史学家们对此有点怀 疑。 无论如何，图尔战役将查理·马特 变成了整个基督教世界的英雄，尤 其是对于罗马教皇来说，后者开始 将查理的家族视为潜在的盟友 在查理于 741 年去世以后，他的位 置被传给了他的儿子，矮子丕平。 但在成为宫相十年以后，丕平决 定，他想要成为法兰克的真正国 王。

paragraphs.en: So he asked Pope Gregory III for permission, and the Pope agreed saying that the actual leader of a people should also be the legal King. So in 751 Pepin overthrew the last Merovingian King, Childeric III and sent him off to a monastery to become a monk. But before doing that, Pepin had the monks cut off Childeric's hair, because to the Franks, long hair was the symbol of Kingship. Then in return for the Pope's support, Pepin invaded Italy and defeated the Lombards, who were threatening Rome. And he gave the Pope some of the Lombard territory in Central Italy as a gift.
paragraphs.zh: 所以他向教皇格里高利三世请求 许可，而教皇认可地说，人民的真 正领导人也应该成为法律上的国 王。 所以在 751 年，丕平推翻了最后一 任墨洛维王朝国王希尔德里克三 世，送他到一处修道院去做了僧 侣。 但在做这些事情之前，丕平先让其 他僧侣剪断了希尔德里克的头发， 因为法兰克人相信，长头发是国王 地位的象征。 随后，为了回报教皇的支持，丕平 入侵了意大利并击败了伦巴底族 人，后者当时威胁着罗马。 然后他将一部分位于意大利中部 的伦巴底领土作为礼物献给了教 皇。

paragraphs.en: This territory was called the Donation of Pippin and it made the Pope not just a spiritual leader, but the political leader of that territory as well. The Donations of Pippin became territory that was directly ruled by the Pope. He collected taxes and enforced laws and even had his own army. This territory became known as the Papal states and they existed until the 1870s! Pepin the Short died in 768 and left his kingdom to his two sons: Carloman and Charlemagne. But Carloman died just 3 years later in 771. He was only 20 years old and had no heir.
paragraphs.zh: 这块领土被称为丕平献土，它使得 教皇不再只是一位精神领袖，也成 了这块领土上的政治领袖。 这块由丕平进献的领土直接归入 了教皇统治下，他在此课税，执法， 甚至有了自己的军队。 这块领土被称为教皇国，它一直存 在到了十九世纪七十年代！ 矮子丕平死于 768 年，将王国留给 了他的两个儿子：卡洛曼和查理 曼。 但卡洛曼仅仅三年后就死了，那是 公元 771 年，年仅二十岁，没有继 承人。

paragraphs.en: So Charlemagne became the sole King of the Franks. Beginning with Charles Martel there was now a new dynasty of Kings who ruled the Franks. Since the founder of this dynasty was Charles Martel, and since the name "Charles" in Latin is "Carolus", historians call this dynasty the "Carolingians". And the greatest of the Carolingians was Charlemagne. In life his real name was "Charles" or "Carolus" in Latin. But he was such a great king that people started calling him "Carolus Magnus" or "Charles the Great" in Latin. Carolus Magnus turned into Charles Magne is French and "Charlemagne" in English.
paragraphs.zh: 所以查理曼成了法兰克人的唯一 的国王。 从查理·马特开始，如今是一个新 的王朝的国王在统治法兰克人了。 由于这个王朝的创始人是查理·马 特，而拉丁文中“查理”拼作“卡 洛斯”，历史学家因此将这个王朝 称为加洛林王朝。 加洛林王朝最伟大的国王是查理 曼。 在生时他真正的名字是“查尔斯” 或者拉丁语“卡罗洛斯”，但他是 一位如此伟大的国王，乃至于人们 开始称呼他“Carolus Magnus”， 这是拉丁语拼写的“查尔斯大帝”。 Carolus Magnus 在 法 语 写 成 Charles Magne，在英语则写成 Charlemagne，即查理曼。

paragraphs.en: Like his ancestors, Charlemagne was also a great war leader.And under him the Franks developed a strong cavalry force. First Charlemagne began a six-year war against the Lombards. After completing his conquest of the Lombard in 774 he moved east and conquered Thuringia. In 778 he moved south and took the Pyrenees mountains away from the Emirate of Cordoba, creating a buffer territory between his Kingdom and the Muslims in Spain. Next he began his longest war, a 20-year campaign against the Saxons in the northeast. While that war was going on, he marched east and conquered
paragraphs.zh: 正如他的祖先一样，查理曼也是一 位伟大的战争领导人。在他的领导 下，法兰克人发展出了一支强大的 骑兵军队。 首先，查理曼发动了一场长达六年 的针对伦巴底人的战争。 在 774 年，在完成了对于伦巴底的 征服以后，查理曼移师向东，征服 了图林根。 778 年，他向南进发，从科尔多巴 酋长国手中夺取了比利牛斯山脉， 在他自己的王国与西班牙的穆斯 林之间创造了一个缓冲地带。 随即他开始了他最漫长的战争，一 场长达二十年的针对东北方撒克 逊人的战事。 随着战争的持续，他挥军向东，于 788 年征服了巴伐利亚与卡林西

paragraphs.en: Bavaria and Carintha in 788. Once the Saxons were defeated Charlemagne moved east and conquered theAvars in 796. Then he defeated the Czechs and Moravians in 805. By his death in 814, Charlemagne had created the greatest Kingdom in Western Europe. And of course the Pope in Rome took notice. The Popes had been allies of Charlemagne's family ever since Charles Martel.And so in 799 the Pope asked Charlemagne for help against his enemies in Rome and also in the Byzantine Empire. And Charlemagne agreed, he went south to Rome and strengthened the Pope's power.
paragraphs.zh: 亚。 一旦撒克逊人被击败，查理曼继续 向东进发，在 796 年征服了阿瓦尔 人。 随后，在 805 年，他又击败了捷克 人和摩拉维亚人。 到他在 814 年去世为止，查理曼建 立了西欧最大的王国。 当然，罗马教皇注意到了这一点。 罗马教皇自查理·马特时代就与查 理曼的家族互为盟友，所以，在 799 年，教皇为击败他在罗马和在拜占 庭帝国内的敌人而向查理曼寻求 帮助。 查理曼同意了，他南下来到罗马， 增强了教皇的权柄。

paragraphs.en: And then on Christmas Day 800 AD, the Pope returned the favor by declared Charlemagne the New Roman Emperor. But unlike the old Roman Emperor's, this emperor would be crowned by the Pope making him the "Holy Roman Emperor". And Charlemagne's Kingdom became known as the Holy Roman Empire. So now there were two Kingdoms claiming to be the Roman Empire. There was Charlemagne's Frankish Empire in the west and the Byzantine Empire in the east. But Charlemagne did more than just conquer, he also attracted scholars to his court from all across Europe. Together Charlemagne and these
paragraphs.zh: 然后，在公元 800 年的圣诞日，教 皇报之以李，宣布查理曼为新一任 罗马皇帝。 但与原本的罗马皇帝不同的是，这 位皇帝将由教皇加冕，使得他成为 “神圣罗马皇帝”。 而查理曼的王国也因此成为神圣 罗马帝国。 所以现在有两个自称罗马帝国的 王国了，分别是西方查理曼的法兰 克帝国与东方的拜占庭帝国。 但查理曼所做的不止是四处征伐， 他也从整个欧洲招揽学者到他的 宫廷。 查理曼与这些饱学之士一起，在西

paragraphs.en: men of learning launched a revival of civilization and learning in Western Europe. And one of these scholarsAlcuin of York became Charlemagne's best friend. These two men, the warrior and the scholar put the Holy Roman Empire on a strong foundation. They set up schools in monasteries all across the empire where the sons of the rich could learn to read and write. This launched a revival of learning and preservation of Latin books that historians call the Carolingian Renaissance. The best evidence of this renaissance is seen in manuscripts. From the years 1-800 we only have
paragraphs.zh: 欧发动了文明和学识的复兴运动。 其中的一位学者，约克的阿尔琴， 成了查理曼最好的朋友。 这两个人，战士和学者，共同为神 圣罗马帝国打下了一个强有力的 基础。 他们在整个帝国的各修道院中建 立了学校，使得富人的儿子们可以 学习读写。 这开始了一场复兴学习以及保存 拉丁书籍的运动，历史学家称之为 加洛林复兴。 这次复兴运动最好的证据可以从 手稿中看到。从公元1年到公元800 年，仅有大约 2000 份拉丁文手稿

paragraphs.en: about 2000 surviving Latin manuscripts. But from the years 800-900 we have 7000. These scholars and copyists , mostly working in Monasteries, also developed a standard style of writing letters called Carolingian miniscule. This is when capital and lower-case letters began to develop, as well as the practice of putting spaces between words. We also have some surviving examples of Carolingian architecture like St. John'sAbbey in Switzerland. Or the LorschAbbey gatehouse in Germany.
paragraphs.zh: 流传给我们。 但从公元 800 到公元 900 年，我们 有 7000 份。 这些学者和抄写员大部分在修道 院中工作，他们同时也发展出了对 于手写字母的一种标准写法，被称 为加洛林体。 这是大小写字母分化的开始，也是 词间空格的开始。 我们还有一些加洛林时代的建筑 留存至今，例如瑞士的圣约翰大修 道院。 以及德国的洛尔施修道院门房。

paragraphs.en: Both of these are UNESCO world heritage sites. The Carolingians also developed beautiful carvings of wood and ivory, usually for book covers. And a tradition of painting developed to accompany the texts that were being copied and preserved. These paintings in books became known as "Illuminated Manuscripts". Charlemagne's scholars also developed a standard money system. They took a pound of silver and divided it into 240 pennies. This Carolingian penny became the basis of western European money
paragraphs.zh: 这两个建筑都是联合国教科文组 织认定的世界遗产。 加洛林王朝还发展出了美丽的牙 雕和木雕，通常用于书籍封面。 也发展出了一种绘画传统，在抄写 和保存文本的时候为其配画。 这些书上的画作被称为“配图手 稿”。 查理曼的学者们还发展出了标准 货币系统。 他们把一磅银分成 240 个银币。 加洛林银币由此成为超过一千年 当中西欧货币的基础。

paragraphs.en: for over 1000 years. It was such a good system that it was adopted by the English King Offa of Mercia. In England the system of 240 pennies to pound remained the standard for English money until 1971! But when Charlemagne died in 814, the old problem of Progeniture re-emerged. Charlemagne had only 1 surviving son, but he had 3 grandsons: Lothair, Louis and Charles. These grandsons divided the Holy Roman Empire into 3 pieces in 840. And then they began fighting each other in 843. The Middle Kingdom was crushed between the eastern and western Kingdoms by 870.And
paragraphs.zh: 这是一个非常不错的系统，乃至于 它甚至被英格兰麦西亚国王奥法 所采纳。 在英格兰，这种 240 枚银币总重一 磅的系统直到 1971 年都还是英国 货币的标准！ 但当查理曼在 814 年逝世，诸子均 分制的老问题又出现了。 查理曼只有一个儿子活下来，但他 有三个孙子：Lothair, Louis 和 这三个孙子在840年把神圣罗马帝 国分成了三块。 然后在843年，他们开始相互争斗， 870 年，地处中央的王国在夹击下 灭亡，在原本查理曼的帝国的领土 上出现了西法兰克和东法兰克。

paragraphs.en: from Charlemagne's Empire emerged West Francia and East Francia. East Francia would eventually evolve into the country of France. And West Francia would eventually become Germany. But those are stories for another day. In 800AD, Charlemagne's Holy Roman Empire was the greatest Power in Western Europe. And for this reason, all later western European countries are connected to it in some way. For that reason, Charlemagne is the ancestor of every Royal Family in Western Europe. That's why Charlemagne is so
paragraphs.zh: 东法兰克最终变成了今天的法国。 而西法兰克最终成了德国。 但这就是属于另一天的故事了。 公元 800 年，查理曼的神圣罗马帝 国是西欧最强大的政权。 也因为这个原因，后来的所有西欧 国家都以某种方式与它有所关联。 因此，查理曼是西欧所有皇室的祖 先。 这就是为什么查理曼如此重要，他

paragraphs.en: important; he is the great ancestor of every Royal Family; he is the "Father of Europe". The British Royal Family are his descendants. The current prince George is 44 generations descended from Charlemagne. In France it is the same story. The Last French King, Louis XVI was a 34th generation descendant of Charlemagne. This is why Charlemagne is so important. European history from 800 until the French Revolution can be simplified to the story of Charlemagne's descendants fighting over power. This is why the French Revolution of 1789 is so important, because it marks a change in European
paragraphs.zh: 是每一个皇室家族的祖先，他是 “欧洲之父”。 英国皇室是他的后代，现在的王子 乔治是查理曼的第 44 代孙。 法国也是一样，最后一任法国国王 路易十六，是查理曼的第 34 代孙。 这就是为什么查理曼如此重要，从 公元 800 年起直到法国大革命，欧 洲历史可以被简化成查理曼的后 代们争夺权力的故事。 这就是为什么 1789 年法国大革命 如此重要，因为它标志着欧洲人思 维的转变。

paragraphs.en: thought. Before 1789 legitimate power came from your ancestral relationship to Charlemagne. After 1789, legitimate power came from the will of the People. This is what separates the Middle Ages and Early Modern Period from the Late Modern Period. But now we will shift our attention north. Because in the later years of Charlemagne's rule, a new terrifying menace began to emerge, one that threatened all Europe from the north. And that is the topic of our next class. I hope you enjoyed that lesson, and want to learn more. If you did, I
paragraphs.zh: 在 1789 年以前，合法权力来自于 你与查理曼大帝的血缘关系。 1789 年以后，合法权力来自人民的 意志。 这也是将中世纪以及现代早期和 现代晚期区别开来的东西。 但现在我们需要将我们的注意力 转向北边，因为在查理曼统治的最 后几年，一个新的可怕威胁开始出 现，它自北而来，威胁着整个欧洲。 这是我们下一次课的主题。 我希望你们享受这次课并且希望 了解更多，如果你们想的话，我有

paragraphs.en: have a recommendation for you. It is the Podcast "In Our Time" by the BBC and their episode on the Carolingian Renaissance. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 一个推荐。 这就是 BBC 的播客“In Our Time”， 以及他们关于加洛林复兴的节目。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第十二讲：诺斯人|你知道 Sunday 是一个舶来词吗？

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on world history! Today we will be learning about the Norse people. More commonly known as the Vikings. We will be focusing on the years between 793 and 1066. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. Our story begins with a monastery in Northern England known as Lindisfarne. Lindisfarne was famous for the beautiful illuminated manuscripts that it produced. But on the morning of January 8th, 793 strange men showed up outside the monastery. They had arrived from the ocean on mysterious long
paragraphs.zh: Sunday 是一个舶来词吗？ 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座!今天我们将学习诺斯 人。更广为人知的是维京人。 我们将把重点放在 793 年到 1066 年之间。在我们的时间轴上是这样 的。 我们的故事从英格兰北部的一个 叫做林迪斯法恩的修道院开始。 林迪斯法恩以其精美的彩绘手稿 而闻名。 但是在 1 月 8 日早上，793 名奇怪 的人出现在寺院外。他们乘坐神秘 的小艇从海上来到这里。

paragraphs.en: ships. These men attacked the monastery without mercy. The stole everything of value, burned the buildings, slaughtered the defenseless monks and took other captive to be sold as slaves. Alcuin, the English scholar who was a close friend of Charlemagne wrote about the attack: Never before has such terror appeared in Britain as we have now suffered from a pagan race ... The heathens poured out the blood of saints around the altar, and trampled on the bodies of saints in the temple of God, like dung in the streets. These men were the Scandinavians, also called Norse-men or Northmen. They had come from Scandinavia.
paragraphs.zh: 这些人毫不留情地袭击了修道院。 他们偷走了所有值钱的东西，烧毁 了建筑，屠杀了手无寸铁的僧侣， 还把其他僧侣抓起来当作奴隶贩 卖。 Alcuin，英国学者，查理曼大帝的 好朋友，这样描述这次袭击: 英国以前从未出现过像我们现在 遭 受 异 教 种 族 之 害 这 样 的 恐 怖……外邦人把圣徒的血倒在圣 坛的四围，在神殿中践踏圣徒的身 体，就像践踏街上的粪土。 这些斯堪的纳维亚人，也被称作诺 斯人或北方人。 他们来自斯堪的纳维亚半岛。包括

paragraphs.en: Which includes the modern countries of Denmark, Norway, Sweden and Finland. Like people everywhere the Norse mainly lived by farming or trade. But some would go off on raids. In the Norse language the word for a raider is "Viking" so that word is also used to describe the Norse of this period. The Norse were not yet Christians, they still worshipped the older Germanic gods. And like many ancient people, the Germanic tribes associated their gods with different planets. There was Odin or Woden, the god of wisdom who was associated with the planet Mercury. Then there was Odin's wife Frigg or
paragraphs.zh: 丹麦、诺斯、瑞典和芬兰等现代国 家。 和世界各地的人们一样，诺斯人主 要以农业或贸易为生。但有些人会 发动突袭。在诺斯语中，“掠袭者” 会用“viking”这个词，所以这个 词也被用来描述这个时期的诺斯 人。 诺斯人还不是基督徒，他们仍然崇 拜古老的日耳曼神。 就像许多古代人类一样，日耳曼部 落将他们的神与不同的星球联系 在一起。 奥丁或沃登是智慧之神，代表水 星。 然后是奥丁的妻子弗莱格(Frigg)

paragraphs.en: Freya, who was associated with the planet Venus. Odin had a son named Thor, who was the thunder god, and was associated with the planet Jupiter. Thor is still popular today among fans of Marvel comic books! And there was also Tyr or Tiw who was associated with the planet Mars. The key to Viking power was their excellent skill as sailors. Their long ship was the fastest ship in the world at that time. It had a top speed of 28 km/hour. No other ship in the world could catch them on the open ocean. From 793 to 865, the Vikings raided the English coast over and over again.
paragraphs.zh: 或弗莱娅(Freya)，代表金星。 奥丁有一个儿子叫托尔，他是雷 神，代表木星。 雷神托尔至今仍深受漫威漫画迷 的喜爱! 还有个神叫做 Tyr 或者 Tiw，代表 火星。 维京人力量的关键是他们作为水 手的出色技能。他们的“龙船”是 当时世界上最快的船，最高时速 28 公里。 世界上没有别的船能在广阔的海 洋上追上它们。 从 793 年到 865 年，北欧海盗一遍 又一遍地袭击英国海岸。

paragraphs.en: But in 865, they came with a large army. This time their goal was conquest, not just simple raiding. For the next 9 years they pushed outward and defeated all of the English kingdoms except for one. The territory that they captured became known as the "Danelaw." Because the English were Christians but the Norse were not, English history refers to this invasion force as the "Great HeathenArmy". Only the King of Wessex,Alfred the Great, was able to defeat the Norse. The rest of the Island fell under Viking rule. This Norse period of English history had a big influence on the English language and culture. Over 2000 modern English words come from
paragraphs.zh: 但是在 865 年，他们带来了一支庞 大的军队。这次他们的目标是征 服，而不是简单的劫掠。 在接下来的9年里，他们向外扩张， 除了一个王国，其他的英国王国全 被打败了。 他们占领的领土被称为“丹麦劳”。 因为英国人是基督徒，而诺斯人不 是，所以英国历史把这支侵略军队 称为“伟大的异教徒军队”。 只有韦塞克斯国王阿尔弗雷德大 帝能够打败诺斯人。岛上的其余部 分都归维京人统治。 这段诺斯人的英国历史对英语语 言和文化有很大的影响。超过 2000 个现代英语单词来自诺斯语。这张 幻灯片上有一些例子。

paragraphs.en: the Norse language. Here on this slide are some examples. The Norse also gave the English language names for its days of the week. The first three are obvious. There is Saturday, named after Saturn; Sunday, named after the Sun; Monday, named after the Moon. But there is also Tuesday, which is Tiw's day, who also represented Mars. There was Wednesday, or Woden's Day, who also represented Mercury. There was Thursday, or Thor's Day, who also represented Jupiter. And there was Friday, or Freyya's Day, who also represented Venus. For four Generations, the English
paragraphs.zh: 诺斯人将自己语言中对星期的名 字应用在了英语中。 前三个很明显。周六，来自于土星； 周日来自于太阳；周一，来自于月 亮。 这里还有周二，Tiw’s day, 也代 表了火星。 还有周三，或者叫做 Woden’s Day, 也代表了水星。 还有周四，或者叫 Thor’s Day， 也代表了木星。 还有周五，或者叫做Freyy‘a s Day， 代表了金星。 韦塞克斯的英国国王与诺斯人争

paragraphs.en: Kings of Wessex contended with the Norse. Alfred the Great was succeeded by his son Edward the Elder. Edward the Elder was followed first by his sonAethelstan and then this other son Edmund. Finally King Eadrid and his brother Eadwig succeeded in pushing the Norse out of England. This is the period in history when the name "England" first emerged. But, the Norse were also settlers as well as raiders and conquerors. In 874, a group of Norse led by a man named IngolfurArnarson became the first people to settle Iceland. Then 108 years later one of the
paragraphs.zh: 斗了四代。 阿尔弗雷德大帝的继位是他的儿 子大爱德华。 大爱德华之后是他的儿子埃塞尔 斯坦，然后是另一个儿子埃德蒙。 最后，国王艾德里德和他的兄弟艾 德威格成功地把诺斯人赶出了英 格兰。 这是历史上“英格兰”一词首次出 现的时期。 但是，除了是掠袭者和征服者，诺 斯人也向其他地方移民。 874 年，一个名叫因戈尔弗·阿纳 尔森(Ingolfur Arnarson)的诺斯 人领导的一群人成为第一批定居 冰岛的人。 108 年后，一位名叫埃里克的冰岛

paragraphs.en: Iceland settlers named Erik the Red became the first person to explore Greenland. Erik and his men explored Greenland for 3 years from 982 to 985, and then they returned to Iceland to recruit settlers. In 985 the Norse established a colony on Greenland which survived for about 400 years. The next year in 986 a Norse explorer named Bjarni Herjólfsson was trying to reach Greenland from Iceland but he got blown off course. As he made his way back to Greenland he sighted a new land that no European had ever set eyes on before. It was Canada. When Bjarni came back to Greenland, he told other people
paragraphs.zh: 移民成为第一个探索格陵兰岛的 人。 从 982 年到 985 年，埃里克和他的 人在格陵兰岛探险了 3 年，然后他 们回到冰岛招募定居者。 985 年，诺斯人在格陵兰岛建立了 一个殖民地，并在那里生存了大约 400 年。 第二年，986 年，一位名叫 Bjarni Herjolfsson 的诺斯探险者试图从 冰岛到达格陵兰岛，但他偏离了航 线。 在返回格陵兰岛的途中，他看到了 一块欧洲人从未见过的新大陆。那 就是加拿大。 当 Bjarni 回到格陵兰岛时，他把 他所看到的告诉了其他人。

paragraphs.en: about what he had seen. These stories interested Erik the Red's son, Leif Erickson. So in the year 1001AD, Leif led another expedition further west and they reached the Canadian island of Newfoundland. This made Leif Erickson's expedition the first Europeans to reach theAmericas, in the summer of 1001AD. On the island of Newfoundland they found lots of cranberries and squash berries which they used to make wine. So when Leif returned to Greenland he called this new country "Vine-land". Intrigued by the promise of more
paragraphs.zh: 这些故事引起了埃里克的儿子利 夫·埃里克森的兴趣。 所以在公元 1001 年，利夫带领另 一支探险队向西，到达了加拿大的 纽芬兰岛。 这使得赖夫·埃里克森的探险队在 公元 1001 年夏天成为第一批到达 美洲的欧洲人。 在纽芬兰岛上，他们发现了许多小 红莓和压碎的浆果，就是他们用来 酿酒的东西。 所以当 Leif 回到格陵兰岛的时候 他把这个新国家叫做“葡萄园”（文 兰德）。 为了得到更多的土地来定居，两支

paragraphs.en: land for settlement, two expeditions traveled to Vinland to set up a colony, one in 1004AD and another in 1009AD. But unlike Greenland, Vinland was already settled by NativeAmerican tribes. Unfortunately, the relationship between the Norse and NativeAmericans turned violent. Eventually repeated Native American attacks forced the Norse to abandon the settlement. So the Norse explorer Leif Erickson was the first European to reach America, nearly 500 years before Columbus. But unlike Columbus, his discovery had no long-term affect on history. But the Norse did not just expand west, they also expanded east.
paragraphs.zh: 探险队前往文兰德建立殖民地，一 支在公元 1004 年出发，另一支在 公元 1009 年出发。 但与格陵兰岛不同的是，文兰岛已 经被美洲土著部落所定居。不幸的 是，诺斯人和美洲原住民之间的关 系变得暴力。 最终，印第安人的多次袭击迫使诺 斯人放弃了这个定居点。 因此，诺斯探险家利夫·埃里克森 是第一个到达美洲的欧洲人，比哥 伦布早了近 500 年。但与哥伦布不 同，他的发现对历史没有长期影 响。 但诺斯人不仅向西扩张，还向东扩 张。

paragraphs.en: In the early 800s the Norse discovered trade routes that could connect the Baltic Sea to the Black sea along the Dnieper, Don and Volga rivers. The Norse began to take advantage of this trade and even reached the city of Constantinople in the early 800s. The Byzantines noticed these new-comers and called them the Varangians. These river trade routes also connected the Norse to theAbassids. So the Norse began trading with the Byzantines andAbassids. They traded furs, amber, ivory, and most terribly slaves, some captured from as far away as Ireland.
paragraphs.zh: 在 8 世纪早期，诺斯人发现了可以 沿着第聂伯河、顿河和伏尔加河连 接波罗的海和黑海的贸易路线。 诺斯人开始利用这个贸易路线，甚 至在公元800年代初到达君士坦丁 堡。 拜占庭人注意到了这些新来者，称 他们为瓦朗日人。 这些河流贸易路线也连接了诺斯 人和阿巴斯。 因此，诺斯人开始与拜占庭人和阿 巴斯人进行贸易。 他们交换毛皮、琥珀、象牙和更可 怕的是，他们还交换奴隶，有些奴 隶甚至是从遥远的爱尔兰被抓来 的。

paragraphs.en: The Norse also raided the local Slavic people along these rivers and took them as captives who they sold into slavery in the great Greek and Arab empires. The Norse sent so many slavic people into slavery down south, that word "slave" actually comes from the word "slav". But the Norse also traded and intermarried with the local slavs, and captured the city of Kiev which became their capital. Over time the Norse and Slavs mixed together and formed the state called "The Kievan Rus". This is the earliest origin of what would later become Russia. We will hear more about the Kievan
paragraphs.zh: 诺斯人还袭击了沿河的斯拉夫人， 把他们当作俘虏，卖给伟大的希腊 和阿拉伯帝国做奴隶。 诺斯人把很多斯拉夫人送到南方 当奴隶，奴隶这个词实际上来自 “斯拉夫”slav。 但诺斯人也与当地的斯拉夫人进 行贸易和通婚，并占领了基辅市， 成为他们的首都。 随着时间的推移，诺斯人和斯拉夫 人融合在一起，形成了一个名为 “基辅罗斯”的国家 这就是后来成为俄罗斯的最早起 源。 我们将在后面的课程中听到更多

paragraphs.en: Rus in latter lectures. Finally, the Norse also had a big impact on French history. In the early 800s the Norse launched Viking raids against cities and Monasteries on the French coast. After Charlemagne's son, Louis the Pious died, he left his empire to his three sons. We discussed this last time. Those three sons started to fight each other, weakening the empire. They even hired the Norse to act as mercenaries, which was a bad idea, because it made the Norse war leaders richer and more familiar with the Frankish countryside. In 845 the Norse even launched a raid all the way down the Seine
paragraphs.zh: 关于基辅罗斯的内容。 最后，诺斯语也对法兰西历史产生 了重大影响。 在 8 世纪早期，诺斯人对法兰西海 岸的城市和修道院发动了海盗袭 击。 查理曼的儿子，虔诚的路易死后， 他把帝国留给了三个儿子。我们上 次讨论过。 这三个儿子开始互相争斗，削弱了 帝国。 他们甚至雇佣诺斯人充当雇佣兵， 这是一个坏主意，因为这使得诺斯 战争的领导人更加富有，也更加熟 悉法兰克人的乡村。 845 年，诺斯人甚至对塞纳河发起 突袭，将巴黎围困。

paragraphs.en: river and put Paris under siege. Eventually, the Frankish king Charles the Bald was forced to pay off the Norse commander Ragnar Lodbrock to get him to leave. But this was just a temporary solution and the Norse kept coming back. The Norse attacked Paris again in the 860s. In 880 they captured the Carolingian capital ofAachen, and again the Frankish king had to pay to get them to leave. And in 885 the Norse came back to Paris again. This army in 885 was led by the Norse commander Rollo. But after 40 years of being kicked
paragraphs.zh: 最后，法兰克国王，秃头查理，被 迫向诺斯指挥官罗布罗克付钱，让 他离开。 但这只是一个暂时的解决办法，诺 斯人还是不断地回来。 诺斯人在 860 年代再次袭击巴黎。 880 年，他们占领了加洛林王朝的 首都亚琛，法兰克国王不得不再次 付钱让他们离开。 885 年，诺斯人又回到了巴黎。 885 年，这支军队由诺斯指挥官罗 洛率领。 但是在被诺斯人鞭笞 40 年之后，

paragraphs.en: around by the Norse, the Franks were better prepared. But the siege dragged on, so Charles the Fat was forced to pay Rollo to leave. Twenty-six years later In 911, Rollo attacked again. But the Franks had a new king named Charles the Simple, and he was more determined to fight. The French defeated Rollo at the battle of Chartres. Then the Frankish King Charles the Simple arrived with a force of mounted horsemen. He drove Rollo's men all the way back to their ships. But instead of trying to finish Rollo off, Charles the Simple decided to use his stronger position to negotiate
paragraphs.zh: 法兰克人有了更好的准备。 但是包围还在继续，所以胖子查理 不得不付钱给罗洛让他离开。 26 年后的 911 年，罗洛再次发动袭 击。 但是法兰克人有了一个新的国王， 名叫查理（糊涂王查理三世），他 更有决心去战斗。法兰西人在沙特 尔战役中击败了罗洛。 法兰克国王查理斯率领骑兵到达。 他把罗洛的人一路赶回船上。 但是，查理决定利用他的强势地位 来谈判，而不是努力了结罗洛。

paragraphs.en: a deal. So Charles the Simple gave Rollo three terms: 1)Convert to Christianity 2)Keep Peace with the Crown 3)Provide troops in war Then Charles would give Rollo the province of Normandy in northwest France. Rollo agreed. This new province became known as "Normandy" after Rollo's Norse who settled it. The other advantage was that Normandy contained the mouth of the Seine river. Since this was now Rollo's territory, and Rollo was now loyal to the King of France, there could be no more Viking raids on
paragraphs.zh: 所以查理给了罗洛三个条件: 皈依基督教 与国王保持和平 在战争中提供部队 然后查理将法兰西西北部的诺曼 底省交给罗洛。 罗洛同意了。 这个新建的省份在罗洛的诺斯人 定居后被称为“诺曼底”。 另一个好处是诺曼底有塞纳河的 入海口。因为这里现在是罗洛的领 土，罗洛现在效忠于法兰西国王， 所以海盗再也不能袭击巴黎了。

paragraphs.en: Paris. These Norse lords took Frankish wives and so within a few generations, their children had assimilated into French culture. Within two generations, Rollo's descendants all spoke French and were Catholics. But they were still proud of their Norse heritage on their father's side. Rollo's Norse Vikings became the ancestors the Norman people. And Rollo was the great-great-great-grandfather of another famous King, William the Conqueror. But that is a story for our next class! Join me next time as we learn about the Normans!
paragraphs.zh: 这些诺斯贵族娶了法兰克人的妻 子，因此几代人之后，他们的孩子 就融入了法兰西文化。 在两代人的时间里，罗洛的后代都 说法语，都是天主教徒。但他们仍 然为自己的诺斯血统感到自豪，站 在父亲这一边。 罗洛的北欧海盗成为了诺曼底人 的祖先。 罗洛是另一位著名国王——征服 者威廉的曾曾曾祖父。 但那是我们下节课的故事! 下次和我一起学习诺曼底人吧!

paragraphs.en: Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lecture and want to learn more. If so I have a recommendation for you. My recommendation is the Youtube channel Extra Credit, they have an excellent series on the Viking expansion and its effect on England, France, Russia and Ireland.
paragraphs.zh: 好了，各位，我希望你们喜欢这堂 课，并且希望你们愿意了解更多。 如果是的话，我会给您们推荐： Youtube 频道的 Extra Credit，他 们有一个非常优秀的系列讲座，关 于维京扩展和它对英国，法兰西， 俄罗斯和爱尔兰的影响。

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History, today we will discuss the beginning of the Wars of Religion. This class will focus on the years between 1555 and 1572. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. I'd like to begin today's lesson by showing you a painting. It is called AHuguenot on St. Bartholomew's Day. Study this painting carefully, you will see it again at the end of the class. Last class, we discussed the wars between Charles V and Francis I in the early 1500s. In 1547, Francis I died and his
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列讲 座，今天我们将讨论宗教战争的开 端。 这节课主要讲的是 1555 - 1572 年 间的历史。这是时间轴。 先给大家看一幅画。这幅画名称是 《一位胡格诺教徒在圣巴塞洛缪 的一天》。 好好看看这幅画，你会在课程结束 时再次看到它。 上节课，我们讨论了 16 世纪早期， 查理五世和弗朗西斯一世之间的 战争。 1547 年，弗朗西斯一世去世，王位

paragraphs.en: crown passed to his son Henry II. Henry II married, Catherine de Medici, an Italian princess and they had 7 children by 1555. In that same year, Charles V retired from being king, the stress of the job had worn him down. His Empire had two parts the Spanish territory and theAustrian territory. Charles passed control of the Netherlands, Southern Italy and his Spanish territory to his son Phillip II but hisAustrian Territory and Holy Roman Imperial crown went to his brother Ferdinand. Charles V belonged to the Hapsburg family, after 1555 no one King ever controlled both Spain and Germany again, but his Hapsburg family
paragraphs.zh: 传给了儿子亨利二世。 亨利二世娶了意大利公主凯瑟 琳·德·梅迪西为妻，到了 1555 年，他们已经拥了有 7 个孩子。 同年，查理五世退位，因为工作的 压力使他疲惫不堪。 他的帝国分为两部分：西班牙和奥 地利。 查理五世将荷兰、意大利南部和西 班牙领土控制权交给了自己的儿 子菲利普二世，而奥地利领土和神 圣罗马帝国的王位则传给了他的 弟弟费迪南德。 查理来自哈普斯堡家族，1555 年之 后的 150 年中，没有哪个国王能够 同时控制西班牙和德国，只有哈普 斯堡家族一直控制着这两个地方。

paragraphs.en: remained in control of both parts of Europe for the next 150 years. We refer to the two sides of this royal family as the "Spanish Hapsburgs" and "Austrian Hapsburgs" Meanwhile, Charles arranged a marriage alliance between Mary I of England and his son Phillip II in 1554. But Mary was unable to have children and died of uterine cancer on November 17th, 1558, so the English crown passed to her younger sister Elizabeth. Elizabeth was crowned Queen on January 15th, 1559 and made England Protestant again. Charles V had also died in 1558 and Philip became the King of Spain,
paragraphs.zh: 我们将这两处的皇家分别称为“西 班牙哈布斯堡王朝”和“奥地利哈 布斯堡王朝”。 同时，在 1554 年，查尔斯安排英 格兰的玛丽一世和他的儿子菲利 普二世联姻。 但玛丽无法生育，并于 1558 年 11 月 17 日死于子宫癌，因此英国王 位传给了她的妹妹伊丽莎白。 1559 年 1 月 15 日，伊丽莎白加冕 成为英国女王，英格兰再次推行新 教。 1558 年，查理五世去世，菲利普成 为了西班牙、意大利南部和荷兰的

paragraphs.en: southern Italy and the Netherlands. Many European princes were interested in marrying Elizabeth, but she turned them all down.. Elizabeth never married, because then all her power would have gone to her husband. So Phillip gave up on the idea of marrying Elizabeth and proposed a marriage between himself and, the oldest daughter of the King of France. Henry II accepted this offer, and decades of war between Spain and France came to an end. OnApril 3rd 1559, Spain and France formalized this agreement with the Peace Treaty of Cateau Cambresis.
paragraphs.zh: 国王。 许多欧洲王子都想娶伊丽莎白，但 她都拒绝了。 伊丽莎白终生未嫁，因为如果她结 婚的话，她所有的权力都将归她丈 夫所有。 于是菲利普放弃了和伊丽莎白结 婚的念头，转而向法国国王的长女 求婚。 亨利二世接受了这个提议，就这 样，西班牙和法国之间长达数十年 的战争结束了。 1559 年 4 月 3 日，西班牙和法国正 式签署了《卡图坎布雷齐和约》。

paragraphs.en: And then Phillip II and Elizabeth of Valois were married 3 months later on June 22nd, 1559. By all accounts it was a happy marriage.And Phillip loved his French wife, although he was 18 years older than her. Henry II must have felt good about this marriage alliance. Just the year before, he had secured another great marriage alliance for his eldest son Francis II. Henry had married Francis to a Scottish princess named Mary Stuart. This marriage was also a wise strategic choice. Mary Stuart was the only daughter of James V, the King of Scotland, and a descendant of Henry VII of England. This meant that Mary was
paragraphs.zh: 1559 年 6 月 22 日，菲利普二世和 伊莉莎白完婚。 尽管菲利普比他的法国妻子大 18 岁，但他十分爱妻子，在众人看来， 这是一段幸福的婚姻。 亨利二世一定觉得这种联姻很好。 就在一年前，他还为长子弗朗西斯 二世争取到了另一个伟大的联姻。 亨利让弗朗西斯二世迎娶了苏格 兰公主玛丽·斯图亚特为妻。 这段婚姻也是一个明智的战略选 择。 玛丽·斯图亚特是苏格兰国王詹姆 斯五世的独生女，也是英格兰国王 亨利七世的后裔。这意味着玛丽是 苏格兰合法的女王，是继伊丽莎白

paragraphs.en: the rightful Queen of Scotland, and next in line to the English throne after Elizabeth. And on Mary's maternal side, she was a member of the House of Guise, one of the most powerful noble families in France. With four healthy sons, three healthy daughters and two excellent marriage alliances, Henry II must have felt like he was on top of the world. So on June 30th, 1559, Henry II called for a tournament in order to celebrate the peace with Spain and the marriage of his daughter. And that's when everything began to go wrong... Henry himself took part in the tournament, and during the jousting
paragraphs.zh: 之后英国王位的第二顺位继承人。 而玛丽的母系是法国最有权势的 贵族家族之一——吉斯家族。 拥有四个健康的儿子，三个健康的 女儿以及两个优秀的婚姻联盟，亨 利四世一定感觉自己身处世界之 巅。 因此，1559 年 6 月 30 日，亨利二 世发起了一场锦标赛，庆祝与西班 牙的和平以及女儿的婚礼。 就从那以后，一切开始变得糟 糕…… 亨利亲自参加了比赛，在与苏格兰 卫队队长加布里埃尔·德·蒙哥马

paragraphs.en: the lance of Gabriel de Montgomery, the Scottish captain of his guards, shattered against his shield. This sometimes happens in jousting as you can see in this picture. And they attacked the unarmed men, women and children in their church.After this they set the church on fire. By the time it was all over, Francis had killed 62 innocent people and wounded over 100 more. All across France, Huguenots were enraged by the massacre. So a Huguenot's rebellion began. It was the beginning of the French Wars of Religion. The Catholic forces were led by the
paragraphs.zh: 利的骑枪比赛中，他的盾牌被对方 打得粉碎。 正如你在这幅图中所看到的，这种 情况在格斗中时有发生。 弗朗西斯他们袭击了教堂里手无 寸铁的男女老少。之后他们放火烧 了教堂。 最终，他们共杀害了 62 名无辜者， 打伤了 100 多人。 整个法国的胡格诺派都对这场大 屠杀感到十分愤怒。 于是，胡格诺派发起了叛乱。这就 是法国宗教战争的开始。 天主教势力由吉斯家族领导，被称

paragraphs.en: Guise family and were called the Catholic Leauge. Several Protestant noblemen became leaders on the Protestant side, and the Royal family and more moderate Catholics were stuck in the middle and were known as the "Politiques". Sporadic fighting and killing continued for 8 years from 1562 to 1570 as Protestants and Catholics battled each other all across the country. Meanwhile back in Spain, Phillip had his own concerns. He was running out of money. Like his father Phillip II was constantly at war. Here is a list of
paragraphs.zh: 为天主教联盟。 一些新教徒贵族成为新教的领袖。 皇室与更温和的天主教徒被夹在 中间，称为“政治派”。 从 1562 年一直到 1570 年，新教徒 和天主教徒在全国各地不断地进 行零星的战斗和杀戮，共持续了 8 年。 同时，西班牙的菲利普也有所担 忧。 同时，西班牙的菲利普也有所担 他的钱不够花了。 就像他经常打仗的父亲菲利普二 世一样，这是他所有打过的战争的

paragraphs.en: all his wars. And wars eat money. Despite all the Gold and Silver coming from America, the Spanish crown got deeper and deeper into debt. So Phillip decided that the Dutch part of his empire needed to pay more taxes. This is a map of the Habsburg Netherlands, home of the Dutch people. It main city was Antwerp. At the time, the city ofAntwerp was rich. That's becauseAntwerp and the Netherlands were the center of European trade. At the time, Phillips half sister Margaret of Parma ruled the Netherlands, and was assisted by
paragraphs.zh: 列表。 战争消耗金钱。尽管有美洲不断输 送金银，西班牙国王还是变得负债 累累。 所以菲利普决定向荷兰部分征更 多的税。 这是荷兰人的家园——哈布斯堡 的地图。它的主要城市是安特卫 普。 当时，安特卫普是一个富裕的城 市。 因为安特卫普和荷兰是欧洲贸易 的中心。 当时，菲利普同父异母的妹妹玛格 丽特·帕尔马统治着荷兰，并得到 三位荷兰贵族的帮助：拉莫拉、菲

paragraphs.en: the three leading Dutch noblemen: Lamora, Philip and William the Silent. These three men formed her council of government. Over the last 30 years John Calvin had been sending Calvinist ministers to the Netherlands, and they had been converting people and establishing Calvinist churches. So by 1566, the northern Netherlands had become majority Calvinist, just like Scotland. The Netherlands also had a strong tradition of liberty and self rule. Power was divided among many different factions, and the Dutch ruled themselves with a Parliament called the Estates General. Phillip was determined to change all that.
paragraphs.zh: 利普和沉默者威廉。这三个人组成 了她的政府委员会。 在过去的 30 年里，约翰·加尔文 不断地派遣加尔文派神父到荷兰， 在那里收服信众，并建立了加尔文 派教会。 到了 1566 年，荷兰北部成为了加 尔文主义的势力范围，就像苏格兰 一样。 荷兰拥有坚实的自由和自治传统。 荷兰人将权力划分成许多不同的 派别，并通过一种叫做三级会议的 议会进行统治。 菲利普决心改变这一切。

paragraphs.en: He wanted to end self rule. Destroy the Protestant movement. and Increase taxes. So Philip began a persecution of Protestants in the Netherlands. This combined with a bad harvest in 1565 to make the Dutch Protestants deeply angry. So in 1566, Dutch Protestants began attacking Catholic churches and smashing their decorations. Dutch historians call this the Beeldenstorm or "Statue Storm" Here is a painting of Dutch Protestants tearing down a statue in a Catholic church. After a few months, the Beeldenstorm was stopped by Margaret and her council.
paragraphs.zh: 他想结束自治； 摧毁新教运动； 增加税收。 于是菲利普开始迫害荷兰的新教 徒。 再加上 1565 年的歉收，这一切激 怒了荷兰的新教徒。 1566 年，荷兰新教徒开始攻击天主 教教堂，砸毁教堂装饰。 荷兰历史学家称之为“圣像破坏运 动”或“雕像风暴”。 这是一幅描绘荷兰新教徒推倒天 主教教堂雕像的画。 几个月后，“圣像破坏运动”被玛 格丽特和她的委员会阻止了。

paragraphs.en: But the Beeldenstorm enraged Phillip.And he decided to send a Spanish army to the Netherlands to punish the Dutch and take direct control of the situation. This army was led by Don FernandoAlvarez, the Duke of Parma. This army was led by Don FernandoAlvarez, the Duke of Parma. Alvarez marched 10,000 to Brussels and arrived on the 22nd ofAugust, 1567. In September Alvarez invited Lamoral and Phillip to have dinner with him, and then arrested them in his house. But William of Orange smelled a trap and escaped. Margaret was horrified by this
paragraphs.zh: 但是这场运动激怒了菲利普。他决 定派一支西班牙军队去荷兰惩罚 这些人，并且控制局面。这支军队 由帕尔马公爵唐·费尔南多·阿尔 瓦雷斯领导。 1567 年 8 月 22 日，阿尔瓦雷斯率 领 1 万人前往布鲁塞尔。 阿尔瓦雷斯行军 1 万英里，于 1567 年 8 月 22 日抵达布鲁塞尔。 9 月，阿尔瓦雷斯邀请拉莫拉和菲 利普共进晚餐，然后在家中逮捕了 他们。 但是奥兰治的威廉嗅到了陷阱的 味道，于是逃跑了。 玛格丽特对同事们所受到的待遇

paragraphs.en: treatment of her colleagues and resigned in protest. Lamoral and Philip were both put on trial, found guilty under false pretenses, and executed on June 5th, 1568. ThenAlvarez set up special courts called the Council of Troubles. These courts began executing Protestant leaders by the hundreds. But instead of crushing Dutch resistance, these actions enraged the Dutch and a rebellion began. And then William returned to the Netherlands at the head of an army in 1568. The Dutch Revolt had begun. The Spanish were more powerful, but they kept running out of money,
paragraphs.zh: 感到震惊，辞职以示抗议。 拉莫拉和菲利普都被送上了法庭， 以莫须有的指控被判有罪，并于 1568 年 6 月 5 日处决。 后来，阿尔瓦雷斯设立了特别法 庭，名为“麻烦委员会”。 这些法庭处决了数百名新教领袖。 但是，这非但没有粉碎荷兰人的抵 抗，反而激怒了他们，于是，叛乱 开始了。 1568 年，威廉率领军队回到荷兰。 荷兰起义开始了。 西班牙人更强大，但他们总是缺 钱，荷兰人为保卫家园而战，所以

paragraphs.en: and the Dutch were fighting to defend their homes, so they fought harder.
paragraphs.zh: 他们更拼。

titleZh: 第九讲：穆斯林的黄金时代

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History, today we will learn about the Dutch Revolt and other historical events connected to it. In this lesson we will focus on the years between 1572 and 1609. Last time we learned about the murder of Gaspard de Coligny and the Huguenots in the St. Bartholemew's Day Massacre. After this the war between the Catholics and Protestants resumed in France, and hatred became even stronger in the hearts of both sides. But the war went badly for the Huguenots in 1573 because their leaders were either dead or captive.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程，今天我们将学习荷兰起 义以及与之相关的历史事件。 这节课我们将主要回顾 1572-1609 年间发生的故事。 上节课我们学习了加斯帕德·德·科 利尼和胡格诺教派在圣巴塞莱穆 日大屠杀中被杀的故事。 此后，天主教徒和新教徒之间的战 争在法国复燃，双方心中的仇恨更 加强烈。 但战争形势在 1573 年对很胡格诺 派不利，因为他们的领袖不是死了 就是被俘虏了。

paragraphs.en: So thousands of Huguenots began leaving France. However, things did not go too well for the Royal family either. Charles IX had been close to Gaspard de Coligny, so the murder of his close friend by his own mother caused him to lose his mind with grief and confusion. He became more and more ill from the stress and died of Tuberculosis on May 30th, 1574. The crown then passed to his younger brother Henry III. By 1564, three of Henry II's children had died. And then the youngest brother, Francis ofAnjou decided to rebel against his family and join the Protestant side in September of
paragraphs.zh: 于是，成千上万的胡格诺派教徒开 始离开法国。 然而，对王室来说，事情也不是很 顺利。查理九世与加斯帕德·德·科 利尼交往甚密，也就是说，他的好 朋友被自己的母亲杀害了，这使他 陷入了悲伤和困惑之中。 1574 年 5 月 30 日，查理九世死于 肺结核，王位传给了他的弟弟亨利 三世。 到 1564 年时，亨利二世死了三个 孩子。 1575 年 9 月，他最小的弟弟，在安 茹的弗朗西斯决定叛变，加入新教 阵营。

paragraphs.en: 1575. Soon afterwards, Henry of Navarre escaped from captivity in February of 1576. With these two noblemen leading them, the Protestant cause was strengthened as the war continued. Meanwhile back in Spain Phillip II had a new opportunity. The King of Portugal, Sebastian I, went to war against the Muslim king of Morocco and fought a battle at Ksar el Kebir, known to history as the battle of the three kings. Unfortunately for Portugal, Sebastian died in that battle. Sebastian left no heir, so his uncle Phillip was actually next in line to the throne.
paragraphs.zh: 不久之后，1576 年 2 月，在纳瓦尔 的亨利从囚禁中逃脱了。 在这两位贵族的领导下，新教势力 在战争中得到了加强。 同时在西班牙，菲利普二世有了一 个新的机会。 葡萄牙国王塞巴斯蒂安一世与摩 洛哥穆斯林国王开战，在克萨埃尔 克比尔打了一场仗，历史上称为 “三王之战”。 不幸的是，塞巴斯蒂安在那场战役 中牺牲了。 塞巴斯蒂安没留下后嗣，所以实际 上，他的叔叔菲利普是王位的下一 个继承人。

paragraphs.en: It took some time, but Phillip eventually secured his claim as the King of Portugal. In 1580 he unified the Portuguese Empire and the Spanish Empire into the Iberian Union. This also led to a union of the overseas empires of Spain and Portugal. Here is a map of both empires in 1579. And this map shows us the Iberian Union in 1580. Here we see a map of all of Philip's European territories in 1580. Generally speaking, during his reign, Phillip gained Portugal and lost the Netherlands. Because on July 26th, 1581, the Dutch decided to formally declare
paragraphs.zh: 菲利普费了一番周折，但最终还是 获得了葡萄牙国王的王位。1580 年，他统一了葡萄牙帝国和西班牙 帝国，形成了伊比利亚联盟。 这也促成了西班牙和葡萄牙帝国 在海外的联合。这是 1579 年两个 帝国的地图。 这张地图显示的是 1580 年伊比利 亚联盟的地图。 这是 1580 年菲利普所拥有的欧洲 领土的地图。 通常的说法是，在菲利普统治期 间，他得到了葡萄牙，失去了荷兰。 因为在 1581 年 7 月 26 日，荷兰人 决定通过废除法令，正式宣布独

paragraphs.en: their independence with theAct of Abjuration. Here is a painting of the Dutch leadership signing the act. In 1581, the Dutch leader, William of Orange also offered Francis of Valois the title of King of the Netherlands in exchange for his support. Francis accepted, and the Dutch were happy because with French support they felt sure they would defeat Spain. As a reward for this accomplishment, Elizabeth made Francis Drake a Knight on April 4th 1581. Of course, Drake also attacked Spanish ships during this voyage.
paragraphs.zh: 立。 这是一幅荷兰领导人签署该法案 的画作。 1581 年，荷兰领袖奥兰治亲王威廉 还授予瓦卢瓦的弗朗西斯荷兰国 王的头衔，以换取后者的支持。 弗朗西斯接受了这一头衔，荷兰人 为此很高兴，因为有了法国人的支 持，他们确信自己能打败西班牙。 1581 年 4 月 4 日，伊丽莎白授予弗 朗西斯·德雷克骑士称号，以奖励 他的成就。 当然，德雷克在这次航行中也袭击 了西班牙船只。

paragraphs.en: And this, along with all the other privateers made Phillip II quite angry. Then Elizabeth did something which pushed Phillip too far. In 1585 she signed the treaty of Nonsuch with the Dutch. The English offered the Dutch soldiers and money to support their war, and the Dutch recognized Elizabeth as their honorary Queen, and gave the English two seats in their Parliament. Enraged, Phillip decided to attack England. He built a great fleet called the "SpanishArmada" to carry out this invasion. TheArmada set sail in July of 1588. But the English navy organized its defenses well and they attacked the
paragraphs.zh: 德雷克以及其他所有海盗船的行 为让菲利普二世非常生气。 后来，伊丽莎白做了一件逼菲利普 太甚的事。1585 年，她与荷兰人签 订了《诺萨奇条约》。英国人给荷 兰人士兵和钱支持他们的战争，作 为交换，荷兰人承认伊丽莎白为荣 誉女王，并给英国人议会中的两个 席位。 菲利普被激怒了，他决定进攻英格 兰。他建立了一支名为“西班牙无 敌舰队”的庞大舰队去完成这次入 侵。 这支舰队于 1588 年 7 月启航。 但是英国海军很好地组织了防御， 他们在 1588 年 7 月 28 日袭击了无

paragraphs.en: armada at the Battle of Gravelines on July 28th, 1588. The English were led by Francis Drake, and the Spanish byAlonso Perez de Guzman. The English were led by Francis Drake, and the Spanish byAlonso Perez de Guzman. After their defeat at Gravelines, the Spanish tried to escape by sailing around Scotland, but storms destroyed even more of the fleet and the Spanish limped home having lost 1/3 of their ships. Meanwhile, back in France Henry II decided that theArmada's defeat had weakened Spain, the main supporters of the Guise family. So in December of 1588 Henry III decided to assassinate the Guise
paragraphs.zh: 敌舰队，史称格拉弗林战役。 英格兰战队由弗朗西斯·德雷克率 领，西班牙战队由阿隆索·佩雷 斯·德·古兹曼率领。 英格兰战队由弗朗西斯·德雷克率 领，西班牙战队由阿隆索·佩雷 斯·德·古兹曼率领。 在格拉弗林战役中战败后，西班牙 人试图绕过苏格兰逃跑，但风暴摧 毁了更多的船队，西班牙人失去了 三分之一的船只，一瘸一拐地回了 家。 与此同时，在法国，亨利二世认为 无敌舰队的失败削弱了吉斯家族 的主要支持者——西班牙的力量。 所以，1588 年 12 月，亨利三世决 定刺杀吉斯兄弟。

paragraphs.en: brothers. So in December of 1588 Henry III decided to assassinate the Guise brothers. But the Catholic population of France loved the Guise brothers, so they rebelled against their King, and Henry III had to flee Paris. By killing the Guise brothers Henry III had angered so many Catholics, that he was forced to join the Protestant side led by Henry of Navarre. However, the next year a Catholic monk named Jacques Clement pretended to deliver a secret message for the King.As the King leaned forward to hear his message, Clement stabbed him the belly with a hidden knife.
paragraphs.zh: 所以，1588 年 12 月，亨利三世决 定刺杀吉斯兄弟。 但是法国的天主教徒喜欢吉斯兄 弟，所以他们背叛了国王，亨利三 世不得不逃离巴黎。 杀死吉斯兄弟的亨利三世激怒了 很多天主教徒，以至于他被迫加入 纳瓦尔·亨利领导的新教阵营。 然而，第二年，一个名叫雅克·克 莱门特的天主教修道士假装要给 国王传递了一个秘密信息，当国王 探身要听消息时，克莱门特用一把 藏着的刀刺伤了他的腹部。

paragraphs.en: Henry's guard's killed Clement within moments, but the King was mortally wounded. Henry III lingered for a day before dying from the wound. On his deathbed he made Henry of Navarre his heir. And Henry of Navarre became Henry IV, the King of France, on the 8th of January 1589. The war continued for 4 more years, and on July 25th, 1593, Henry and his army were outside the gates of Paris. Henry faced a choice, he could try to capture Paris after a long grinding siege, or he convert to Catholicism and take the city without a fight. Henry's quipped: "Paris is worth a
paragraphs.zh: 亨利的卫兵立刻杀死了克莱门特， 但国王却受了致命伤。 亨利三世强撑了一天后，还是因伤 去世了。临终时，他立纳瓦尔·亨 利为继承人。 1589 年 1 月 8 日，纳瓦尔·亨利成 为法国国王，即亨利四世 战争又持续了 4 年，1593 年 7 月 25 日，亨利和他的军队来到了巴黎 城外。 亨利面临着一个选择，他可以选择 尝试苦战占领巴黎，或者是选择皈 依天主教，不战而胜，拿下这座城 市。 亨利说了一句俏皮话:“巴黎，值

paragraphs.en: mass", and converted to Catholicism on the spot. Overjoyed the city of Paris opened its gates and welcome Henry in. On July 25th, 1593, the French wars of Religion were finally over. The civil war had lasted 30 years and killed nearly 10% of France. But even though Henry had converted to Catholicism, he was still sympathetic to his Protestant friends, so inApril 1598 he passed the Edict of Nantes which gave Protestants freedom of worship everywhere in France except for Paris.
paragraphs.zh: 得一场弥撒。”当场就皈依了天主 教。 欣喜若狂的巴黎人民敞开大门欢 迎亨利的到来。1593 年 7 月 25 日， 法国的宗教战争终于结束了。 内战持续了 30 年，死了将近 10% 的法国人。 尽管亨利皈依了天主教，但他仍然 同情新教朋友，1598 年 4 月，他通 过了《南特敕令》，准许新教徒在 法国除巴黎外的其他各地都拥有 宗教自由。

titleZh: 第十讲：阿里乌派，哥特人和伊比利亚|信仰真主的西班牙

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on world history, today we will learn about the 30-years war. This lecture will focus on the years from 1578 until 1648 As we learned last time, the King of Portugal, Sebastian I, became involved in a war in Morocco in 1578. Unfortunately for Portugal, he was killed in battle at the Battle of the Three Kings. Sebastian died without an heir, so the next in line to the Portuguese throne was his uncle Philip. It took Philip about 2 years to
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。今天我们要讨论的是欧洲三十 年战争。 这一讲主要聚焦1578-1648年间的 历史。 上节课我们讲过，葡萄牙国王塞巴 斯蒂安一世于 1578 年在摩洛哥卷 入了战争。 不幸的是，他在三王之战中阵亡。 塞巴斯蒂安去世时没有继承人，所 以葡萄牙王位的下一个继承人是 他的叔叔腓力。 腓力花了 2 年时间才获得政权。

paragraphs.en: secure power, but in 1580 he unified Portugal and Spain. This new Kingdom was called "The Iberian Union." This was a big deal because in 1580, only Portugal and Spain had overseas empires. Here is a map of these empires in 1579. And here is a map of unified empires in 1581. And here is a map of Philip’s European territory in 1580. Basically Philip succeeded in gaining Portugual and losing the Netherlands. Because while all this was going on, the Dutch were fighting a war of liberation against Spain which
paragraphs.zh: 1580 年，他统一了葡萄牙和西班 牙。 这个新王国被称为“伊比利亚联 盟”。 这可是件大事，因为在 1580 年， 只有葡萄牙和西班牙拥有海外帝 国。 这是 1579 年这些帝国的地图。 这是 1581 年统一帝国的地图。 这是 1580 年腓力的欧洲领土。 腓力成功获取葡萄牙，同时也失去 了荷兰。 这是因为荷兰人此时正在和西班 牙打一场解放战争，这场战争持续 了 80 年。

paragraphs.en: would last for 80 years. Now before 1580 the Portuguese were the main providers of spices in Europe.And since the Dutch economy was based entirely on trade, the Dutch did a great deal of business with the Portuguese. But when Philip unified Spain and Portugal to create the Iberian Union, he made Portugal stop trading with the Dutch, as a way of punishing them for the rebellion. But instead of just accepting this trade embargo, the Dutch decided to go toAsia and get their own spices. Given the chaos of the war it took them some years to organize an expedition, but in 1595 the Dutch made their first expedition toAsia. Then in 1598 a better organized
paragraphs.zh: 1580 年前，葡萄牙人是欧洲香料的 主要供应者。由于荷兰的经济完全 建立在贸易的基础上，荷兰与葡萄 牙进行了大量的贸易往来。 但当腓力统一西班牙和葡萄牙、建 立伊比利亚联盟时，他要求葡萄牙 停止与荷兰的贸易，以此作为对荷 兰造反的惩罚。 但是荷兰人不仅接受了贸易禁运， 还决定自行去亚洲购买香料。 由于战乱，他们花了数年时间组织 这次航行。终于在 1595 年，荷兰 人第一次远征亚洲。 1598 年，一支更精心组织的探险队

paragraphs.en: expedition made a second voyage under the leadership of Jacob Van Neck. This second expedition turned a profit of 400%, and that got the interest of all the Dutch merchants! Around the year 1600, the Dutch made the decision to take Portugal's Asian trade for themselves. But this would mean an organized effort beyond the ability of any individual or ship captain. So to accomplish this, the Dutch organized a powerful company called the Dutch East Indian Company. Outside of Europe this company would have a monopoly on the Asian trade. It would have the power to build navies, construct
paragraphs.zh: 在雅各布·范·内克的领导下进行 了第二次航行。 第二次探险获取了 400%的利润，这 引起了所有荷兰商人的兴趣! 大约在 1600 年，荷兰人决定自己 接管葡萄牙在亚洲的贸易。 但这将意味超越任何个人或船长 能力的有组织的努力。为了实现这 个目标，荷兰人成立了一个强大的 公司：荷属东印度公司。 在欧洲以外，这家公司将垄断亚洲 的贸易。它将拥有建造海军、建造 堡垒和组建军队的能力。它可以和 亚洲的国王签订条约，也可以在亚

paragraphs.en: forts and raise armies. It could make treaties withAsian kings, and it could administer its own laws in Asia.And to pay for all this the Dutch raised 6.4 million gold coins called guilders. This made the Dutch East India company one of the richest companies in history, and a combination of a company and a state. To raise the money to pay for all this, the Dutch invented the world's first stock market, which met in the courtyards of the company's headquarters building. And so began a 60-year long war with Portugal for control of the Asian trade routes. The Dutch and Portuguese fought each other inAmerica, in Europe, in Africa and inAsia. In many ways
paragraphs.zh: 洲执行自己的法律。为此，荷兰筹 集了 640 万枚叫做荷兰盾的金币。 这使得荷兰东印度公司成为历史 上最富有的公司之一，是一个公司 和一个国家的结合体。 为了筹集资金，荷兰人在公司总部 大楼的院子里创建了世界上第一 个股票市场。 于是，葡萄牙开始了长达 60 年的 争夺亚洲贸易航线控制权的战争。 荷兰人和葡萄牙人在美洲、欧洲、 非洲和亚洲相互争斗。在许多方 面，这场冲突可以被视为第一次世

paragraphs.en: this conflict can be considered the first world war. The Portuguese held on to some of their territory, but the Dutch were able to establish colonies in north America, southAmerica,Africa and Asia as you can see here. And it was Asia which Dutch cared about the most. They were able to capture all the colonies that are orange-colored on this map. But the Portuguese were able to hold on to Goa in India and Macau in China. Once the Dutch established themselves inAsia they realized that the most profitable business was not to buy spices, but to become the middle-men for the entireAsian trade. The Japanese,
paragraphs.zh: 界大战。 葡萄牙人占领了他们的一些领土， 但荷兰人却在北美，南美，非洲和 亚洲建立了殖民地。 而荷兰人最在意的是亚洲。他们在 地图上橘色的区域都建立了殖民 地。 而葡萄牙人则紧紧控制了印度的 果阿邦和中国的澳门。 荷兰人在亚洲站稳脚跟后，他们意 识到最赚钱的生意不是买香料，而 是成为整个亚洲贸易的中间商。日 本、中国和印度的皇帝没有建造海 军，也没有投资造船。

paragraphs.en: Chinese and Indian Emperors did not build navies or invest in ship building. So when Japanese, Indians, and Chinese wanted to trade with each other, they hired Dutch ships to do the work. The Dutch acted as middlemen and made a profit on each voyage. And all of this wealth fromAsia flowed back to the Netherlands making it the richest country in Europe. Meanwhile, while all this was going on, trouble was brewing the Holy Roman Empire. By 1600 this is what the Holy Roman Empire looked like. It consisted of hundreds of little states all joined together by the Empire and governed by an emperor, but
paragraphs.zh: 因此，当日本人、印度人和中国人 想要彼此进行贸易时，他们雇佣荷 兰船只来做这项工作。荷兰人充当 中间商，每次航行都能够获利。 所有这些财富从亚洲流向荷兰，使 荷兰成为欧洲最富有的国家。 与此同时，神圣罗马帝国也日渐不 安。

paragraphs.en: having different local rulers. Germany was very divided at this time in history. And in all of these different states, the people worshiped at different churches. German was a confused mix of Catholic Churches, Zwinglian Reform Churches, Calvinist Reform Churches, Lutheran Churches, and Hussite Churches. This map shows you what that looked like. At the time, Germany was governed by the eastern branch of the Hapsburg family, theAustrian Hapsburgs. The Holy Roman Empire was not directly owned by the Hapsburgs,
paragraphs.zh: 德国在当时是非常分裂的。 在各个州，人们信奉不同的教会。 德国人的宗教信仰混合了天主教， 茨温良改革教会，加尔文改革教 会，路德教会和胡斯教会。 如地图所示。 当时，德国由哈布斯堡家族的东部 分支奥地利哈布斯堡王朝统治。 神圣罗马帝国并不直接归哈普斯 堡家族所有，但自 15 世纪以来，

paragraphs.en: but they were always elected to the position of Holy Roman Emperor ever since the 1400s. If you remember theAustrian Hapsburgs and Spanish Hapsburgs were cousins, both sides of the family descended from Philip the Handsome ofAustria and Joanna of Castile. At first their son Charles V had ruled the unified empire, but then it became divided between his son and brother after 1555. At this point one of the Great powers in Europe was the Protestant Swedish Empire led by a warrior-king named Gustavas Adolphus. GustavasAdolphus was one of the greatest generals in human history,
paragraphs.zh: 他们一直被选为神圣罗马帝国的 皇帝。 还记得吗？奥地利的哈布斯堡王 族和西班牙的哈布斯堡王族是堂 兄妹，这两个家族都是英俊的奥地 利人腓力和卡斯提尔的乔安娜的 后裔。 起初，他们的儿子查理五世统治着 统一的帝国，但公元 1555 年以后， 其国土分由弟弟斐迪南与儿子腓 力继承。 此时，欧洲强国之一：信奉新教的 瑞典帝国正由骁勇无比的国王古 斯塔夫斯·阿道尔弗斯领导。 阿道尔弗斯是人类历史上最伟大 的将军之一，他的军事创新使一些

paragraphs.en: and his military innovation have led some to call him the "Father of Modern Warfare." So Louis made a secret deal with Gustavas and offered to send him a large amount of money if he would attack the Holy Roman Empire. Gustavus agreed. On July 4th, 1640, Gutavas' army landed in northern Germany near a region called Pomerania. This picture shows you their landing in German and the beginning of the 3rd phase of the war: The Swedish Phase. The Hapsburgs had won every major battle up to this point, so they were feeling pretty confident went they attacked the Swedes on September 16th, 1631.
paragraphs.zh: 人把他称为“现代战争之父”。 于是路易斯和古斯塔夫斯做了一 笔秘密交易，他提出如果古斯塔夫 斯攻打神圣罗马帝国，就给他一大 笔钱。 古斯塔夫斯同意了。 1640 年 7 月 4 日，古特瓦斯的军队 在德国北部的波美拉尼亚登陆。 这张照片展示了他们登陆德国的 情景以及战争第三阶段的开始:瑞 典阶段。 到目前为止，哈布斯堡王朝赢得了 每一场战役，所以他们很有信心在 1631 年 9 月 16 日进攻瑞典。

paragraphs.en: But GustavasAdolphus showed his military genius in this battle: The Battle of Breitenfeld. The Hapsburg army was completely destroyed, and the Protestants won their first major victory in the war. By November of 1632, most of northern Germany was controlled by Sweden, and these victories had persuaded many German princes to switch sides. On the 16th of November, 1632, there was a minor battle near the town of Lutzen. It would have been an unimportant skirmish, except that Gustavas Adolphus was killed by a stray bullet while leading his troops. The Swedes were devastated by his
paragraphs.zh: 但古斯塔夫斯·阿道尔弗斯在布雷 滕菲尔德之战中展示了他的军事 天赋。 哈布斯堡军队被彻底摧毁，新教徒 在战争中赢得了第一次重大胜利。 1632 年 11 月，德国北部大部分地 区被瑞典控制，这些胜利说服了许 多德国王公改变立场。 1632 年 11 月 16 日，洛肯镇附近发 生了一场小规模的战斗。 如果不是古斯塔夫斯·阿道尔弗斯 在率领他的部队时被流弹击中身 亡，这本来是一场无关紧要的小冲 突。 瑞典人对他的死感到非常悲痛，他

paragraphs.en: loss, and his body was returned to Sweden for burial. Without their brilliant King to lead them, the Swedish army was unable to beat the Hapsburgs and they were driven north from 1632 to 1635 and the Imperial armies slowly recaptured their territory. By 1635, the Swedish position had become so weak that Louis XIII decided that France needed to fight in the war directly. And so France went to war against both theAustrian and Spanish Hapsburgs in 1635. The next 13 years of fighting were the longest and bloodiest phase of the war: the French phase. What had begun as a local religious rebellion was now a European-wide
paragraphs.zh: 的遗体被送回瑞典埋葬。 没有了英明国王的领导，瑞典军队 无法打败哈布斯堡王朝，1632 年到 1635 年，瑞典军队被迫北上，帝国 军队慢慢夺回了他们的领土。 到 1635 年，瑞典的地位变得非常 不稳固，路易十三决定法国需要直 接参战。 1635 年，法国同时与奥地利和西班 牙的哈布斯堡王朝开战。接下来的 13 年是战争中最漫长、最血腥的阶 段:法国阶段。 这场混战一开始只是小范围的宗 教叛乱，现在变成了欧洲大陆上两

paragraphs.en: war between the two greatest Catholic powers on the continent. The French won a huge victory against the Spanish at the Battle of Rocroi on May 19th, 1643, which surprised many people. Indeed, the 30-years war is the moment in history where the power of France surpassed the power of Spain. Never again would Spain be the most powerful country in Europe. Eventually the 30-years war came to an end on October 24th, 1648 when the different sides all signed the Treaty of Wesphalia. And this is an important moment in European history, because the 30-years war was the last religious war in European history.After 1648
paragraphs.zh: 个天主教大国之间的、范围涉及全 欧洲大混战。 1643 年 5 月 19 日，法国人在罗克 洛伊战役中战胜了西班牙人，取得 了巨大的胜利，这让很多人大吃一 惊。 事实上，30 年战争是法国超越西班 牙的历史时刻。 西班牙再也不是欧洲最强大的国 家了。 30 年战争终于在 1648 年 10 月 24 日结束了，当时各方都签署了《威 斯特伐利亚条约》。 这是欧洲历史上的一个重要时刻， 因为 30 年战争是欧洲历史上最后 一场宗教战争。1648 年后，所有的 战争都是因为民族主义，而不是宗

paragraphs.en: all future wars would be motivated by nationalism instead of religion. For this reason, many historians choose the year1648 to mark the end of the Protestant Reformation There were many effect of the 30-years war, many of these were changes in power as some countries became stronger and other became weaker. But perhaps the biggest change was the realization that Europe needed to accept religious toleration as an idea, otherwise it would be torn apart with war. Catholic-Protestant tension would continue as a major factor in western history for another 300 years, but this was the last time it explicitly caused a war.
paragraphs.zh: 教。 因此，许多历史学家选择将 1648 年作为新教改革的结束年。 30 年战争产生了很多影响，其中有 很多是权力的变化：一些国家变得 强大，另一些变得弱小。 但或许最大的变化是欧洲意识到： 如果再不接受宗教信仰自由的理 念，整个欧洲都会因为战争而四分 五裂。 天主教和新教之间的紧张关系将 继续作为西方历史上的一个主要 因素持续 300 年，但这是它最后一 次明确引发战争。

paragraphs.en: Now, before we end, I want to take some time to look at the human cost of the 30 years war. In the first place tens of thousands of people died directly in the many battles of this war. And indeed, archaeologists have found mass graves near some of the battlefields. But it wasn't just soldiers, civilians suffered severely from the wars. Over the span of a generation hundreds of thousands were probably killed, robbed or raped by rampaging soldiers. All the armies supported their men by pillaging enemy civilian. All the armies supported their men by pillaging enemy civilian.
paragraphs.zh: 现在，在我们结束之前，我想花点 时间来看看 30 年战争导致的人口 损失。 首先，数以万计的人直接死于战 争。 事实上，考古学家在一些战场附近 发现了万人坑。 但除士兵外，平民也在战争中遭受 了严重的损失。在一代人的时间 里，可能有数十万人被狂暴的士兵 杀害、抢劫或强奸。 所有的军队都通过掠夺敌方平民 来供给士兵。 士兵们经常袭击手无寸铁的平民， 并对他们施加暴行。

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we will be discussing the scientific revolution. This will focus on the years between 1543 and 1727. So I'd like to begin by telling you a story from when I was 4 years old, back in 1989. I remember that I was just beginning to understand the world, and I noticed how the Sun moved through the sky each day. I still remember how I confidently told my mother: Mom! The Sun goes around the Earth! And she replied: "No sweetie, the Earth goes around the Sun!"
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。今天我们将讨论的是科学革 命。 这节课我们将聚焦 1543-1727 年 间。 首先，我想给大家讲一个我 4 岁时 的故事，那是 1989 年。 我记得我刚刚开始了解这个世界， 我注意到太阳每天是如何在天空 中运动的。 我仍然记得我是如何自信地告诉 妈妈：妈妈!太阳绕着地球转! 她回答说:“不，亲爱的，地球绕 着太阳转!”

paragraphs.en: I still remember how astonished this piece of information made me feel. Because of course, to us on Earth, it seems like the Sun is moving and we are standing still. So the goal of today's lesson is to understand how humans discovered the truth and in so doing, created Science. To my 4-year old self, and to stone-age people, two things seemed obvious: 1. The Earth is flat. 2. The Sun goes around the Earth. And indeed this seems to be what ancient people believed.Around 3000 BC theAncient Egyptians believed that the night sky was
paragraphs.zh: 我至今仍然记得这句话让我多么 惊讶。 因为，对我们地球上的人来说，似 乎太阳总在运动，而我们却站着不 动。 所以今天这节课的目的是理解人 类是如何发现真理的，以及我们如 何在此过程中创造了科学。 对 4 岁的我和石器时代的人来说， 有两件事是显而易见的: 1. 地球是平的。 2. 太阳绕着地球转。 古人似乎就是这么认为的。公元前 3000 年左右，古埃及人认为夜空实 际上是一位名叫纳特的巨大女神 的身体。

paragraphs.en: really the body of a giant goddess named Nut. And 2200 years later, theAncient Greeks still believed something similar. The works of Homer and Hesiod portray the Earth as a flat place covered with the sky, which the God of the Sun travelled across each day. But 300 years later, the Greeks were beginning to develop a more sophisticated understanding of the world. Around 500 BC, the Greek scholars Parmenides and Pythagoras both taught that the Earth was round. 150 years later,Aristotle believed the same things, and he defended this belief with 3 arguments in his book On the Heavens.
paragraphs.zh: 2200 年后，古希腊人仍然相信相似 的理论。荷马和赫西奥德的作品把 地球描绘成一个被天空覆盖的平 坦的地方，太阳神每天都要经过天 空。 但 300 年后，希腊人开始对世界有 了更深刻的理解。 公元前 500 年左右，希腊学者巴门 尼德斯和毕达哥拉斯都认为地球 是圆的。 150 年后，亚里士多德也持同样的 观点，他在《论天堂》一书中提出 了三个论点来捍卫这一观点。

paragraphs.en: First he pointed out that tall ships disappear from the bottom-up as they sail towards the horizon. Here is a modern photograph of this phenomenon.As you can see, the bottom of the tanker Eagle Kinabulu is obscured by the curvature of the Earth, because it is 19 kilometers away. Second,Aristotle pointed out that shadow of a lunar eclipse is curved. If a lunar eclipse is caused by the Earth's shadow, this shows the curvature of the Earth. And third, as you move north and south, the stars change their position. This makes sense on a curved Earth, but makes no sense on a flat Earth.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，他指出，高大的船只在驶向 地平线时，会从自下而上消失。 这是这一现象的现代照片。正如你 所看到的，鹰基纳布鲁号油轮的底 部被地球的曲率遮住了，因为它远 在 19 公里之外。 其次，亚里士多德指出月食的阴影 是弯曲的。 如果月食是由地球的阴影造成的， 这显示了地球的曲率。 第三，当你向北和向南移动时，星 星的位置也会随之改变。 这在一个弯曲的地球上能说得通， 但如果地球是平的，这一切就都说 不通了。

paragraphs.en: Aristotle if you remember was Alexander the Great's tutor. AndAlexander conquered a great Empire. But after he died, his generals divided it up into the 4 Hellenistic Kingdoms. This began the Hellenistic age, which lasted for 3 centuries. And the greatest city of that Era was Alexandria, the great port-city of Egypt.. And as we've learnedAlexandria developed the world's greatest library at that time. And many scholars came to study at Alexandria, including Eratosthenes who famously proved not just that the Earth was curved,
paragraphs.zh: 还记得吗？亚里士多德是亚历山 大大帝的导师。 亚历山大征服了一个伟大的帝国。 但在他死后，他的将军们将其划分 为四个希腊王国。 希腊化时代开始了，一直持续了 3 个世纪。 那个时代最伟大的城市是亚历山 大港——埃及最大的港口城市。 我们还学到过：亚历山大建立了当 时世界上最大的图书馆。 许多学者来到亚历山大港学习研 究，包括著名的埃拉托色尼，他不 仅证明了地球是弯曲的，而且还通 过比较夏至时赛尼城和亚历山大

paragraphs.en: but also figured out its true size by comparing the angle of shadows in Syene andAlexandria during the summer solstice. Eratosthenes estimate that the earth had a circumference of 40,000 kilometers. Which is only 75 kilometers off of the true size. However, at this point it was still just a theory. Copernicus lacked strong evidence. This evidence would be provided by the two men from the next generation: Galileo Galilei and Johannes Kepler. We will look first at Galileo. Galileo was born 31 years after Copernicus died, in the city of Pisa in Italy.
paragraphs.zh: 港阴影的角度，计算出了地球的真 实大小。 埃拉托色尼估算出地球的周长为 4 万公里。距离实际大小只有 75 公 里。 然而，在这一点上，它仍然只是一 个理论。哥白尼缺乏有力的证据。 这一证据将由来自下一代的两个 人提供:伽利略和约翰内斯·开普 勒。 我们先来看伽利略。 哥白尼去世 31 年后，伽利略出生 在意大利比萨城。

paragraphs.en: Pisa is famous for its great Cathedral and leaning tower, which you can see in this photograph. When he was 16 years old, the young Galielo attended a lecture by the mathematician Ostillo Ricci and developed a life-long love of Math. Galileo began his scientific career by studying the motion of falling objects, which he measured by rolling balls down ramps. Here is an example of the kind of ramp that Galileo built. And he placed bells along the ramp at equal intervals so that he could better time the speed of rolling balls. Aristotle had taught that heavier objects fall faster. But Galileo proved him wrong. He argued that
paragraphs.zh: 比萨因其大教堂和斜塔而闻名，你 可以从这张照片中看到。 16 岁时，年轻的加利洛参加了数学 家奥斯蒂略·里奇的讲座，并对数 学产生了终生的热爱。 伽利略的科学生涯是从研究物体 下落的运动开始的。 这是伽利略建造的斜坡。 他在坡道上每隔一段距离就放一 个铃铛，这样他就能更好地掌握滚 球的速度。 亚里士多德曾教导说，较重的物体 下落得更快。但是伽利略证明他错 了。他认为重物和轻物以同样的速

paragraphs.en: heavy object and light objects fall at the same speed. He explained this discovery with a thought-experiment which involved dropping two balls of different weight off the tower of Pisa. But this was just a description. He never actually did the experiment because it would have been dangerous. 20 years later, a Dutch man named Hand Lippershy realized that two lenses, held the right distance apart greatly magnified the apparent size of distant objects. He had invented the telescope. When Galileo learned about this invention, he decided that he had to build one for himself.
paragraphs.zh: 度下落。 他用一个想像实验来解释这个发 现，这个实验包括从比萨斜塔上扔 下两个不同重量的球。 但这只是解释。他从未真正做过这 个实验，因为那样做会很危险。 20 年后，一个名叫汉德·利伯希的 荷兰人意识到，只要两个透镜相距 合适，就能极大地放大远处物体的 表面尺寸。 他发明了望远镜。 当伽利略得知这项发明时，他决定 自己动手做一个。

paragraphs.en: So in 1609 Galileo created his own telescope. Here it is. Galileo's telescope was not super powerful. It was about as good as a pair of modern binoculars. But it was good enough to show Galileo things that had never been seen before by any human. His first discovery came we he looked at the planet Jupiter. You can see Jupiter with you own eyes without a telescope. It looks like a bright star. Here it is compared to the Moon. Here is what Galileo saw. Four bright dots around Jupiter that moved from night to night. These dots are invisible with the naked eye, but can be seen with a telescope.
paragraphs.zh: 1609 年伽利略发明了自己的望远 镜。如图所示。 伽利略的望远镜并不十分强大。其 性能约等于现代的双筒望远镜。 但是这座望远镜向伽利略展示了 人类从未见过的景致。 他的第一个发现是观察了木星。你 不用望远镜就能亲眼看见木星。它 看起来像一颗明亮的星星。这里是 它和月亮的对比图。 这是伽利略所看到的。木星周围有 四个明亮的点，每晚都移动。 这些圆点是肉眼看不见的,但可以 用望远镜看到。

paragraphs.en: Galileo realized that these were moons! Not everything in the universe orbits the Earth. He named these moons: Io, Europa, Calisto, and Ganymede. Today we call them the Galilean moons. They are each named after a mythological figure who has a close relationship to the God Jupiter. The next discovery happened in 1610 when Galileo spent several weeks observing the planet Venus. And Galileo realized that Venus has phases just like the moon! This was a key piece of evidence! If Venus orbits the Sun, its phases make perfect sense. But they make no sense if Venus orbits the Earth. The moons of Jupiter and the
paragraphs.zh: 伽利略意识到这些是卫星!宇宙中 并非所有的东西都绕着地球转。 他把这些卫星命名为:木卫一、木 卫二、卡利斯托和木卫三。今天我 们称它们为伽利略卫星。 它们都是以神话人物的名字命名 的，这些神话人物都与朱庇特关系 密切。 下一个发现发生在 1610 年，当时 伽利略花了几周时间观察金星。 伽利略意识到金星和月球一样也 有相位! 这是一个关键的证据! 如果金星围绕太阳公转，那么它的 相位是完全合理的。但如果金星绕 地球运行，那就说不通了。 木星的卫星和金星的相位是哥白

paragraphs.en: phases of Venus were strong evidence in support of Copernicus' theory. The next piece of evidence would come from the German scientist Johannes Kepler. Kepler was another skilled mathematician, but he was poor. So in 1600 he accepted a job to work for Tycho Brahe a Dutch nobleman. In 1576 Tycho had set up an observatory on the Island of Hven in Denmark. This is what Hven looks like today. The observatory was called Uraniborg. Brahe spent the next 24 years making careful observations night after night. By 1600 he had
paragraphs.zh: 尼理论的有力证据。 下一个证据将来自德国科学家约 翰内斯·开普勒。 开普勒也是一位有经验的数学家， 但他很穷。1600 年，他开始为荷兰 贵族第谷·布拉赫工作。 1576 年，第谷在丹麦的赫文岛上建 立了一座天文台。 这就是赫文今天的样子。 这座天文台被称为乌拉尼堡。 在接下来的 24 年里，布拉赫夜以 继日地仔细观察。到 1600 年，他 收集了地球上最好的夜空数据。

paragraphs.en: compiled the best data on Earth about the night sky. But Brahe lacked the mathematical ability to make sense of all his data, so he asked Kepler to work for him.
paragraphs.zh: 但是布拉赫缺乏相应的数学能力 来分析他所有的数据，所以他请开 普勒为他工作。

titleZh: 第十二讲：诺斯人|你知道 Sunday 是一个舶来词吗？

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on World History, today we will learn about the early American colonies. This lesson will focus on the years between 1563 and 1642, here is what that looks like on our timeline. In the 8th, 9th and 10th lessons we learned about the Dutch Revolt, the French Wars of Religion, and the Anglo Spanish Wars. Really, these 3 wars are best understood as parts of one large war which connected Spain, France, The Netherlands and England) In a previous lesson we saw how Henry of Navarre converted to
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。今天我们将学习早期的美洲殖 民地。 这节课的重点是 1563-1642 年，这 是我们的时间轴。 在第 8、9 和 10 讲中，我们学习了 荷兰起义、法国宗教战争和英西战 争。 确实，这三场战争可以被理解为西 班牙、法国、荷兰和英国之间大规 模战争的一部分。 在前几节课中，我们了解了纳瓦拉 的亨利如何在巴黎城外皈依了天

paragraphs.en: Catholicism outside of Paris with the famous words: "Paris is worth a mass". And then the city welcomed him inside on July 25th, 1593. But Henry remembered his old Protestant friends and passed the Edict of Nantes which gave the Huguenots religious toleration. Henry's first marriage was to Margaret ofAnjou. But this was followed by the murder of many of his friends on St.Bartholomew's Day. We learned about this in the lesson on the Wars of Religion. so of course this did not lead to a happy marriage. In 1599 Henry and
paragraphs.zh: 主教，他说了一句著名的话: “巴 黎，值得一场弥撒”。 随后，在 1593 年 7 月 25 日，巴黎 向他敞开了大门。 但是亨利想起了他新教的老朋友， 并通过了南特敕令，给予胡格诺派 教徒宗教上的宽容。 亨利的第一次婚姻是与安茹的玛 格丽特。 但紧随其后，他的许多朋友在圣巴 多罗买节被杀。 我们在宗教战争一节中学到过这 些。 当然，这场婚姻并能走向幸福的结 局。1599 年，亨利和玛格丽特宣布

paragraphs.en: Margaret had their marriage annulled. And in 1600 Henry married a new Queen: Marie de Medici. Marie gave Henry 5 children who lived into adulthood: Louis XIII, Elisabeth, Christine, Gaston and Henrietta Maria. Unfortunately, Henry IV was assassinated while riding his carriage through Paris on May 14th, 1610. The crown then passed to his son Louis XIII. During this same time period, Phillip II died n 1598. And he was succeeded by his son Phillip III. And Queen Elizabeth of England
paragraphs.zh: 婚姻无效。 1600 年，亨利娶了一位新王后:玛 丽·德·美第奇。 玛丽给亨利生了 5 个孩子，他们都 活到了成年:路易十三、伊丽莎白、 克里斯汀、加斯顿和亨丽埃塔·玛 丽亚。 不幸的是，亨利四世于 1610 年 5 月 14 日在巴黎乘坐马车时被暗杀。 王位传给了他的儿子路易十三。 与此同时，腓力二世于 1598 年去 世。 王位传给了他的儿子腓力三世。 五年后，英国女王伊丽莎白二世于

paragraphs.en: died 5 years later in 1603. And she was succeeded by her cousin James I. Now it was at this point, in the first decade of the 1600s that France, England and the Netherlands began to pay serious attention toAmerica. Although England and France had made earlier efforts in the 1500s... From 1415 to 1580, world exploration and colonization had been dominated by the Spanish and Portuguese. And these two empires became one empire when Philip unified the two kingdoms in 1581. In the 1500s the French had made one serious effort at Colonization in 1563. Gaspard de Coligny had decided that French Huguenots
paragraphs.zh: 1603 年去世。 她的堂兄詹姆斯一世接替了她。 此时，也就是 17 世纪头十年，法 国，英国和荷兰开始认真关注美 洲。 尽管英国和法国早在 16 世纪就做 出了努力…… 从 1415 年到 1580 年，世界探险和 殖民一直由西班牙人和葡萄牙人 主导。 1581 年，腓力统一了两国，两大帝 国合并为一个帝国。 16 世纪，法国人在 1563 年进行了 一次的殖民活动。加斯帕德·德·科 利尼决定让法国胡格诺派教徒在 美洲建立自己的殖民地。

paragraphs.en: should set up their own colony in America. So in 1564 they established Fort Caroline in what is now Florida. But this angered Philip II who viewed all of theAmericas as belonging to him. So in 1565 a Spanish force attacked Fort Caroline and massacred the Huguenot settlers. And then they founded the town of St.Augustine a short distance away. St.Augustine still exists and it is now the oldest town in the United States. And here in yellow are all the gentlemen. These were men who had never worked in their life, and
paragraphs.zh: 1564 年，他们在现在的佛罗里达建 立了卡洛琳堡。 但这激怒了腓力二世，他认为整个 美洲都应该属于他。 1565 年，西班牙军队袭击了卡洛琳 堡，屠杀了胡格诺派殖民者。 然后他们在不远的地方建立了圣 奥古斯丁镇。 圣奥古斯丁仍然存在，现在它是美 国最古老的城镇。 标黄的这些都是绅士。这些人从来 没有工作过，他们习惯于发号施令 而不接受命令。

paragraphs.en: were used to giving orders and not taking them. And so the leadership immediately began arguing among themselves. And they had built Jamestown on a swampy island, which was good for defense, but didn't have good water or good soil for growing food. This is what the area looks like today. And of course Jamestown was located in land that was already occupied by a group of Native Americans called the Powhatan tribe. The Powhattan were lead by a chief called Wahunsunacawh. The English treated the Powhatan quite badly, and by 1609 they had started a war.
paragraphs.zh: 于是领导层内部立刻争论不休。 并且，他们在一个潮湿的岛上建造 了詹姆斯敦，虽有利于防御，但没 有良好的水和土壤来种植食物。 这是这片土地今天的样子。 詹姆斯敦所在的位置已经被波瓦 坦部落的印第安人占领了。这些人 由一个叫瓦胡苏纳卡瓦的酋长率 领。 英国人对待波瓦坦人非常恶劣，到 1609 年，他们开始了一场战争。

paragraphs.en: This led to Indian attacks and made it impossible to grow or trade for food. FromApril 1607 until June 1610, three expeditions had carried around 800 settlers to Jamestown, and during that time over 700 of them died from Indian attacks, starvation or disease. This was a mortality rate of at least 85%. But in June of 1610, Thomas West, the Baron De La Warr arrived with 3 big ships, 150 more settlers, and a large supply of food. Under his leadership the colony avoided destruction. Then in 1614 the English were able to capture Pocohantas the daughter of Wahunsunacawh, the chief of the
paragraphs.zh: 这引发了印第安人对英国人的攻 击，导致粮食无法种植或交易。 从 1607 年 4 月到 1610 年 6 月，三 次探险将 800 名左右的移民带到詹 姆斯敦，在这段时间里，其中 700 多人死于印第安人的袭击、饥饿或 疾病。 死亡率至少为 85% 但是在 1610 年 6 月，托马斯·韦 斯特，德拉瓦尔男爵带着 3 艘大船、 150 多名移民和大量的食物来到这 里。 在他的领导下，殖民地幸存了下 来。 1614 年，英国人俘虏了当地波瓦坦 印第安部落首领瓦亨苏纳卡瓦的 女儿波科汉塔斯。

paragraphs.en: local Powhatan Indian tribe. They took her as a captive to Jamestown. There an English colonist named John Rolfe fell in love with Pocohontas. And the two of them were married onApril 5th, 1614. This marriage allowed the English and Powhatan to make a peace treaty called "The Peace of Pocahontas" And John Rolfe did something else that was very important. He tried growing tobacco. Tobacco grew quite well in Virginia, and so John Rolfe discovered a way for the colony to make money.
paragraphs.zh: 他们把她俘虏到詹姆斯敦。 在那里，一位名叫约翰·罗尔夫的 英国殖民者爱上了波科洪塔斯。 两人于 1614 年 4 月 5 日结婚。 这段婚姻使英国人和波瓦坦人达 成了一项和平条约，名为“波科洪 塔斯和平条约”。 约翰·罗尔夫做了一件非常重要的 事。他尝试种植烟草。 烟草在弗吉尼亚长得很好，所以约 翰·罗尔夫发现了一个让殖民地赚 钱的方法。

paragraphs.en: The peace treaty and Tobacco crop ensured the survival of Virginia. Meanwhile the French were also setting up NorthAmerican colonies. They had learned from their earlier mistake and established their settlement far away from the Spanish, along the St. Lawrence river in what is now Canada. In 1610 a French expedition established a colony here. This expedition was led by the explorer Samuel de Champlain.And he founded a small trading post which he named Quebec. Here is an artist's depiction of what Quebec might have looked like. The French were interested mainly in animal furs, and the most desired was the NorthAmerican Beaver.
paragraphs.zh: 和平条约和烟草作物让弗吉尼亚 生存了下来。 与此同时，法国人也在北美建立殖 民地。他们从早期的错误中吸取了 教训，沿着圣劳伦斯河，也就是现 在的加拿大，在远离西班牙人的地 方定居下来。 1610 年，一支法国探险队在这里建 立了殖民地。 探险队由探险家塞缪尔·德·尚普 兰率领。他建立了一个小贸易站， 取名魁北克。 这是一位艺术家对当时魁北克的 描绘。 法国人主要对动物皮毛感兴趣，最 受欢迎的是北美海狸。

paragraphs.en: So French explorers travelled far inland. And they developed trade relationships with local Native American tribes. The NativeAmericans wanted French manufactured goods, and the French wanted furs. So the two sides traded. In this way European trade goods moved deep into the interior of America, meaning many Native Americans started using European goods decades before they ever saw a white man. AndAmerican furs flowed back the other way. In the meantime, the Dutch were pushing intoAsia. They made their
paragraphs.zh: 为此，法国探险家深入内陆。 他们还与当地的印第安部落建立 了贸易关系。 美洲原住民想要法国制造的产品， 而法国人想要皮草。于是双方进行 了交易。 欧洲的贸易商品就这样深入到美 洲内部。这意味着许多印第安人在 见到白人之前的几十年就开始使 用欧洲的商品。 而美洲皮草则以另一种方式回流。 与此同时，荷兰人正在进军亚洲。 他们两次到达香料群岛，进行了贸

paragraphs.en: first two trading expeditions to the Spice Islands. These expeditions brought back enormous profits. And the Dutch began to fight a 60-year long naval war with the Portuguese for control of theAsian trade routes. Because of this help, Plymouth grew enough food to survive the winter of 1621. So they held a Thanksgiving feast thatAutumn. Which is the origin of theAmerican holiday of Thanksgiving. Plymouth survived as a rugged little town for the next 10 years. It probably looked something like this. During this time, James I died in
paragraphs.zh: 易探险。 这些探险活动带来了巨大的利润。 荷兰开始与葡萄牙进行长达 60 年 的海战，争夺对亚洲贸易航线的控 制权。 由于这种帮助，普利茅斯有了足够 的粮食来度过 1621 年的冬天。于 是他们在那年秋天举行了感恩节 宴会。 这就是美国感恩节的起源。 普利茅斯在接下来的十年里发展 成了一个小镇。大概是这样的。 在此期间，詹姆斯一世于 1625 年

paragraphs.en: 1625, and his son Charles I took the throne. Charles I decided to marry the youngest daughter of Henry IV of France, Henrietta Maria, who was a Catholic. This of course upset the Puritans in England. But even worse, Charles I appointed a priest named William Laud to be the Bishop of London in 1630. This was a clear sign that Charles eventually wanted to put William Laud in charge of the whole church of England. And William Laud hated Puritans. And that same year, Charles declared that he would rule without Parliament. So from 1630 to 1642, around
paragraphs.zh: 去世，他的儿子查理一世继位。 查理一世决定娶亨利四世最小的 女儿亨利埃塔玛丽亚为妻，她是一 位天主教徒。这当然惹恼了英国的 清教徒。 更糟糕的是，1630 年查理一世任命 一位名叫威廉劳德的牧师为伦敦 主教。 很明显，查理最终想让威廉劳德掌 管整个英格兰教会。 而威廉·劳德憎恨清教徒。 同年，查理宣布他将在没有国会的 情况下执政。 从 1630 年到 1642 年，大约 45000

paragraphs.en: 45,000 Englishmen moved to the Americas. The majority of them were Puritans.And 20,000 of them moved to the area around Plymouth which they called "New England". The largest of these expeditions arrived in 1630 led by a man named John Winthrop. They founded Boston in that year. Boston grew and grew, but disagreements over religion also developed among the Puritans. One group decided to go off and found their own colony which they called Connecticut. They took all their people and cattle and marched across New England for two weeks. Another dissident named Roger
paragraphs.zh: 名英国人移居美洲。他们大多数是 清教徒。其中 2 万人搬到了普利茅 斯周围——他们称之为"新英格兰 "的地区。 1630 年，大部分人都是跟随约翰·温 斯洛普率领的探险队到达了这里。 同年，他们创立了波士顿。 波士顿不断发展壮大，清教徒之间 也出现了宗教分歧。 一群人决定离开这里，建立他们自 己的殖民地，他们称之为康涅狄 格。 他们带着所有的人和牛在新英格 兰游行了两个星期。 另一位持不同政见者罗杰·威廉姆

paragraphs.en: Williams was driven out of Boston in the autumn of 1635. In the Spring of 1636, he founded Rhode Island which would be a colony dedicated to the idea of religious freedom. Roger Williams would have died that winter, but he was saved by his NativeAmerican friends. The next year he welcomed another dissident named Anne Hutchinson and together they built up the colony. Meanwhile, the British were expanding their colonies elsewhere. Aside from Jamestown their earliest colonies were in Nova Scotia, Bermuda and Barbados. Then in 1632, a Catholic lord named George Calvert got
paragraphs.zh: 斯于 1635 年秋被赶出波士顿。 636 年春天，他建立了罗德岛州， 这是一个致力于宗教自由理念的 殖民地。 罗杰·威廉姆斯本可能在那年冬天 去世，但他被他的美洲土著朋友救 了。 第二年, 他迎来了另一位持不同 政见者安妮·哈钦森，他们一起建 立了殖民地。 与此同时，英国人正在其他地方扩 张他们的殖民地。除了詹姆斯敦， 他们最早的殖民地位于新斯科舍 省、百慕大和巴巴多斯。 1632 年，一位名叫乔治·卡尔弗特 的天主教徒获准在弗吉尼亚北部

paragraphs.en: permission to set up a colony for Catholics just north of Virginia. He called it "Maryland" in honor of the King's wife, Henrietta Marie.
paragraphs.zh: 为天主教徒建立殖民地。 为了纪念国王的妻子亨利埃塔·玛 丽，他将其命名为“马里兰”。

paragraphs.en: Today we are going to learn about the power of European kings in the 1600s with a focus on the Kings of England and the Kings of France. This lesson will focus on the years from 1625 until 1689. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. In 1625 James I of England died and was succeeded by his son Charles I. But the King of England did not have as much power as Kings in other countries, because in England the King's powers were limited by Parliament. Parliament had begun in the 1200 when the nobility of England refused to give King John I any
paragraphs.zh: 今天我们将学习的是 17 世纪的欧 洲王权，重点是英格兰国王和法国 国王。 这节课主要讲的是 1625—1689 年 的故事。这是时间轴。 1625 年，英格兰国王詹姆斯一世去 世，其子查尔斯一世继位。 但是由于受议会限制，英格兰国王 的权力没有其他国家的国王大。 议会起源于 1200 年，当时英格兰贵 族拒绝为国王约翰一世缴纳更多的 税，用于支付与法国的战争。

paragraphs.en: more tax money to pay for his wars in France. On June 19th, 1215 they came to a compromise agreement known as the Great Charter or "Magna Carta". In this agreement the King agreed to limitations on his power in exchange for the end of the rebellion. But neither side kept up their side of the deal and the First Baron's War began. John I died the next year on October 19th, 1216 and was succeeded by his son Henry III. Henry III won the Baron's war, but he reissued Magna Carta in 1225 in exchange for tax money.And from that point on Magna Carta became part of English law.
paragraphs.zh: 1215 年 6 月 19 日，他们互相妥协， 签订了《大宪章》协议。 在这份协议中，国王同意以限制王 权来换取结束叛乱。 但双方都没有遵守协议，于是，第 一次男爵之战开始了。  第二年的 10 月 19 日，约翰 一世去世，他的儿子亨利三世继承 了王位。 亨利三世最终赢得了男爵之战，但 他在 1225 年又重新颁布了《大宪 章》，目的是以此换取税收。从那 时起，《大宪章》就成为了英国法 律的一部分。
